Peter is particularly driven by challenging implementations—where complexity, scale, and real-world constraints demand clear ownership, strong governance, and tight alignment between business, users, and engineering.

------------

Mateusz Gren is Head of Software Development at Atos in Austria and a driving force behind large-scale digital transformation in enterprise environments. Starting his career as a software developer, he brings a hands-on perspective to leading complex programs that combine modern software engineering, DevOps, and AI.

In recent years, Mateusz has been at the forefront of integrating generative AI into software development - from code generation and automated testing to new collaboration models between engineers and AI systems. His focus is not on hype, but on making AI work in real-world enterprise projects with strict requirements for governance, quality, and scalability.

As a speaker, he shares practical insights on how organizations can adopt AI in software engineering without losing control. His perspective bridges technology, delivery, and leadership - with one goal: building better software, faster.

sessions.title: Native Speed, Java Comfort: Calling Rust from the JVM with Project Panama
sessions.description:
The JVM is one of the most impressive pieces of software engineering, making Java incredibly fast for most workloads. But sometimes, "fast" isn't enough. For domains like scientific computing, AI, or processing massive volumes of text, we need to call highly-optimized native libraries written in C++ or Rust to gain a critical performance edge or access functionality not available on the JVM. For decades, this meant using the Java Native Interface (JNI)—a powerful but notoriously complex and unsafe bridge to the native world.

Enter Project Panama. With the Foreign Function & Memory (FFM) API, Java finally has a safe, supported, and elegant way to call native code, eliminating the need for brittle glue code and manual memory management. This talk puts it to the test with a classic Java challenge: the regular expression engine.

Join me for a practical, hands-on session where we will replace Java's capable but often-outperformed regex engine with Rust's highly optimized regex crate. We will walk through two implementations side-by-side: the "old way" with JNI and the "new way" with Project Panama. You will see firsthand how Panama simplifies interfacing with native code and improves safety. We'll cap it off with live benchmarks to compare the performance of both approaches against standard Java regex, helping you understand not just how to call native code, but also when it's truly worth the effort.

speakers.bio: I am a software engineer specialized in developing databases in Java. I love understanding how libraries and frameworks work under the hood and to design and implement high-performance systems. I have worked on prototypes such as ToroDB, the first Spanish database unicorn Devo and since 2022 I'm working at StarTree as an Apache Pinot contributor.

sessions.title: AI Driven Development
sessions.description: In software development, AI has evolved from a better stack overflow to a powerful co-pilot that is already making things possible today that we could only dream of five years ago. After more than 50 customer projects involving AI, David Tielke will show in this session which tools really add value, how they can be used profitably for everyone in development, and where the limits currently lie.

speakers.bio: David Tielke is a consultant, coach, and trainer with over 15 years of experience in high-level software development. His expertise focuses on software quality, software architecture, and agile development processes. Having trained more than 30,000 participants, delivered over 150 presentations at renowned conferences, and published numerous articles in industry magazines, he has shared his knowledge and expertise widely. On his YouTube channel, he shares his passion for software development and provides practical insights into current industry topics.

sessions.title: Vertical Slice Architecture: Micro Frontends Across Mobile, Backend & ML
sessions.description:
Micro frontends are often discussed in the context of web applications. But what if we extend the idea beyond UI - into mobile, backend services, and machine learning?

In this talk, I’ll present a vertical slice architecture where each feature team owns everything required to deliver business value:
- Android UI
- Kotlin Multiplatform shared domain logic
- Kotlin backend service
- Python-based ML model
- API contracts

Instead of organizing codebases by technical layers, we organize by business features. This reduces coordination overhead, increases deployment independence, and aligns teams with product outcomes.

speakers.bio:
Jens Posingies is an Engineering Manager at Redcare Pharmacy with 25 years of experience in software development, architecture and engineering leadership. Born and raised in East Germany, he studied Business Information Systems at the University of Lüneburg and Media & Education at the University of Rostock.

Over his career, Jens has worked in roles ranging from Java and Node.js developer to Solution Architect and Engineering Manager, including positions at Zalando, TUI, HRS and Redcare Pharmacy. His current focus is on lateral leadership, end-to-end ownership and modular software architectures such as microservices, microfrontends and Kotlin Multiplatform.

He is also a certified nerd with deep expertise in Back to the Future and Star Trek.

I'm developing Software for over 20 years now. I've worked at different companies from small 10 men startups up to huge enterprises operating world wide both freelance and as an employee.
For a while I ran my own small personal fitness training studio and dabbled in many side projects that had nothing to do with software development but focused on communication and working with people.

Over time I acquired a lot of experience and knowledge regarding learning and skill development. Be it as a personal trainer, father to two kids (and one dog) or husband to a great agile coach and systemic organizational developer. How people learn and how systems evolve and develop in positive ways has become a passion topic of mine.

I am a Staff Engineer in the Mobile team at Redcare Pharmacy, specializing in Android application development and large-scale architecture design. I lead architectural transformations that enable teams to adopt multi-module strategies and take ownership of domain-driven development within a shared codebase.

I advocate clean architectural patterns that improve scalability, build performance, and team autonomy. Recently, I have been exploring a feature-driven “micro frontend” approach across mobile and backend systems, leveraging Kotlin Multiplatform to share business logic while preserving native UI independence on Android and iOS.

sessions.title: Fighting the Next Wave of Cybercrime
sessions.description: Attackers have AI now too, and defense is racing to keep up. This panel brings together security practitioners to discuss what the next wave of cybercrime looks like and how the fight is changing. A frank conversation about where the threats are heading and how to stay ahead.

speakers.bio: Michele Zuccala is Senior Vice President of Engineering at Sysdig, leading the global engineering organization across open source R&D, runtime security, monitoring, AI, and core platform areas. He drives technical execution and innovation across both commercial and open source initiatives. Michele joined Sysdig to lead the open source team and helped scale Falco, guiding the CNCF runtime security project to graduation and broad industry adoption. He later unified open source and commercial development for runtime security before expanding his leadership across the wider engineering organization. Before Sysdig, he was CTO at ProntoPro and held leadership roles at several high-growth startups.

Misha Bragin is a Berlin-based tech entrepreneur, software engineer, and open source contributor who is the co-founder and CEO of NetBird, an open source cybersecurity company focused on Zero Trust networking.

Ross Kukulinski is Vice President of Product at Tailscale, where he leads product strategy and execution across the company's secure connectivity platform.

Ross is a technical product leader who builds businesses at the intersection of open source, cloud infrastructure, and developer experience. He has spent nearly two decades working on cloud-native technologies, helping organizations move from early adoption to reliable, large-scale production use. His work has focused on turning complex distributed systems into products that are easier to adopt, operate, and trust—without hiding the underlying engineering or limiting flexibility.

Before joining Tailscale, Ross was VP of Product Management at Kong, where he led the transformation from packaged on-prem software to a cloud-first platform. Prior to Kong, he spent four years at VMware following the acquisition of Heptio, where he was an initial employee. At Heptio and VMware, he led product for Kubernetes platforms including Tanzu Kubernetes Grid, working closely with engineering teams and customers to shape how modern infrastructure is packaged, delivered, and operated.

Ross is passionate about building products that balance strong technical foundations with clear customer value, mentoring future product leaders, and earning developer trust through great experiences.

Frank Schlesinger is CIO DACH Platforms and a member of the management team at Nexi Germany. In his role, he is responsible for IT and infrastructure in Germany and the entire DACH region. 

In previous positions, he was Head of Technology at ImmobilienScout24 and, prior to that, Head of Agile and Project Management, where he was responsible for cloud migration and a major organisational restructuring. 

After graduating with a degree in computer science, Frank began his career as a Java software developer, helping German banks and savings banks equip their teams with modern technology for banking processes.

Tomislav Tipurić is a business and technology professional who connects technical expertise with business strategy to clarify new technologies and drive corporate innovation. As a Chief Technology Officer and Lead Hackathon Coach, he focuses on artificial intelligence, multi-cloud platforms, and modern software development practices. He has designed more than 20 custom learning hackathons and delivered over 500 hands-on educational experiences, including more than 150 sessions dedicated to machine learning, generative AI, and agentic AI solutions. Tomislav translates technical concepts into business strategies, delivering workshops and lectures to C-level executives on the business impact of AI in their industries. Additionally, he has experience in sales enablement and corporate training, serving as a program trainer to help enterprise partners market, sell, and adopt generative AI and cloud productivity tools, and delivering cloud sales enablement programs for global telecommunications firms. Tomislav is certified and authorized as an official trainer by Google and Microsoft.

sessions.title: PLACEHOLDER - Asrar Khan (Google Cloud)

speakers.bio: Asrar Khan

sessions.title: The Sound of Your Secrets: Teaching Your Model to Spy, So You Can Learn to Defend
sessions.description:
Every keyboard has a sound signature. Every click and clack carries information. With deep learning and a decent microphone, that information can be weaponized. In this session, we'll explore how modern AI models can identify what you're typing just from the sound of your keyboard. Using a dataset of recorded keystrokes and an open source sound classification pipeline, we'll walk through building a model that can recover text with startling accuracy. You'll see firsthand how a few lines of Python and a trained network can turn your laptop into an acoustic fingerprint.

But this talk isn't about enabling surveillance... it's about understanding it to fight back. We'll unpack why uniform keyboard layouts and consistent typing styles make these attacks so effective, then explore real countermeasures: signal masking, password entropy, and environmental noise defenses. You'll leave with a practical understanding of how these attacks work, how to reproduce them for research or awareness, and how to harden your systems (and yourself) against them.

speakers.bio:
David is a Senior AI/ML Engineer within the Office of the CTO at NetApp, where he’s dedicated to empowering developers to build, scale, and deploy AI/ML solutions in production environments. He brings deep expertise in building and training models for applications such as NLP, vision, real-time analytics, and even classifying debilitating diseases. His mission is to help users build, train, and deploy AI models efficiently, making advanced machine learning accessible to users of all levels.

Before NetApp, he was heavily involved in the AI/ML community, specifically in conversational AI solutions and driving AI platform growth in a DevRel and pre-sales role. David frequently shares his insights at industry conferences and events, offering hands-on guidance for implementing AI/ML in cloud environments. David's prior experience includes contributing to the Kubernetes and CNCF ecosystems, working hands-on with VMware virtualization, implementing backup/recovery solutions, and developing hardware storage adapter firmware and drivers.

sessions.title: Super scaling for the Super Bowl: How to survive 30 million users hitting your backend in 30 minutes
sessions.description: The Super Bowl attracts millions of viewers, and an unprecedented surge in ad traffic. One of adjoe partners secured an advertising slot, requiring us to be prepared for 30M users hitting our backend in less than an hour. We relied on load testing, application optimizations, pre-scaling and increased cloud quotas to withstand extreme loads. Learn how we scaled an ultra-resilient Golang backend to successfully handle traffic spike at one of the biggest events in the world.

speakers.bio: Dr. Irina Brancovic is a Software Engineer at the Hamburg-based adtech company, adjoe. She earned her PhD in Information Engineering in Italy, where she not only conducted academic research but also taught at the university level in Italian, a language she learned from scratch during her studies. Since joining adjoe, Irina has been instrumental in overcoming challenges to build the backend infrastructure, which handles billions of requests from 600 million daily users. She takes pride in her contributions to creating innovative ad technologies and fostering a collaborative and productive work environment.

sessions.title: Running Secure Life Science Research at Scale using Hybrid GPU HPC and Kubernetes 🧬
sessions.description:
AI is transforming life sciences research at institutions such as Imperial College London, but there is a hard infrastructure reality: most research institutes do not have enough on-prem HPC or GPU capacity to meet the growing demand from AI workloads. Researchers need secure access to serious compute, but sensitive data, governance, reproducibility, and sovereignty cannot be an afterthought. 🚀

This session shows how stack8s uses Kubernetes as a global control plane for sovereign AI research. Institutions can start with their own on-premises Kubernetes environment, then securely burst workloads into more than 30 Neo clouds without requiring researchers to manage individual cloud accounts. The result is a governed AI platform where data, policy, identity, and TRE controls remain under institutional control, while GPU and HPC workloads can scale globally on demand. 🌍

Drawing on real-world examples from UKDRI teams, including Imperial College London, working on Dementia, Parkinsons and neurogenomics, the talk will share practical patterns for building Kubernetes-enabled Trusted Research Environments, isolated research enclaves, notebooks, pipelines, private models, shared storage, GPU scheduling, and observability.

speakers.bio:
Jeremy Murray is the Founder and CEO of Stack8s, a UK-based company on a mission to commoditise GPU compute on Kubernetes and make AI infrastructure as accessible and interchangeable as electricity.

With over a decade of experience in enterprise cloud transformation, Jeremy has led large-scale migrations from legacy platforms to modern cloud environments across highly regulated industries. He holds a PhD from the University of Edinburgh, has authored more than 12 peer-reviewed research papers, and is the inventor of three patents spanning AI, IoT, and advanced computing technologies.

Before founding Stack8s, Jeremy worked across cloud, data, and infrastructure programmes, helping organisations modernise mission-critical systems while balancing performance, security, and compliance requirements. Today, he is focused on solving two of the biggest challenges facing AI adoption: GPU scarcity and cloud lock-in.

Under his leadership, Stack8s has built a sovereign-by-design AI compute platform that enables organisations to access and orchestrate GPU resources across multiple cloud providers, data centres, and on-premises environments through a unified Kubernetes control plane. The company works with research institutes, enterprises, and startups to accelerate AI innovation while maintaining control over data sovereignty and compliance.

Jeremy is a regular speaker on Kubernetes, AI infrastructure, sovereign cloud, and multi-cloud computing, advocating for a future where compute becomes a true commodity and innovation is no longer constrained by infrastructure availability.

sessions.title: Angstfreude - AI and Corporate Culture - The Thrill and the Threat
sessions.description: AI delivers in three minutes – and suddenly you’re not sure whether to feel impressed or obsolete. We hired an AI employee named Ada, sent ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini to 2035, and compared their view with People & Culture leaders already shaping this transformation. The result is provocative, funny, and uncomfortably familiar. Why is everyone suddenly a manager? Why can measuring culture destroy it? And why might Europe’s biggest weakness become its advantage?

speakers.bio:
Mag. Axel Ebert is founding partner of wortwelt® and co-creator of identifire®. With experience across more than 300 wording projects for financial services, industry, telecoms, NGOs and public administration, he has established himself as the "Letter Man" – and, since adopting AI, also as 'Chief Prompting Officer'. The only C-level title you actually have to earn every single day. Motto: from artificial to creative intelligence.
wortwelt.at – we craft language. AI pitches in. Jargon-free.

Dr. Karin Krobath is founding partner of identifire® – Austria's first agency for Employer Branding and Corporate Culture. Her focus for 20 years: start from the inside. She turns brands into movements, employees into ambassadors, and "yeah-whatever" sayers into genuine fans. With sharp positioning and strong formats – including Learning Journeys for innovation enthusiasts to Nairobi and Kigali.
identifire.at – 20 years of corporate culture. Now also prompt.

sessions.title: Unlocking the AI Black Box: Building Trust in the Era of Agentic Production
sessions.description:
AI is moving from the lab into production, but traditional monitoring can’t keep up. A system might look perfect on a dashboard while it hallucinates, makes poor decisions, or loops through expensive token calls. These are behavioral failures, not technical bugs, and they often stay hidden inside the black box.
This talk looks at the practical hurdles of scaling AI. We will discuss why teams are currently debugging in the dark and how silent failures in agentic workflows break user trust.
Observability is key to shift from tracking system health to evaluating decisions. We will look at how end-to-end visibility into LLM operations, including prompt tracking and tool usage, provides the data needed for reliability and cost control. This session offers a strategy for building the observability foundations required to scale enterprise AI safely and predictably

speakers.bio: I am a Full-stack Engineer turned DevRel Engineer at New Relic. With a background in software engineering and marketing direction, my experience involves: designing and developing user interfaces, addressing customer/client pain points, marketing automation, and community development.

Passionate software craftsman with 29+ years experience across development technologies and cloud-native architectures. Expertise with Microsoft Aspire, distributed systems (Dapr), and AI-assisted development including Copilot SDK integration. Focus on application modernization, observability, and securing these environments.

As a Developer Relations Engineer at New Relic, Harry helps engineers, DevOps, and SREs implement proactive observability at scale - including observability for AI systems - leveraging open source technologies and the New Relic platform.

sessions.title: Fine-Tuning Small Language Models for Agentic AI
sessions.description:
Agentic AI promises significant business value, yet many proof-of-concepts fail in production: Large foundation models are expensive, slow, and vulnerable to prompt injection, a risk that escalates when agents interact with untrusted data sources like emails, websites, or documents.

Specialized, fine-tuned Small Language Models (SLMs) with just a few billion parameters can address these challenges. They offer enforceable control through domain constraints and structured outputs, dramatically reduced costs and latency, and inherent security through limited complexity.

This talk explores two approaches to creating specialized agents: Distillation via Synthetic Data (using large models as "teachers" to train smaller "student" models) and Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR).

The core of this presentation focuses on practical evaluation: We present original experiments applying these techniques to real-world agentic applications. If you have a successful agentic AI application, this approach can help make it more efficient without loss of quality, but it doesn't always work. We evaluate when specialized SLMs can replace or even outperform large models, and when they fall short. Attendees will leave with concrete guidance on where this technique delivers value and where it doesn't.

speakers.bio: Björn Buchhold is a Technology Evangelist at CID, specializing in AI and Data Science. With a PhD in Computer Science and over 15 years of experience in academia and industry, he helps organizations navigate the full journey from AI strategy and data architecture to hands-on ML implementation.

sessions.title: Completing the Feedback Loop
sessions.description: Coding via AI agents is a solved problem. We've come a long way from the "tab experience" Cursor has given us to approaches like "Ralph Wiggum". And almost every time - the AI comes up short, leaving you to implement the finishing touches (if not more). In this talk we will explore how we can give the agent  the leg-up it needs to really complete the task.

speakers.bio: Nimrod Kor is a software engineer specializing in code analysis, cloud security, and developer tooling. As the #1 contributor to Checkov, he has helped shape best practices in Infrastructure as Code (IaC) security. His work on open-source projects like Checkov, Yor, and Airiam—focused on creating and analyzing code—has been central to his past talks.

sessions.title: Structured Concurrency in Practice: CoroutineScope vs StructuredTaskScope
sessions.description:
Structured concurrency is a familiar concept to Kotlin developers, and CoroutineScope has long been the go-to tool for managing lifecycles and structuring asynchronous code in a safe, predictable way. But now, the Java ecosystem is stepping up: Java’s new StructuredTaskScope API brings structured concurrency concepts natively to the platform, and it’s capturing a lot of attention with its promise of clarity and safety in concurrent programming.

Since Kotlin and Java work very well together, we'll see how we can use this new API from Kotlin and how it's better or worse from the already known CoroutineScope API.

We’ll explore real-world use cases that require structured concurrency, then take a deep dive into both CoroutineScope and StructuredTaskScope. We’ll compare their APIs, features, and design philosophies.

speakers.bio: Software engineer with extensive experience building robust backend and frontend applications. Skilled in writing clean, maintainable code, and optimizing performance and scalability of complex systems. Proven ability to lead critical architecture migrations, mentor development teams, and deliver efficient, impactful solutions under pressure. Passionate collaborator and continuous learner, eager to improve and share knowledge.

sessions.title: The Sound of Privacy – What Your Spotify Data Reveals About You
sessions.description:
It's just a music app – but how much can one actually learn about a person when granted access to their Spotify data? In this talk, we present what we learned about our colleagues through their Spotify user data.

By leveraging the GDPR, we looked into various questions: How often do people lose or damage their phones? Where did they travel? And how regular are their sleep patterns? Alongside detailed insights into Spotify data, we provide a brief overview of the legal framework and examine how strictly other companies comply with the GDPR. While everyone in theory understands that data can hold immense power, this talk presents concrete examples – particularly given that we're dealing with what seems like just a music app.

speakers.bio: Dennis Schulz is a Senior Consultant at TNG Technology Consulting. He holds a PhD in low temperature physics from the University of Heidelberg. Besides being a programmer, he organized and hosted the TV show Quasi Klar for RNF, published a book that was translated to Korean and Russian, and won Science Slam competitions all over Germany. As a part of the Innovation Hacking team at TNG, he worked on different AI showcases, fine-tuning embeddings, and data mining.

Thomas Hugle is a Senior Consultant with TNG Technology Consulting, with a focus on AI and frontend development. His PhD thesis at the Max-Planck-Institut für Kernphysik in Heidelberg was mostly about data analysis for the CONUS neutrino experiment and computer simulations of physical systems. Besides his work as a software consultant, he handles data mining projects, to apply his data analysis skills to the Spotify data of his colleagues, and gives talks about how to turn from a physicist into a software developer.

sessions.title: Your Enterprise RAG Has No Legal Basis
sessions.description:
Your RAG system works beautifully. Under GDPR, it has no legal basis to exist.

Clean architecture. Proper embeddings. Maybe even agentic tool calling. You followed best practices. But when the auditor asks "what's the legal basis for this processing?" there's no answer. General-purpose "ask anything" chatbots have no defined purpose. Without a defined purpose, no legal basis can exist under GDPR. The architecture itself is the violation.

In this talk, I'll live-code a "best practices" enterprise knowledge bot, then ask the questions nobody asks: Where is purpose limitation enforced? Where is legal basis documented? I'll show why anonymization doesn't save you. 97% accuracy isn't "anonymous" under GDPR. It's still PII.

Then I'll show you the fix: a purpose-scoped bot architecture where legal basis is a first-class configuration item. Each bot gets a defined purpose, scoped data access, configured tools, and documented legal basis. The architecture enforces the boundaries. The retrieval layer can only access documents within that bot's scope. Non-compliance becomes architecturally impossible.

The good news? You didn't waste your investment. This is a 50k governance layer, not a rewrite.

Takeaways:
  - Why your RAG has no legal basis and how to fix it
  - The purpose-scoped bot pattern: document, enforce, and audit compliance by design
  - A brownfield rescue roadmap for existing systems

speakers.bio:
Hybrid AI and enterprise technology leader with 10+ years designing, implementing, and scaling production-grade AI platforms in complex enterprise environments. Bridges executive vision with technical delivery. From strategy and governance down to architecture, infrastructure, and code.

As Enterprise Architect at Rödl & Partner (~7,000 employees), architected a multi-tenant generative AI platform serving 6,000+ users with EU AI Act-aligned access controls. Led enterprise data strategy and Azure Integration Services rollout at enterprise scale. Previous experience includes ITSM transformation for German public sector (VBG) and AWS data architecture for Deutsche Bahn subsidiary.

Tilman Mürle is Managing Director and Co-Founder of Komplyzen, where he helps organizations turn AI governance from a compliance exercise into an operational capability. He brings over a decade of leadership experience across SaaS, enterprise IT, and regulated environments, including his role as former CEO EMEA at Valiantys.

Tilman works with engineering, risk, and leadership teams to design AI systems that are measurable, auditable, and production-ready by design. His focus lies on operational AI governance, AI risk management, and translating regulatory expectations into practical architecture and workflows that scale.

He regularly speaks at industry conferences on AI risk, governance, and the realities of bringing AI safely into production.

sessions.title: YOLO Developer Workflows with a Coding Agent in a Box
sessions.description:
As coding agents like Claude Code, OpenAI Codex and Gemini CLI become part of developers’ daily workflows, one problem looms large: we’re letting them run wild on our machines (or put ourselves into a Skinner box, playing an agent-approval slot machine game). The ideal workflow is “YOLO mode". where an agent can freely edit, build, and test code. But that freedom shouldn’t come at the cost of safety or control.

In this talk, we’ll explore how Docker is building an opinionated developer experience for running coding agents safely inside sandboxes, combining the isolation benefits of containers with the seamless UX developers expect. We’ll discuss what we learned from Devcontainers (and why they never saw broad adoption), and how sandboxes evolve those ideas to make “safe by default” not just possible, but pleasant.

You’ll leave with a new mental model for local coding agent based development: fast, flexible, and fearless, the YOLO workflow done right.

speakers.bio:
Manuel is an OSS Software Engineer at Docker where he works in the Clients & SDKs team, also maintaining "Testcontainers for Go". Since 2003, he has held various roles in different parts of the development process, starting in 2003 working for the regional public administration in Castilla-La Mancha (Spain) until 2007, then worked at more traditional consulting firms. In 2011, he transitioned to product-oriented and Open Source companies, where he has served as a support engineer, trainer, and Core Engineer at Liferay, as QA Tech lead at Liferay Cloud. From 2019 to 2022 he was involved in Engineering Productivity at Elastic as part of the Observability product, and since 2022 doing pure OSS at AtomicJar, which was acquired by Docker in Dec' 23. In every job he tries to improve the quality of the software products and processes from the automation and testing point of view.

He has also founded and managed a couple of small web development and systems consulting companies. Additionally, he organises the Google Developers Group in Toledo, Spain (GDG Toledo), where they run monthly discussions about software in its various aspects, serving as a small community outside the bustling Madrid. Manuel has delivered talks at different national and international events too.

Manuel holds a BS in Computer Science (UNED Spain), and a master degree in Research in Software Engineering and Information Systems (UNED Spain). You can find him on Internet as "mdelapenya" everywhere.

sessions.title: Code Once, Use Everywhere: Building Shared Libraries for Multiple Projects
sessions.description:
Developers who have worked on shared libraries know that maintaining them can quickly become a headache, often leading to bugs or bottlenecks across projects.

* What should go into a shared library?
* How do you keep it small and easy to maintain?
* How do you update it without breaking everything?

These and other questions arose when I built a shared Java library that supports integration with Spring Boot across multiple projects in my company. 
In this talk, I’ll show how I addressed these challenges and developed a solution that has worked successfully for over three years. I'll walk you through the structure and code so you can see exactly how it’s done and avoid common mistakes.

By the end, you’ll have actionable insights to start building or improving your own shared libraries that support Spring Boot.

Join my talk to discover practical solutions you can apply to your projects right away, whether you're just starting or looking to enhance an existing shared codebase. 

I look forward to seeing you at my talk!

speakers.bio:
Hands-on Architect, IT Trainer, and Conference Speaker with 20+ years of experience, passionate about building high-quality software, mentoring developers, and sharing knowledge.

I've trained over 550 professionals in Architecture, EventStorming, and TDD, built and deployed more than 10 production-ready projects, led multiple development teams, and created a successful Java department from scratch. Along the way, I’ve mentored 20+ developers and conducted 100+ technical interviews.

My expertise lies in Java, but I also have experience with Kotlin, Groovy, and other programming languages. I believe that strong technical skills, continuous learning, teamwork, and knowledge-sharing are the keys to success in software development.

sessions.title: Engineering/Manager Pendulum: Generating compound interest on your career
sessions.description:
Most career advice tells you to climb the ladder, one step at a time. I'm going to tell you why I jumped off it - and why that "demotion" became the best decision of my career.

I started as an engineer in startups, then moved into management at a large company, leading around 40 people. But I had never been an engineer at that scale myself, and it showed. So I did something that surprised everyone: I swung back to an individual contributor role.

It wasn't free. I reported to managers with less experience than me, so I had to keep my ego low. And a zig-zag CV made recruiters suspicious, which made offers harder to get.

But the return was bigger than I expected. My years as a manager made me a sharper engineer - I knew which fights were worth having and which to drop. And when I swung back to management, I didn't start from zero. I came back with fresh technical confidence and real empathy for my team, because I had just lived their reality.

I'll show you why the pendulum isn't really a back-and-forth motion. Done right, it's a curve that keeps going up.

speakers.bio:
Marcin is an Engineering Manager with over a decade of experience spanning small startups to big enterprises. Having served in multiple roles - from Senior Engineer to Tech Lead and Manager - he treats his career as a laboratory for leadership and philosophy.

Most recently, Marcin navigated a unique "Pendulum" career arc: transitioning from management roles in large organizations (like William Hill and NetEnt) to a Staff Engineer role at Qonto, before returning to leadership. He uses this cross-functional experience to help teams bridge the gap between backend infrastructure and organizational strategy, advocating for "Radical Career Ownership" along the way.

sessions.title: Cultural Intelligence: How to Build Teams That Work Anywhere Building belonging, not just headcount.
sessions.description: In today’s borderless world, technical excellence isn’t enough — cultural intelligence (CQ) has become the real differentiator. This keynote unpacks how CQ helps global teams communicate better, collaborate smarter, and perform stronger across time zones and cultures. You’ll walk away with fresh insights and practical tools to build teams that don’t just work everywhere — they thrive anywhere.

speakers.bio: With 20+ years in Global Talent Acquisition and Employer Branding, I have built high-performing teams across industries and led global remote teams for 6+ years. Passionate about community building, I’ve founded several HR and recruitment groups to foster collaboration and growth. I’ve also served as a jury member for employer branding competitions. As an international speaker and panelist, I share insights on recruitment, sourcing, employer branding, and the future of work, helping organizations attract and retain top talent while strengthening their ability to lead remote teams effectively.

sessions.title: Europe's Regulatory Paradox: Burden, Barrier or Competitive Advantage?
sessions.description: Europe's rules are often blamed for slowing its tech sector - but some argue they could become an edge. This panel takes the paradox head on, with founders who build under that regulation every day. An honest debate about whether the rules hold Europe back or push it toward something stronger.

speakers.bio: Andreas Klinger is the founder of PROTOTYPE, an early-stage VC fund investing in European frontier tech. As former CTO and engineer at Product Hunt and AngelList, amongst others, he backs radical founders with crazy ideas as early as possible. Andreas is convinced Europe can be the best place on earth to build hardware. He is building a community of hyper-ambitious European builders with his fast-growing YouTube channel and is engineering the ecosystem with hyper-ambitious projects like the policy movement EU-INC, the robotics club network ESRA and a few others.

Jakobus Schuster is the founder and CEO of Notarity, the leading platform for digital notary appointments in Europe. He studied law at Bucerius Law School and worked in international law firms during his studies. Already as a student, he developed a strong interest in legal tech, taught himself fundamental coding skills, and implemented his first legal data science projects.

After completing his legal clerkship in Hamburg, he began building Notarity, which is now used worldwide to handle notarial processes digitally, efficiently, and independently of location.

Thomas Eriksson, Founder and CEO of SASHA- Safe Share, is a Danish technology leader at the forefront of watermarking technology. Driven by the mission to end online image abuse, Thomas developed SASHA’s patented "in-pixel" signature technology that embeds ownership and consent directly into digital content.

As a serial entrepreneur with a background in edtech and medtech, Thomas has scaled SASHA from a vision to a robust product presented at the EU Presidency Meeting for Digital Affairs and awarded the 2025 World Summit Global Champion Award. SASHA tech provides platforms with the tools to streamline moderation and neutralize image abuse at the source.

Katharina Schneeberg is Head of Innovation & Technology at the German Association for the digital economy (BVDW). In this role, she is deeply engaged in developments and trends in emerging and future-oriented technologies. Alongside leading and coordinating projects and initiatives, she also acts as a source of inspiration and a technology expert for the digital economy, the association, and its members. In addition, she regularly serves as a moderator and speaker at industry events and conferences.

5 Things in Tech That Matter Now - 2026 Edition

speakers.bio: Former Yahoo, Mozilla, Microsoft. JavaScript and Accessibility expert. Worked on Chromium Developer Tools. Author of the Developer Advocacy Handbook, LinkedIn Learning and Skillshare Trainer. Cinephile.

Senior Developer at Google Cloud. Co-host of the Kubernetes Podcast by Google and CNCF Ambassador. My focus areas include High-Scalable distributed systems on Kubernetes, Service Mesh, and Serverless. With a background in datacenter scale architecture, operations, and consulting. He seeks to reimagine the plumbing behind business process to effect step changes in productivity.
Much of his focus is now on building the frameworks that lets the Cronofy team do their best work. Other than that he's a keen cyclist, clumsy language learner and hates wasting time.

Adele is a Software Engineer and Consultant at Trifork Amsterdam where she is working on systems for the educational sector. Most of her work day is spent in the JVM/Spring and React ecosystems, although increasingly she plays a pivotal role as trusted advisor to Trifork’s customers.

Adele is an experienced international speaker, having spoken at multiple editions of NDC, goto, Devoxx and JavaZone. As a speaker, she uses her exposure to real-world customer projects, experiences outside of tech and passion for story-telling to distill complex ideas into their essential parts. All with an air of good humour.

When she’s not at her computer or on stage, you can find her in the gym pumping some serious iron as she pursues the sport of powerlifting.

Adrian has been involved with containers from the early days of Docker and authored the O’Reilly book “Using Docker”. 

He works at Chainguard  whose mission is to make the software lifecycle secure by default. His current focus is on improving the standard of security and provenance guarantees in container images.

Agur Jõgi is an experienced technology leader with extensive experience in building and scaling SaaS platforms. He most recently served as Chief Technology Officer at Pipedrive, a leading sales CRM platform used by over 100,000 companies globally. With his 35 years of experience across Skype, Bigbank, Elion, and askRobin, Agur shares insights into his leadership through engineering and product innovations. At Pipedrive, he has championed the adoption of data-driven product strategies, blending deep technical execution with a user-first mindset.

Ahmed Tikiwa is a Senior Software Engineer at Disney and a Google Developer Expert for Android with over 12 years of software engineering experience and possesses a strong passion for developing world-class applications that can help make a difference. He has experience in frontend and backend systems, where he has built enterprise solutions for major telecommunication companies. He is currently a Senior Software Engineer based in Utrecht, The Netherlands, and an official contributor to Google Developers Dev Library.

Ainhoa is a Senior Software Engineer at Twilio specializing in cloud infrastructure. Over the past four years, she has driven initiatives to modernize Twilio's public and internal edge infrastructure, executing a long-term vision that has systematically elevated security and performance standards. As an infrastructure owner, she and her team build the tooling that abstracts away complexity, enabling internal teams to seamlessly deploy their own ingresses while ensuring a reliable, high-performance experience for end users.
When she isn't modernizing edge routing, Ainhoa can usually be found away from the terminal—either hitting the basketball court or out on a trail hiking.

Alberts Jekabsons is a Developer Evangelist at HERE Technologies, the world’s leading mapping company, with over 12 years of experience working with location data. With a background in foundation engineering, he has built a strong foundation in geospatial data and production-ready pipelines on AWS, working with services like Lambda and S3 to process and manage location data at scale.
His invested interest in data visualization led him to develop spatial analytics solutions as a React frontend developer, before shifting his focus to supporting developers in HERE’s growing developer ecosystem.
At WeAreDevelopers, Alberts will help participants learn how to identify common failure points, design for reliability, and ship robust location-driven systems.

About HERE Technologies

HERE is the global leader in mapping and location technology. For over 40 years, we’ve been powering innovation for the world’s most recognizable companies: from launching our first digital map in 1985, to shaping the future of software-defined vehicles today. With the industry’s freshest and richest unified map and a portfolio of products, services and solutions that serve the needs of multiple industries, HERE reveals opportunities that drive progress and unlock new possibilities for every moving vehicle. Discover more at here.com.

I’m Alex, CPO and co-founder at Resizes, building infrastructure that removes operational friction for scaling companies. We help teams ship faster, control cloud costs, and scale confidently without vendor lock-in.

I'm a Principal PM at UiPath, an engineer at heart and always looking at everyday things to figure out how they could work better. Ask me anything about how to develop, deploy, and operate in UiPath products.

Alex Olivier is the Co-founder & CPO at Cerbos, an open-source authorization platform, and co-chair of the OpenID AuthZEN working group, which standardizes fine-grained authorization. He specializes in authorization systems, with a recent focus on workload identity and AI security.

With over a decade of experience building and scaling authorization systems at Microsoft, Qubit, Zencargo, and multiple startups, Alex has published extensively on securing Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and AI agents. A frequent speaker at events on authorization, AI, security, and identity, including KubeCon, Google Cloud NEXT, Identiverse, and EIC, he combines standards development with practical implementation experience, focusing on the future of authorization for traditional applications, distributed workloads, and emerging AI systems.

As Chief Technology Officer, Alexander Gerfer is responsible for establishing the Würth Elektronik eiSos Group as a technology enabler and service leader. The company is one of Europe's largest manufacturers of electronic and electromechanical components and part of the Würth Group. Gerfer began his career at Würth Elektronik eiSos 29 years ago as a technical sales representative. Since then, he has held various positions in the company. 
In his role as CTO, he particularly promotes technology partnerships in future-oriented areas. With his expert knowledge, he passionately supports and advises innovative start-ups, especially when they encounter unexpected technical problems in the implementation of their ideas. 
In line with the principle ‘Electronics for Positive Impact’, Alexander Gerfer is committed to sustainable, environmentally friendly and socially forward-looking technologies. For example, the company is researching LED light recipes for optimal photosynthesis and more efficient electronics. Würth Elektronik particularly supports innovative, ecological start-ups, for example in the areas of algae and mushroom cultivation, harvesting robots or biodiversity measurement.
During the course of his career, Gerfer has also played a key role in the development and expansion of research and development, as well as product, quality and supply chain management. As an author of specialist books, the electrical engineer and trained radio and television technician presents complex interrelations in an easy-to-understand and practical way. He is also a sought-after keynote speaker at conferences around the world.

Dr. Alexander Glätzle is CEO and co-founder of planqc, the company building Germany’s first quantum computer based on neutral atoms. A quantum physicist turned entrepreneur, he is on a mission to turn quantum technology from a scientific breakthrough into a powerful industrial tool. 

After leading research on ultracold atoms and quantum annealing at the University of Oxford and the Centre for Quantum Technologies in Singapore, Glätzle moved into industry to shape strategies for quantum computing and blockchain at a leading global consultancy. In 2018, he took the leap to build the future himself. 

Today, he is driving Europe’s quantum ambition with planqc. Backed by €50 million in funding, the company is developing a 1,000-qubit quantum computer for the Leibniz Supercomputing Centre and building quantum computing capabilities for research and industry. His vision is bold and clear: quantum computers made in Europe, solving real-world problems worth billions.

Alex is a Senior Solutions Architect at AWS with 20 years of IT experience in expert and leadership roles. He is a strong advocate of agile and DevOps practices, and he enjoys seeing serverless, cloud-native and event-driven architectures deployed at scale. He has delivered large transformation projects and successfully developed own and customers’ businesses. As an international speaker, he has held advanced technology sessions at a wide range of events.

Alexander Opalic is a Senior Full Stack Developer from Germany. He writes at alexop.dev about Vue, TypeScript, and AI in software. He speaks at international conferences on local first apps, testing, and building better developer experiences.

Alexander Schwartz is a Principal Software Engineer at IBM working full time as a Keycloak maintainer and technical team lead. At work and in his spare time he codes for Open Source projects. In previous jobs he worked as a software architect and IT consultant. At conferences and user groups he talks about JavaScript front ends, Java back ends, Kubernetes and single-sign-on with Keycloak.

Alexander Schwarz is a Robotics Solutions Architect at NVIDIA, based in Zurich, Switzerland, where he helps customers and developers accelerate AI training and robotics simulation with NVIDIA Cosmos and Isaac.

Alexander holds an M.Sc. in Robotics, Cognition, and Intelligence from the Technical University of Munich, and conducted research in computer vision and robot learning at ETH Zurich, Johns Hopkins University, and Delft University of Technology. He is passionate about guiding developers and product teams to build next-generation Physical AI workflows.

He regularly delivers hands-on DLI workshops and technical talks at leading developer conferences including WeAreDevelopers, GTC, and SIGGRAPH, covering topics from physical AI simulation pipelines to industrial digital twins with Omniverse.

Alexandre is the founder and CEO of Kiro. He defines Kiro’s vision and strategic direction and serves as the guardian of the company’s culture.

Alexandre graduated from ESSEC in Paris (Management) and from UC Berkeley in California (Innovation and Law), and specialized in innovation at the Luigi Bocconi University in Milan. He was recently named among Forbes’ 30 Under 30 and Choiseul’s 100. Passionate about the contribution of new technologies to improving healthcare at scale, Alexandre decided to create a leading company to help every patient benefit from technology and access better care.

As a strong advocate for Linux, I bring extensive knowledge of IT systems, specializing in deploying containerization tools and Kubernetes to improve operational efficiency and scalability. My work focuses on designing resilient, cutting-edge systems that stay ahead of the technological curve.

Aliaksandr is a co-founder and the principal architect of VictoriaMetrics. He is also a well-known author of the popular performance-oriented libraries: fasthttp, fastcache and quicktemplate. Her work spans AI governance, physical AI, and retrieval research—she recently won "Most Impressive Project" at Physical AI Hack 2026 for a VLA-powered robot that reads books to children. Alison serves on the US NIST Generative AI Public Working Group and has studied AI and Data Science at Northwestern University at the Master's level. Previously, he served as Director of AI Engineering at SambaNova Systems, architecting Generative AI solutions on novel accelerator hardware, and as Principal Data Scientist at ExxonMobil, applying machine learning to complex physical and industrial systems. He holds a Ph.D. in Engineering from Stanford University, where his research focused on scientific computing and large-scale simulations.

Ana is a Java Champion Alumni, Senior Developer Advocate for the Java Platform Group at Oracle, with an extensive background in Java and cloud development, while contributing to Java community growth and events. Her exposure to varied technical challenges involving Java-based frameworks has endowed her with deep insights into the evolution of the JDK tools, practical use, and advanced features. To learn more about/from her, follow her on Twitter @ammbra1508.

Ana holds a bachelor's degree in Business Management and IT from University of Hull, Post Graduate degree in Human Resources from University of Kingston, UK and has completed Harvard Business School Executive Education.

Anaëlle is an ML / AI Engineer at Microsoft, working on large-scale production systems where machine learning is part of critical, user-facing workflows. Her work focuses on designing reliable, scalable AI systems that operate under real-world constraints.

With a background spanning software engineering and machine learning, Anaëlle is particularly interested in the intersection of AI, distributed systems, and platform engineering—and in helping teams move from experimental models to production systems they can confidently operate.

Anastasia holds a diploma in Mathematics and works as a Quality Engineering Specialist at Accenture, where she leads the Test Data Management practice. With a background in mathematics and a passion for problem solving, she specializes in designing strategies that combine effective testing with data safety, security, and compliance. With a strong focus on data protection and a particular interest in AI security, her work focuses on protecting sensitive data in test environments and integrating security into quality engineering practices. She is passionate about helping teams build software that is not only functional, but secure by design.

I’m a data professional with over decade of experience in analytics and consulting, specializing in data, storytelling and machine learning to unlock the data value. Anastasija Removic

With over 20 years of experience leading multidisciplinary technology teams—including a pivotal role at the Commonwealth Bank of Australia, where he built and scaled a platform engineering function supporting 7,000 engineers—Andrew brings a practical, real-world perspective to DevOps and software delivery.

He is a frequent speaker at major industry events, and his thought leadership explores how AI and modern practices can transform the developer journey. Andrew holds an MBA from Southern Cross University and is passionate about empowering teams to achieve more.

Andrew is Partner Integrations Lead at Algorand Foundation and has worked on the IT side of the payments industry for the past 20 years and has been an active builder in the Algorand ecosystem for the past 4 years. He has built several public-good products such as AlgoTools, FUNC Node Manager, and Lute Wallet. He now leads partner integrations for the Foundation.

Andrey Abramov is the Founder and CTO of SereneDB, where he is building a real-time analytical search and OLAP database. With over 15 years of experience in C++, Andrey specializes in production-grade search engines and database kernel internals.
Prior to SereneDB, Andrey was the mastermind behind ArangoSearch, a search engine natively integrated into ArangoDB's distributed multi-model database core.
Earlier in his career, Andrey held senior engineering roles at EMC and Quest Software, where he managed teams and led the development of enterprise-scale systems.

Anemari Fiser is a tech leadership coach and trainer who helps engineers grow into confident, people-centered tech leads. She is the author of O’Reilly’s book ‘Leveling Up as a Tech Lead’ and the creator of the O’Reilly course ‘Soft Skills for Tech Leads’.

With over a decade of experience in the tech industry, she has held roles from software engineer to engineering leader. 

As an independent coach and trainer, Anemari has worked with 500+ engineers and trained 400+ tech leads across different organisations worldwide. 

She also shares practical leadership insights with a community of 30,000+ tech professionals on LinkedIn.

Angela Wen is a software engineer at GitHub working on the Copilot Core Platform, which, as its name suggests, builds and supports the inference platform underlying GitHub Copilot. Over the past year, she has led availability efforts across the organization: driving reliability best practices and incident response processes that keep GitHub Copilot dependable for developers. In a former life, Angela worked in international education as a classroom teacher and college counselor, and these days, she continues to find joy in teaching and mentoring people of all stripes.

Angelika Shvets, a software developer based in Israel.
I've been in the IT industry since 2004.
Over the last 7 years, my work has primarily involved software development, encompassing research, implementation, integration, and testing of systems of varying complexity.
Prior to my current role, I spent 8 years as a full-stack web application developer. 
In the early stages of my career, I gained a solid foundation with 5 years of experience as a system administrator. 
I also served as a system administrator in the military.
I hold a Masters in Mathematics and Computer Engineering.

Anja is a Principal Fullstack Engineer based in Berlin. She loves systems thinking - but doesn't love buzzwords all that much. Her aim is to understand and influence human dynamics behind software and organizations.
She's equally passionate about hard and soft skills. Over time, she's become more and more convinced that soft skills are the true hard skills.
Off stage, she's a sports enthusiast, current local champion in wakeskating, and a fan of pushing limits – whether on the water or in the workplace.

Ankit Patel is a Senior Director at NVIDIA, leading developer engagement for the company’s extensive collection of libraries, developer tools, and ecosystem software. Ankit joined NVIDIA in 2011 as a GPU Product Manager helping pioneer GPU-accelerated virtual machines, and pushed the boundaries of server GPUs, including NVIDIA's early 8-GPU server appliance. His career transitioned to software, notably leading product management for NVIDIA's OptiX library for ray tracing and AI denoising, a key development that leveraged RT Cores and deepened his connection to deep learning. Prior to NVIDIA, he held product management roles at Matrox Video and Blackmagic Design. Working on full systems for over two decades has given him perspective and expertise at the intersection of silicon and software. Ankit holds a Bachelor's in Computer Science from Concordia University and an MBA from Cornell University and serves on the PyTorch Governing Board.


Ankit Patel is a Senior Director at NVIDIA, leading developer engagement for the company’s ecosystem of libraries, compilers and developer tools. Ankit joined NVIDIA in 2011 as a GPU Product Manager helping pioneer GPU-accelerated virtual machines, and pushed the boundaries of server GPUs, including NVIDIA's early 8-GPU server appliance. His career transitioned to software, notably leading product management for NVIDIA's OptiX library for ray tracing and AI denoising, a key development that leveraged RT Cores and deepened his connection to deep learning. Prior to NVIDIA, he held product management roles at Matrox Video and Blackmagic Design. Working on full systems for over two decades has given him perspective and expertise at the intersection of silicon and software. Ankit holds a Bachelor's in Computer Science from Concordia University, an MBA from Cornell University and currently serves on the PyTorch Governing Board.

Anna Heim is a freelance reporter at TechCrunch and event moderator focusing on tech and startups, with a keen eye for innovation outside of Silicon Valley. Her byline has appeared in publications including CNet, Fortune, Resilience Media and The Next Web. A former startup founder and Sciences Po Paris alum, she is fluent in multiple languages, including French, English, Spanish and Brazilian Portuguese.

Dr. Annabella Bassler is Group CFO of Ringier, board member and Initiator of the EqualVoice initiative. She combines financial leadership with a strong commitment to advancing gender equality, driving initiatives that address gender stereotypes and unconscious bias through the EqualVoice-Assistant. With extensive experience in corporate governance and strategy, she stands for modern, responsible leadership. In her keynote, she shares insights on leading large organizations through cultural change and company-wide transformation.

Anni is an AI nerd, productivity hacker and Developer Evangelist at Twilio. Brewing rich developer experiences, blending AI with APIs, and serving the Python community across the UK and EMEA with a perfect mix of code and community. She's always hungry for the next great idea (and meal).

Anshul is a Sr. Solution Architect at NVIDIA's DGX Cloud team, he specializes in assisting customers with deploying their workloads at scale. Anshul has a strong background in SRE and has extensive experience in managing production-grade Kubernetes clusters across various Cloud Service Providers (CSPs). He has received Ph.D. in computer science from TU Munich, graduating summa cum laude.

Anton Vedeshin, Ph.D. is a serial entrepreneur, TEDx speaker, and investor. He holds a Ph.D. in cloud computing and cybersecurity and has authored scientific papers and patents across NLP, blockchain, and data protection (IEEE, ACM, Springer).

He co-founded Vespia, a KYB and business verification platform acquired by Veriff in February 2026, and previously taught Cloud Computing and Data Security at TalTech while helping design e-government and security systems for Estonia and the European Commission.

Today Anton is focused on Snoika - an AI marketing engine built for a new reality: AI is the first place people discover products. Snoika builds brand context for LLMs and makes companies show up in answers across ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Perplexity, and Google AI. Unlike generic AI writers, Snoika produces expert-structured, human-sounding content designed to be cited, trusted, and ranked - reinforced through structured signals and continuous testing.

Anton has worked with teams including NASA, Google, Microsoft, Bosch, BMW, Cisco, and 15% of Global 2000 companies. That same systems thinking now powers how Snoika engineers visibility in AI search.

Antonella is an Italian executive and multiple board member with many years of strategy consulting experience at BCG, where she became one of the youngest partners and a member of its global executive committee. Her work focus is in media, technology, and consumer goods. She was named amongst the top 25 consultants worldwide by Consulting Magazine and was honored with the Women Leaders in Consulting Lifetime Achievement Award in 2013.
 
From 2018 to 2022, she was Special Advisor to the Austrian Chancellor and headed the ThinkAustria strategy unit. She also co-chaired the Kofi Annan Award for Innovation in Africa.
 
Today, she is CEO of iSi Group, Executive Vice Chairwoman of Pochtler Industrieholding and contributes her in-depth transformation expertise as non-executive director in international supervisory boards (Generali, Publicis, Westwing, and DEAG). Previously, amongst other positions, she was Chairwoman of the Supervisory Board of Wolford and an IFC-nominated director at the Ivorian company SIPRA. 
 
She is strongly engaged in the non-profit sector, among others as a Vice-President of the European Forum Alpbach (EFA) and co-founder of the Sovereign Europe Forum, board member at TUM Venture Labs and advisory board member of the Drucker Forum. She has been committed to equal opportunities in education for many years (Teach For All, Business@School, Phorms Schools) and has published essays on leadership and strategy (“Acupuncture for Management”). 

Antonella studied in Munich and Rome and has an MBA with distinction from INSEAD. She lives with her family in Vienna.

Antonia Eneh is the co-founder of Elva, the AI-native relocation and immigration partner that makes hiring across borders effortless. They started Elva to remove the wall of relocation, so leaders could optimize for the best hire instead of the nearest one, without the work landing on leaders, HR, founders, leaving everyone free to just do their jobs. They're rebuilding the industry the way engineers would: a team made up mostly of engineers, supported by immigration and relocation experts, turning messy, manual processes into optimized, compliant workflows with the employee experience at the core.

ML engineer turned educator. Fractional CTO for AI companies. Works in developer relations at NVIDIA, researches vision-language models at HPI, and has delivered computer vision workshops to thousands across Europe.  He likes arepas.

April is a Senior Developer Advocate at GitHub, where she helps organizations modernize how they build, deploy, and operate software at scale. She specializes in application transformation and DevOps ways of working, with a strong focus on enabling enterprises to adopt modern, code-first practices using GitHub.

April is actively involved in technical content creation and education, producing enterprise-focused blogs, videos, workshops, and presentations. Through speaking engagements, executive briefings, and community events, she shares practical, real-world insights to help teams successfully adopt modern DevOps practices.

Outside of work, April enjoys spending time outdoors hiking, skiing, and scuba diving. She is also a competitive triathlete, participating in Ironman and Half Ironman events.

Platform Engineering Lead and Infrastructure Expert with over 25 years of experience designing and operating highly scalable, distributed systems. Currently serving as Head of Platform Engineering at zooplus SE, where I lead container orchestration and observability teams, running 40 k8s clusters and 3000 microservices, supporting one of Europe's largest online pet retailers.

Ariel Shulman is an accomplished executive with extensive experience in technology management, business development, marketing, and strategy. Since joining Bright Data in 2021, Ariel has leveraged his networking, security, and Internet expertise to drive innovation to access high-quality public web data solutions at scale. He now serves as VP of Product, responsible for Bright Data’s AI-integrated product suite that leads innovation.

Fluent in English, French, Spanish, and Hebrew, and with professional experience across multiple countries, Ariel plays a pivotal role in shaping Bright Data's global positioning.

Aris is a Staff Software Engineer at RudderStack with over 20 years of experience building scalable, high-performance systems across telecommunications, banking, European institutions, and data infrastructure. A core contributor to the open-source rudder-server, he specializes in Go, PostgreSQL, Kubernetes, and cloud-native architectures. Aris is passionate about designing elegant solutions, driving engineering best practices, and sharing insights on scalable data pipelines, enterprise DevOps, and software architecture.

Helping Devs build cool stuff, learn faster, and laugh along the way — one bug at a time. Senior Developer Advocate at AWS, speaker, and agentic AI enthusiast turning complex cloud patterns into fun, hands-on demos

I am a senior software engineer and consultant with 9+ years of experience working in banking, telecom, government, and enterprise domains.

A systems thinker and pragmatic generalist. Backend-first, infrastructure-aware and privacy-minded. Currently exploring applied AI direction.

Experienced mentor, speaker, and consultant, organizer of "summer academies" and different internal knowledge-sharing sessions with a strong focus on practical learning and software engineering reality.

Ash Jones is the Founder of Great Influence, where they act as a Strategic Partner to global brands helping them activate their senior leadership teams on social media to support external comms, employer brand and company culture. 

Ash holds a decade of experience in helping CEOs and Entrepreneurs build their personal brands including Steven Bartlett of Diary Of A CEO fame, ex-Manchester United footballer Gary Neville, best-selling authors Matthew Syed and John Amaechi and senior leaders at the likes of Booking.com and Santander.
Asrar Khan

Aurélie Vache is a Developer Advocate at OVHcloud in Toulouse, France. She is Docker Captain, CNCF ambassador, Google Cloud Developer Expert, Women techmarkers Ambassador & GitPod Hero. She has been working as a Developer and Ops for over 18 years. Cloud enthusiast and advocates DevOps/Cloud/Golang best practices.

Conferences and meetups organizer since 2016. Technical writer (dev.to/aurelievache), a book author & reviewer, a sketchnoter and a speaker at international conferences.

Mentor and promote diversity and accessibility in technology.

She created a new visual way for people to learn and understand Cloud technologies: "Understanding Kubernetes/Istio/Docker in a visual way" in sketchnotes, books and videos.
 
Blog: https://dev.to/aurelievache/
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/c/AurelieVache

Atlassian Chief People and AI Enablement Officer, Avani Prabhakar, leads Atlassian's People and Transformations organisation with a sharp focus on unlocking potential in the AI era – blending purpose, systems thinking, and modern leadership to shape the future of work.

Avani’s focus is on enabling the cultural transformation required alongside the technology shift – to build AI-fluent teams and unlock opportunity. She has been instrumental in pioneering the distributed work models and ways of working that underpin Atlassian’s Team Anywhere approach, and is actively embedding AI up-skilling, scaling, and innovation across the organisation.

With 20+ years across tech, aviation, engineering, professional services and consulting, Avani keeps Atlassian's people-first approach practical and grounded, to build human + AI collaboration and capability in complex, high-growth environments.
speakers.company_position: Chief People  and AI Enablement Officer

speakers.bio:
Mag. Axel Ebert is founding partner of wortwelt® and co-creator of identifire®. With experience across more than 300 wording projects for financial services, industry, telecoms, NGOs and public administration, he has established himself as the "Letter Man" – and, since adopting AI, also as 'Chief Prompting Officer'. The only C-level title you actually have to earn every single day. Motto: from artificial to creative intelligence.
wortwelt.at – we craft language. AI pitches in. Jargon-free.

Axel Schell is driving the AI adoption at Allianz Technology and transforming the company into an innovative and customer centric tech company. He joined the Executive Management Team of Allianz Technology, the IT company of the Allianz group, as the Chief Technology and Transformation Officer in January 2021.

His mission and passion to modernize and transform the IT landscape as well as the ways of working was rewarded with the “CIO of the Year 2021” award by the German speaking CIO Magazine and the “European Digital Leader of the Year 2023” award by CIONET.

He started this transformation journey already in his previous roles: Since his start at Allianz in 2002 he has demonstrated not just a specialist knowledge on IT management but also a distinct feeling for the needs of the business and future Tech trends. 

He is a techie with knowledge for economy and vice versa: Axel completed his studies in Business Administration and obtained a doctoral degree in Information Systems from the University of Augsburg.

He is fluent in German, English, and French. Axel is married and has two children. In his free time, he enjoys spending time with his family on a tennis court, coding and watching American basketball.

Aydin Mir Mohammadi, partner and architect at bluehands, has been creating software for more than twenty years. He specializes in the development of distributed complex business applications based on Microsoft technologies. His focus is on cloud migration, AI integration, software modernization, and twin transformation.

Aydin is co-initiator of the .NET User Group Karlsruhe, organizer of the Green Software Development Karlsruhe, Azure MVP, initiator of the CO2-Challenge and host of the #nossued Open Space. He is also involved in the training of young software developers as a lecturer at the Karlsruhe Cooperative State University.

Barbara Kryslak is a talent leader with extensive experience in both strategic recruiting and the operational deployment of HR technologies. She has led cross-functional teams through AI adoption initiatives, driving measurable improvements in hiring quality and operational efficiency across diverse talent markets.

I help tech companies built at scale and expand their operations internationally. 
Seasoned Talent Acquisition and people leader, experienced working with fast growing early stage, late stage, IPO-bound and PE-backed tech organizations.

With over a decade of experience in B2B leadership, Bastian brings a wealth of insights from both the traditional corporate world and the dynamic startup scene. As VP Product/Marketing at WorkMotion, he is at the forefront of shaping the "Future of Work."

Bastian's passion for remote work has grown during the pandemic, leading him to develop expertise in leading decentralized, global, and intercultural teams. His experience and insights make him a valuable speaker on the ever-evolving landscape of work.

Studied mathematics and computer science. Software developer roles across multiple industries. Moved on to the Software Architect role and leading role. Since 09/2022 Lead Cloud Native Solution Architect at Zeiss.

Ben De St Paer-Gotch is the Director of Product at GitHub, where he oversees the strategic vision and development for core developer tools including GitHub Actions, Packages, Codespaces, Microsoft Dev Tunnels, and npm. Driven by a mission to improve developers' lives, Ben leads his teams in building seamless solutions that automate day-to-day workflows. In addition to his leadership role, Ben is a published author and an avid technologist who thrives on collaborating with passionate, forward-thinking people. Outside of the tech world, he enjoys spending time with his family and immersing himself in his love for all things Warhammer and Warhammer 40k.

Ben has a strong background in software security having worn many hats from ethical hacker, penetration tester and security consultant to application security strategist focused on secure software development programs and maturity assessments. He is co-author of multiple BSIMM annual reports and the P-SSCRM framework, with a decade-plus advising security teams on SSDLC, SSDF, and security champions programs.

Benedikt Höck is Head of Artificial Intelligence (AI) for KPMG in Germany and the EMA region. In this role, he is responsible for the AI go-to-market strategy, the development of service offerings, and the firm’s internal transformation through AI. He supports clients end-to-end on their AI journey - from strategy design and the implementation of high-impact use cases to organizational enablement and ensuring the secure, trusted deployment of AI.

Ben is an AI-skeptic software developer in Berlin who believes in a healthy balance between over-engineered creativity and creative over-engineering. He was an early hire at Stack Overflow and spent over 12 years there, in addition to working at other startups like Alloy, Wonder, and most recently Stacks Labs.

Benjamin was appointed Chief Technology Officer (CTO) at Delivery Hero in June 2024. He leads the engineering teams driving the technological expertise, operations, and processes supporting Delivery Hero’s platform applications across all its markets. Prior to joining Delivery Hero in January 2023 as Senior Vice President of Engineering, Benjamin was CTO at foodpanda, one of Asia’s leading food delivery apps. With a career spanning 25 years in the technology industry, Benjamin has been and continues to be at the forefront of innovation across the globe.

A Developer Relations leader with 15+ years of experience driving global adoption of cloud and fintech platforms. With roles at AWS, Stripe, and Broadcom, I’ve built scalable developer engagement engines, shaped compelling product narratives, and launched hands-on programs that turn complex technology into real business impact. Known for high-energy keynotes, strategic program design, and cross-functional leadership, I bridge developers, product teams, and executives to accelerate innovation and deliver results.

Bernardo is a software engineer working on the MUI X Charts team to build the next-generation of React charts. He likes performance work and data visualization in general (charts, maps, etc.) and is very excited to have the opportunity to improve the ecosystem of charting in the web.

Bernd Datler is managing director of the tolling company of ASFINAG since November 2009 and CIO/CDO of the ASFINAG group. Mr. Datler is responsible for technical development and operation of the tolling systems of ASFINAG covering all highways and expressways of Austria as well as for various ITS activities within ASFINAG. In addition to that, Mr. Datler is responsible for all IT- and digitalisation related activities of ASFINAG.

Bianca Errigo is a wellness expert, global speaker, and founder of HumanOS, a pioneering company integrating health and performance into the workplace. With a background in psychology and over 12,000 coaching hours, she blends science with real-world insight. Once hospitalised from corporate burnout, Bianca now partners with companies such as American Express, Cisco, Snapchat and The University of Cambridge to support individuals and organisations on their journeys of sustainable high performance.

Bill Boorman has spent his working life in around recruiting, starting out at the age of 16 in what he saw at the time as a short term gig as a trainee recruiter. He has worked as a third party recruiter, run and owned recruiting firms, been HRD and Trainer for international business, helped some of the worlds largest brands evolve the way people get hired through the age of digital and social media. Boorman advises tech companies and investors on the technology needed for the modern day. He is on the board of tech brands Vonq, PlacedApp and StaffCircle.

Boorman has won numerous awards including Recruiter Life Time Achievement, and was recently listed in HR Techs Top 100 influencers. Away from work he is a Dad, Husband, Street Artist and Stand Up Comedian. He is an in demand speaker known for sharing realities to challenge the people people.
