Björn Buchhold is a Technology Evangelist at CID, specializing in AI and Data Science. With a PhD in Computer Science and over 15 years of experience in academia and industry, he helps organizations navigate the full journey from AI strategy and data architecture to hands-on ML implementation.

Björn Stahl is a Software Architect at AVS which is a competence center at Bechtle. He is advising customers on architecture decisions, the tradeoffs and how to leverage the advantages of cloud concepts or gain certain degrees of digital souvereignty. Besides the day to day work he is interested in architectural concepts, high-traffic-low-latency applications and performance optimizations in the Java world. Besides the technical topics he likes to learn and discuss all topics around leadership and how to lead people.

Boris Hecker is the Managing Director of Atos Germany and in this role, he directs the
strategy and operational execution of the business activities of Atos in Germany. He also
heads the Public Sector and Defense industry in Germany in addition to his overall
responsibility for business growth in Germany, Austria, and Central Europe (Poland and
Czech Republic).

Boy Baukema is a Senior Principal Application Security Consultant at Veracode with over twenty years of experience in the software security industry. Specializing in Software Composition Analysis (SCA), static analysis, and secure CI/CD pipeline integrations, Boy helps organizations build robust defenses against modern threats. He brings deep practical knowledge of vulnerability remediation and supply chain security to his workshops, empowering developers to effectively balance speed and security in their daily workflows.

AI is making software cheaper to produce, but not necessarily better. We need to reframe our roles as designers and engineers as quality stewardship: making intent legible, running fast verification loops, and building workflows that compound learning over time. This talk offers durable principles and concrete habits for using AI to explore more, ship faster, and still deliver software that feels coherent, resilient, and well-crafted.

Brian is a Principal Systems Engineer in Intercom/Fin's Dublin office. He fixes problems, builds things and grows people. He loves helping teams, and what they build, to be both resilient and delightful, increasingly using AI to do so.

Brian is the Senior Director of Integrations and Developer Tools at Algorand Foundation, where he helps devs learn, integrate, and contribute to Algorand's open source blockchain technology to solve real world problems. Before joining the Foundation, Brian led global strategic transformation programs at Morgan Stanley, where he led technology change programs for commodities, exchange-traded and OTC derivatives, Prime Brokerage, and digital assets businesses over 15 years. When not on his laptop, he's probably cooking slow food that makes the house smell good.

AI Platform Architect, bridging DevOps & MLOps to build scalable AI platforms. Passionate about open source, platform engineering, and driving AI adoption. Focused on delivering real customer impact by aligning architectures with business outcomes.

Carey Liu is a Product Security professional on TikTok's Global Security team, focusing on product security, AI-SDLC operations, and AI-native security governance. As part of the team, he is currently contributing to SEVA (Spec-driven Engineering Validation Agent), a product designed to bring security validation into product and technical design reviews earlier in the SDLC.

With 10 years of experience across security product operations, technical program management, and DevSecOps solution pre-sales, Carey is passionate about making security more scalable, actionable, and developer-friendly. His work focuses on shift-left security and emerging spec-driven AI coding workflow, helping engineering teams build securely by design before code is written.

Carey holds a Computer Science degree from the University of British Columbia and a Master’s degree in Information Security from UC Berkeley.

Proud dad and product engineer at the German Airforce. I have a passion for progressive web apps and local-first. I organize and speak at the Munich TypeScript Meetup and love sharing my passion and excitement about my craft. When not at the desk, coding or with my family, you'll find me working out, playing badminton or cooking pasta in the kitchen.

Carl is a cloud architect who has worked across IoT, smart homes, smart locks, distributed systems, and global cloud governance at IKEA, ASSA ABLOY, and Axis. He has used coding agents since they became useful enough to change how software gets built, and now helps Oplane’s customers apply agentic threat modeling to real systems and real security problems.

Cédric Gégout, Canonical's VP of Product, is a strong believer in open source as both the foundation of major platform shifts and the primary source of innovation. Before Canonical, he founded Streamezzo, later led cloud-native strategy at Amdocs, and holds a PhD in AI. He has spent his career adopting new technologies early, from AI and mobile platforms to cloud-native systems, Kubernetes, secure containers, and edge computing with the same passion for helping developers turn open innovation into enterprise-ready solutions.

Celeste is a Sr. OSS Developer Advocate at Snowflake, focusing on open source data communities. She is a previous member of Kubernetes SIG Docs, founded the Inclusive Naming Committee at the Linux Foundation, and is a regular speaker on open source data topics for the past 3 years. Her work on inclusive language in tech has been featured in the New York Times. She currently resides in London, UK.

Chadha is a Developer Relations Engineer at Qdrant focused on vector search, retrieval systems, and AI agents. Her academic background includes a dual engineering degree specializing in cybersecurity and mathematical and computational engineering, along with a master’s degree in computer vision. She is particularly interested in building scalable and reliable AI systems powered by semantic retrieval and generative models.

Chris Daden is the Chief Technology Officer at Criteria, a global Talent Success platform operating at the
center of the Future of Work. Criteria helps organizations measure real talent signals through more than
80 million assessments delivered worldwide in over 25 languages, enabling fairer, more predictive hiring
and development decisions. Backed by over $65 million in private growth equity investment, Criteria
works with 4,000+ leading global organizations to move beyond static credentials toward dynamic,
skills-based insights that reflect true capability and potential.
 
Chris is a multi-time founder and technology executive with deep expertise in artificial intelligence,
including applied AI systems, model fine-tuning, and frontier model development. His work focuses on
how AI is reshaping work, education, and human capital strategy at scale. He regularly advises leaders
on the intersection of technology, workforce transformation, and organizational design, and frequently
speaks globally as a keynote presenter, podcast guest, and industry contributor.
He is a contributor to the Forbes Technology Council and Forbes Young Executives Council, and serves
as the founding technology advisor for The Hungry Lab, a global initiative working alongside the United
Nations to advise and support startups worldwide. Chris also leads SoCal Tech Forum, one of Southern
California’s largest technology non-profits, dedicated to educating companies and communities on
emerging and disruptive technologies.
 
Chris brings a data-driven perspective on Work 4.0, the future of education, and how organizations can
design talent systems that prioritize adaptability, equity, and long-term human potential in an AI-driven
world.

Chris is a trained Process Communication Model (PCM) practitioner and will be certified as a PCM coach by June 2026. With over 15 years of experience in talent acquisition, he has helped scale teams across startups and high-growth environments. Today, he works as a fractional Head of Talent Acquisition and leadership mentor, supporting recruiters, founders and managers in building high-performance teams.

His journey into leadership began the way it does for many, promoted for strong individual performance, but without formal training on how to actually lead people. That experience led him to PCM, where he discovered a practical, psychology-based framework for understanding how people communicate, what motivates them, and how to spot early signs of stress.

Now, he brings those insights to fast-paced teams, helping leaders build not just headcount, but trust, motivation, and sustainable performance.

Former Yahoo, Mozilla, Microsoft. JavaScript and Accessibility expert. Worked on Chromium Developer Tools. Author of the Developer Advocacy Handbook, LinkedIn Learning and Skillshare Trainer. Cinephile.

Christian Liebel is a developer with a passion for modern web technologies. He has been recognized as a Microsoft Most Valuable Professional (MVP) and a Google Developer Expert (GDE) and speaks at user groups and conferences worldwide. As a member of the W3C Technical Architecture Group (TAG), he helps push the boundaries of the web.

Christian Trummer is the founder and Chief Scientist of Bitpanda. Previously serving as CTO for nearly a decade, he scaled the engineering team to over 400 people, building the company's core trading platform and in-house banking system. He now leads a specialized team focused on embedding AI across the organization to enhance products and efficiency. Christian combines deep technical expertise with a vision for AI-driven transformation in fintech.

Christian Voigt, Global Employment Specialist at Remote.com, has been working in tech for over five years. He knows from personal experience what it’s like when relocation and on-site work are mandatory, having had to change employers because of it. That’s why he believes opportunities shouldn’t depend on where you live or work — for candidates or companies alike.
With this perspective, Christian advises companies in Switzerland, Germany and Austria on how to achieve growth goals, retain employees affected by relocation, and make international expansion and hiring abroad simple and innovative.⁠

Actually, I'm an Egyptologist and should (probably) be looking for mummies. However, I've preferred to use the last 16 years to find people who are still alive.

I started out as a sourcer in a recruitment consultancy, worked for a long time in tech start-ups and scale-ups both as a hands-on recruiter as well as in leadership roles and founded my own company, The UnleashPeople, together with a business partner at the beginning of 2024.

My focus is on data-driven recruiting processes and strategies, employer branding and a bit of workforce planning. For me, all of this is always under the banner of an excellent candidate experience.

Christian Wenz is a consultant, software architect and entrepreneur specializing in web technologies and security. A prolific writer, he has authored or co-authored over 100 books for various publishers. Christian’s work spans both open-source and proprietary web technologies, a unique combination that has earned him recognition as a Microsoft MVP for Developer Technologies and the distinction of being the lead author of the Zend PHP certification. He is also credited in several open-source projects for his contributions. In addition to his writing and technical endeavors, Christian is a frequent speaker at developer conferences worldwide, where he shares his expertise in web technologies.

Christin Pohl is a Global Black Belt Solution Engineer for AI Infrastructure at Microsoft (Switzerland), now in her third year. After building her first chatbot in 2018 and 5+ years at SAP, she helps enterprises worldwide choose the right GPU, run LLM training and inference end-to-end (LLMOps), and optimize performance (latency, throughput, and cost per token). She studied Information Technology and Computer Science in Berlin, Toronto, and at Harvard.

Christoph is a passionate Software Crafter with a strong focus on software architecture and continuous learning.

In his role as Lead Software Engineer for Transformation at SQUER, he helps customers modernize their legacy applications, improve their software architecture, and reshape their software engineering culture.

He is convinced that principles and practices from the Domain-Driven Design universe are most helpful for tackling the complex challenges of large transformation initiatives.

Dr. Christoph Lohrmann is Senior Software Developer at Q.ANT, a deep-tech scale-up developing photonic processors that compute with light to enable energy-efficient AI and high-performance computing. At Q.ANT, he contributes to the development and application of next-generation computing architectures based on photonics and advanced algorithms.
Previously, he conducted research in computational physics and complex systems, focusing on the behavior of self-propelled agents, bio-inspired motion strategies, and reinforcement learning approaches for micro-robotics. 
Christoph Lohrmann studied physics at the University of Stuttgart, where he developed a strong interdisciplinary background bridging physics, machine learning, and emerging computing technologies.

Christoph Menzel is Head of Security Excellence at inovex. With more than 15 years of experience in software development, he combines practical developer expertise with security know-how from technical, strategic, and business perspectives.
His clear goal is to help organizations understand security not as a burden, but as an integral part of software development.
As a speaker, trainer, coach, and author, Christoph regularly shares his practical, field-tested knowledge. His hands-on mentality and focus on sustainable solutions make him a valuable partner for companies looking to take their software security to the next level.

Claudia Tattanelli is the Founder and CEO of C-YourCulture and the Chairwoman of the global think tank Future Talent Council. Formerly, Claudia was the Chairwoman of the Universum Strategic Board, and for over 25 years she has led many culture and employer branding strategy projects, supporting the leadership teams of many of the world's leading companies, including Accenture, Alexander McQueen, Apple, AB InBev, Audi, Barclays, Barilla, Campari, Citibank, Coca-Cola, Coty, Exxon, EY, Facebook, FedEx, H&M, Goldman Sachs, Google, IBM, JPMorgan, Luxottica, Lamborghini, L’Oréal, Mondelēz, Lenovo, P&G, SAP, Siemens, the United Nations, Unilever, YSL, and Zalando. Claudia is a regular keynote speaker at conferences and events worldwide and has been quoted in many leading newspapers, journals, and podcasts, including Businessweek, CNN, The New York Times, Financial Times, Forbes, Fortune, The Washington Post, Il Sole 24 Ore, Corriere della Sera, and The Economist. Claudia has also been a guest on Bloomberg, Fox, and CNBC’s Morning Call, as well as many other media outlets.

Clemens Vasters is Lead Architect in Microsoft’s Azure Messaging team that builds and operates a fleet of hyper-scale messaging services, including Event Grid, Service Bus, Event Hubs, Stream Analytics and Microsoft Fabric Eventstreams. Clemens represents Microsoft in messaging standardisation in OASIS (AMQP, MQTT) and CNCF (CloudEvents, xRegistry) and writes too much code for being an "Architect". He looks back at nearly 30 years in professional software development and has seen the same fashion come and go a few times.

Clemens Wasner is CEO of enliteAI, an Austrian venture studio that achieved its first AI exit just 18 months after investment. He is also founder and chairperson of AI Austria, an independent think tank driving Austria’s competitiveness in artificial intelligence. Since 2017 he has published the Austrian AI Landscape, the reference overview of the country’s AI ecosystem. Clemens hosts the weekly “AI Talk” podcast and is an active member of the European AI Forum, a coalition of national AI associations that advises on and shapes EU regulation.

I lead Developer Relations at UiPath, helping developers run business orchestrations that bring together agents, bots, and humans at enterprise-scale. Previously led DevRel at Auth0 and spent 16 years at Microsoft across .NET, Windows, and Azure.
Cole Groom

Colleen Lavin is a Developer Advocate at GitLab, with a background in embedded systems, software development and product management. She’s worked with NASA and was featured on Gimlet Media’s Startup podcast. When she’s not playing with robots or coding you can usually find her lost in the woods or on twitter  (@colleenjokes).

Corinna lives in Hannover and works in renewable energies, always trying to connect coding with sustainability ideas. She ues C# since the year 2002 and has recently jumped into TypeScript for an LCIA project.

Cristina Musceleanu is a software developer in large-scale enterprise systems, with a strong focus on software architecture, modular design, and sustainable modernization. She is currently involved in evolving a legacy alert and notification platform from a centralized scheduler-based model to a more modular, event-driven architecture, applying principles from Clean Architecture and Onion Architecture to improve resilience, maintainability, and team ownership.
Cristina is passionate about turning complex systems into clear, understandable designs and believes that great architecture is built through small, thoughtful steps rather than big rewrites. She enjoys sharing real-world lessons from ongoing projects and helping teams modernize responsibly in regulated and high-impact environments.

Cyrille is a customer engineer in EMEA and he specializes in cloud databases like Firestore and their crucial role in building high-impact Generative AI applications. He architects database solutions that provide the scalability, performance, and availability necessary for successful GenAI deployments.

Dagmar de Haan is a freelance software architect and software developer. Her focus is on the design and development of backend systems in the Java environment. Since her beginnings in software development a good 20 years ago, she has been concerned with the question of what makes good software, how it is created and, above all, how it is maintained. She is not only interested in the technical aspects, but also the domain-specific and methodological ones.

Damandeep Kochhar is EVP, Chief Platform & Technology Officer at HERE Technologies. In his role, he leads the company’s core engineering activities, including the development and operation of our highly automated mapping system for creating, updating and publishing map content.

Damiana Casile is the Director of People Business Partners for Europe at Dow Jones. Based in Barcelona, she drives the people strategy and organizational design behind fast-evolving, tech-driven teams. Damiana specializes in scaling inclusive, high-performing cultures and leading change management to turn business transformation into high performance.

Dana Lawson is the Chief Technology Officer at Netlify, where she leads the Engineering, Product, and Design teams to innovate and deliver transformative digital experiences. With over two decades of leadership at industry-leading companies like GitHub, Heptio, and New Relic, Dana has a proven track record of scaling teams, fostering collaboration, and navigating complex technical landscapes.

Throughout her career, she’s worn many hats across the product lifecycle, showcasing her versatility and expertise building impactful technology solutions. Driven by a genuine passion for people, Dana infuses her leadership with a blend of technical expertise and a spirit of fun, creating an inspiring and dynamic work environment.

Dani began his tech journey at a startup 10 years ago as a mobile developer. Over the years, he worked at several consulting companies, gaining some experience across multiple stacks. Eventually, he specialized in frontend development and is now focused on advocating for the field by writing technical content, conducting workshops and giving talks.

Daniel currently works as a Senior Platform Advocate for NETWAYS Web Services, a public cloud provider and MSP from Germany.

In the past, he has helped clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies to SMBs with adopting cloud native solutions like Kubernetes, GitOps, and Observability as a consultant and systems engineer.

His work and personal interests have led him to become a Grafana Champion as well as a CNCF Kubestronaut, and he enjoys sharing his experiences at meetups, conferences, and through blog posts on his website.

Daniel Branski is a Lead Engineer at Finanz Informatik, the central IT service provider of the German Savings Banks Finance Group, responsible for their Data & Intelligence Platform. He led the architectural transformation of their central data warehouse from mainframe-based infrastructure to a modern, decentralized architecture.  
His expertise and enthusiasm range from enterprise-wide system design all the way down to query-level optimization.

Daniel started his IT career as an administrator for ETL and BI tools. He then worked some time in a data warehousing team before diving into all topics devops and cloud native. In his current role he is a DevOps and cloud-native consultant with FullStackS GmbH. Daniel holds a BSc in Industrial Engineering, a MA in Innovation Management and an MBA. He's a Kubestronaut, CNCF Ambassador, eager learner and teacher, lecturing at two Austrian universities of applied science.
Daniel currently lives in Graz with his girlfriend and his tomcat Manfred 🐈.

🌐 drackthor.me
📧 daniel@drackthor.me

Daniel Evenschor is ML Ops Engineer at Finanz Informatik, specializing in deploying, scaling, and monitoring LLMs and ML models in the banking sector. With a background as a Data Scientist at Evicon, he bridges data science and production systems to drive real-world impact. His expertise spans MLOps, CI/CD, Kubernetes, OpenShift, and advanced model observability using tools like Prometheus, Dynatrace, and OpenTelemetry.

Daniel Gebler is CTO of Picnic, the world’s fastest growing online supermarket that makes grocery shopping simple, fun, and affordable for everyone. 

Previously, he was Director R&D of Fredhopper, responsible for the product and technology roadmap, and led engineering teams located in Amsterdam and Sofia. 

Daniel holds a PhD in Computer Science and an MBA. He published papers, edited journals, and chaired international workshops on software correctness.

Daniel Geisel is CEO of Mercedes‑Benz Tech Innovation and Head of Home of Tech. In this role, he is responsible for the global software organization within Mercedes‑Benz IT, driving technological development and innovation in close collaboration with the business functions.

Under his leadership, the organization is evolving from individual tech hubs into a globally integrated software tech organization, combining software engineering excellence, digital solutions and strong execution capabilities. He works closely with international teams across locations including Spain, South Africa, India and Malaysia.

Dr Daniel Meilak is a Senior Software Engineer with a background in Computational Physics. After 9 years of grueling studies, he found that life became a lot more exciting when he was able to write bug ridden lines of C++ at EGYM

Dr. Daniel Mühlbauer is an expert in HR-IT solutions and focuses on the meaningful use of modern technologies in personnel management within a global corporation. He shares his knowledge on his blog and YouTube channel called “HR Datenliebe" and as co-host of the HRDataDudes-Podcast. His passion lies in the combination of data analytics, artificial intelligence, and HR tech to pave the way for the future of personnel management. After completing his PhD at the Institute for Human Resource Management at LMU Munich, he co-founded a startup specializing in People Analytics.

Daniel Oh is a Java Champion and Senior Principal Developer Advocate at IBM, where he leads efforts to advance cloud native innovation through open source technologies. Renowned for his ability to bridge technical and collaborative gaps, he empowers developers and organizations to build transformative solutions from AI-driven applications and serverless architectures to resilient microservices. As a CNCF Ambassador and TAG DevEx co-chair, Daniel actively shapes the cloud native ecosystem, fostering partnerships between enterprise developers, AI engineers, and platform engineering teams to accelerate hybrid cloud adoption. His technical leadership extends beyond code contributions, as he mentors communities and champions strategies that enable businesses to thrive in evolving digital landscapes. A dynamic storyteller and educator, Daniel captivates global audiences through keynote speeches, workshops, and interactive sessions, where he demystifies emerging technologies and inspires the tech community to embrace the future of open source innovation.

A seasoned Full Cycle Web Development Expert and R&D Manager, brings over two decades of experience, skillfully leading both local and international development teams from concept to delivery. As a recognized public speaker and blog writer, he also makes notable contributions to the open-source community.

In addition, Daniel leads the "NG-Heroes" meetup, creating networking and knowledge-sharing opportunities within the tech industry. Beyond his professional endeavors, he values family time above all else, proudly owning the title of "The Best Developer in the World," humorously awarded by his wife. Combining technical leadership, community involvement, and a strong family focus.

Daniel Schley is a senior engineering leader with over 20 years of experience building and scaling software, data, and platform teams in complex, fast-growing organizations. He has led teams responsible for production systems through periods of low maturity, high autonomy, and structural ambiguity, helping turn recurring incidents and operational chaos into clearer decision-making, stronger technical foundations, and more effective engineering practices. 

His work sits at the intersection of decision-making under pressure, incident-driven learning, and modern platform engineering. With a growing focus on how AI-assisted development amplifies existing strengths and weaknesses in engineering organizations, Daniel speaks from hard-earned experience, sharing concrete failure modes, real incident patterns, and pragmatic approaches teams can apply immediately to improve reliability and learning at scale.

Daniel is the Chief Architect for Developer and Cloud Infrastructure Platforms at Mercedes-Benz Group AG.
In recent years, he has worked and led platform and infrastructure topics and enabled product teams to have an easily accessible runtime
environment.
Besides that, he invests a lot in cultural topics, like engineering development programs, standardised engineering hiring processes
and on community building efforts.
Additionally, he has multiple years of experience designing and developing backend and frontend applications across different technology stacks.
Before joining the corporate world, he co-founded two startups and worked mostly in the financial services sector as a software
engineer.

Daniel started his career as PhD student in the  area of cloud computing with a focus on distributed databases in the cloud. Further interests cover cloud orchestration, model-driven engineering, and performance evaluations of distributed systems. After completing his PhD, Daniel has co-founded the Benchmarking-as-a-Service platform benchANT where he is responsible for the product development and large-scale benchmark projects.

Daniel is a member of Google Cloud's Application Modernization practice in EMEA. He focuses on bringing best practices in the areas of developer productivity and the effective use of cloud native development practices to enterprise customers. He works closely with the Google Cloud product teams to scale new technologies and share best practices in the form of webinars, blog posts and open source assets.

Daniel Terzenbach has been a Member of the Executive Board of the Federal Employment Agency since 2019, where he is responsible for operational business areas. 

From October 2023 to July 2024, he also served as the Federal Government’s Special Representative for the labor market integration of refugees.

At the beginning of his career with the Federal Employment Agency, he held a leadership position at the Jobcenter Märkischer Kreis. In 2009, he moved to the agency’s headquarters in Nuremberg, where he took on various leadership roles and, from 2015 to 2018, was responsible for the labor market integration of refugees.

Daniël has been working on TiDB and related projects. Before that he worked on scaling MySQL for a large company that sells hotel rooms. One of the side projects he has been working in is the MySQL protocol dissector in Wireshark.

Daniele Tonella is the chief technology officer at ING. A lifelong technologist, he describes himself as “a mechanical engineer by mistake” with an enduring passion for tech. He started coding when he was 10 years old, and at 18, “with the self‑confidence of that age”, he believed he already understood technology well enough to study something different, so he pursued mechanical engineering. Despite that, he never stopped tinkering and coding on the side, eventually moving fully into the technology world.

Daniele has over 20 years of experience in senior technology and leadership roles in the financial industry and currently serves in advisory and board positions for several companies. Before joining ING, he worked at UniCredit, including as Group Chief Information Officer and CEO of UniCredit Services. Prior to that he held roles at AXA Group, Evalueserve, and Swiss Life. He began his career as a consultant at Mercer and later McKinsey & Company in Switzerland.

He holds a master’s degree in industrial engineering from ETH Zürich and completed executive education programmes at Harvard, IMD, INSEAD, and MIT Sloan.

Hybrid AI and enterprise technology leader with 10+ years designing, implementing, and scaling production-grade AI platforms in complex enterprise environments. Bridges executive vision with technical delivery. From strategy and governance down to architecture, infrastructure, and code.

As Enterprise Architect at Rödl & Partner (~7,000 employees), architected a multi-tenant generative AI platform serving 6,000+ users with EU AI Act-aligned access controls. Led enterprise data strategy and Azure Integration Services rollout at enterprise scale. Previous experience includes ITSM transformation for German public sector (VBG) and AWS data architecture for Deutsche Bahn subsidiary.

David Soria Parra is a Member of Technical Staff at Anthropic based in London. He co-created the Model Context Protocol (MCP). He leads its technical direction, governance, and ecosystem development. Before joining Anthropic in 2024, David spent nearly a decade at Meta leading engineering organizations, and served as an Entrepreneur in Residence at Sutter Hill Ventures. He is a long-time open-source contributor with a track record spanning PHP, Git, and Mercurial.
speakers.company_position: Co-creator of MCP, Member of Technical Staff

speakers.bio: David Tielke is a consultant, coach, and trainer with over 15 years of experience in high-level software development. His expertise focuses on software quality, software architecture, and agile development processes. Having trained more than 30,000 participants, delivered over 150 presentations at renowned conferences, and published numerous articles in industry magazines, he has shared his knowledge and expertise widely. On his YouTube channel, he shares his passion for software development and provides practical insights into current industry topics.

David is a Senior AI/ML Engineer within the Office of the CTO at NetApp, where he’s dedicated to empowering developers to build, scale, and deploy AI/ML solutions in production environments. He brings deep expertise in building and training models for applications such as NLP, vision, real-time analytics, and even classifying debilitating diseases. His mission is to help users build, train, and deploy AI models efficiently, making advanced machine learning accessible to users of all levels.

Before NetApp, he was heavily involved in the AI/ML community, specifically in conversational AI solutions and driving AI platform growth in a DevRel and pre-sales role. David frequently shares his insights at industry conferences and events, offering hands-on guidance for implementing AI/ML in cloud environments. David's prior experience includes contributing to the Kubernetes and CNCF ecosystems, working hands-on with VMware virtualization, implementing backup/recovery solutions, and developing hardware storage adapter firmware and drivers.

Dei is the CEO of HASH, the open-source platform for data integration and simulation. HASH develops ultra high-accuracy AI solutions for firms in safety-critical, error-sensitive, and regulated industries.

HASH has been developing "agentic" simulation tooling since 2019, when Dei established the firm with Joel Spolsky (the former founder of Stack Overflow, Trello, and Glitch). Today, HASH is profitable and has offices in New York, London, and Berlin.

Dei also leads one of the technical areas of the UK Advanced Research + Invention Agency's "Safeguarded AI" program, focused on developing high-trust interfaces for effective human-AI collaboration.

Dennis is a Microsoft MVP and Principal Consultant at Dutch Microsoft consultancy firm Aviva Solutions. With 29 years of experience under his belt as a software architect and/or lead developer, he specializes in designing full-stack enterprise solutions based on .NET as well as providing coaching on all aspects of designing, building, documenting, deploying and maintaining software systems in an agile world. He is the author of several open-source projects, including Fluent Assertions, Pathy, Reflectify, PackageGuard, and several .NET Solution Templates, and has been maintaining coding guidelines for C# since 2001. You can find him on Twitter, Mastodon and BlueSky.

Dennis Schulz is a Senior Consultant at TNG Technology Consulting. He holds a PhD in low temperature physics from the University of Heidelberg. Besides being a programmer, he organized and hosted the TV show Quasi Klar for RNF, published a book that was translated to Korean and Russian, and won Science Slam competitions all over Germany. As a part of the Innovation Hacking team at TNG, he worked on different AI showcases, fine-tuning embeddings, and data mining.

Des Traynor is a Co-founder and the Chief Strategy Officer of Intercom and sits on Intercom’s board of directors. Des has led many teams within the company, including Product, Marketing, Customer Support, and Content, and has been directly responsible for building out even more functions within Intercom as well. Today, Des oversees Intercom's R&D team, based in the Dublin and London offices.

Before being part of Intercom’s founding team, he was a UX consultant, a university lecturer in computer science, and also a Ph.D. researcher studying how to improve the quality of computer science education. Des also previously co-founded Exceptional.

Des is an author and speaker on the topics of starting a startup, scaling a business, creating a product strategy and more. He shares his ideas and expertise in books, podcasts, and conference presentations. He also invests in many startups, some of which include Stripe, Miro, Notion, and Hopin.

Diana is a global Talent Intelligence and Talent Acquisition leader currently working at Stellantis, where she focuses on skill-based hiring, workforce analytics, and competitive talent intelligence. She has built and scaled talent strategies in complex, high-growth environments, with prior experience at Meta, Nubank, and HelloFresh. Her work sits at the intersection of HR, data, and leadership decision-making, helping organizations anticipate capability risk and turn market signals into strategic workforce action.

Dmitry specializes in Embedded Systems Software Development and Applied Artificial Intelligence on the Edge. In addition to his position at Edge Impulse, a Qualcomm company, he runs a YouTube channel focused on Edge AI, robotics, and machine learning, which has grown to over 15,000 subscribers. Through the channel, he collaborates with various hardware manufacturers, reviewing and showcasing their products in the videos. While YouTube is his primary platform, he also complements the video content with articles published on Hackster.io and authoring books.

Dmitry is a Senior Deep Learning Solutions Architect at NVIDIA. His focus is on large scale efficient deployment of LLMs and their inference. He co-authored several NVIDIA DLI Workshops on the topic, including the one on Sizing LLM Inference Systems. Prior to NVIDIA, Dmitry served as a Co-Founder and CTO of a startup. He had been integrating Computer Vision into gold mining, transportation, energy and other industries.

Dmitry Yanter is an engineering manager with 13+ years at Deutsche Bank, 20 years experience in the industry, holding multiple roles in research, start ups and enterprise. He is member of International Chamber of Commerce working group that defines digital standards for Supply Chain Finance.

Dominik is a Product Manager at ING, where he works we a team composed of multiple entities on platform capabilities for safer and more scalable software delivery. After leading products around process automation and testing capabilities, he now drives a broader initiative towards Continuous Delivery for a complex banking environment with multiple technologies in use, teams and regulatory requirements.

Dominik is a Group Product Manager for Platforms at Giant Swarm with the mission of simplifying developers' lives through intuitive developer platforms. He started as a Full Stack Software Engineer, discovered his passion for DevOps and product organizations, and transitioned into Product Management for cloud technology. He actively shares his experiences through talks, articles, and publications.

For 20 years Dominik has worked in software development, covering roles from hands-on developer to architect to technical leader. For the last 5 years he's been deeply immersed in AI technologies - building real systems, running experiments, and figuring out what survives contact with production.

CEO and Lead Architect of DAENET GmbH – ACP Digital, Microsoft's long-term ADvanced Certified Partner and a leading technology integrator specialised in software technologies, with a strong focus on AI, Cloud Computing and IoT.

Dr. Damir Dobric is a highly skilled and experienced Lead Software Architect specialising in delivering innovative, highly customised software solutions and consulting services.

In addition to his role at DAENET, Damir is also a Microsoft Most Valuable Professional (MVP). The Microsoft MVP Award is a prestigious recognition given to exceptional technical experts. Damir is also a part of an elite group of professionals known as Microsoft Regional Directors who actively offer guidance, support, and expertise in various Microsoft technologies across industries.

Damir's commitment to excellence in software development and his dedication to helping others have earned him a reputation as a thought leader in the industry. He is a well-known international speaker and keynote speaker at conferences, inspiring people, showcasing his expertise and passion for technology.

With a keen eye for innovation and a deep understanding of cutting-edge technologies, Damir Dobric is a valuable asset to both DAENET, ACP Group and the broader Microsoft ecosystem. His work continues to inspire and support other professionals, young people and students in their pursuit of technical excellence and innovation.

Furthermore, he serves as an external professor for Software Engineering and Cloud Computing at the Frankfurt University of Applied Sciences, teaching AI, Cloud and Software Development. Damir holds a PhD in Computational Intelligence from the University of Plymouth, UK.

Dr. Ramin Hasani is the co-founder and CEO of Liquid AI.  Previously, he was jointly appointed as a Principal Machine Learning Scientist at the Vanguard Group and a Research Affiliate at MIT CSAIL.  Ramin’s research focuses on robust deep learning and decision-making in complex dynamical systems.  Prior to that, he was a Postdoctoral Associate at CSAIL MIT, leading research on modeling intelligence and sequential decision-making.  He received his Ph.D. degree with distinction in Computer Science from the Vienna University of Technology, Austria.  His Ph.D. dissertation and continued research on Liquid Neural Networks have been recognized internationally with numerous nominations and awards.

Du’An Lightfoot is a Sr. AI Engineer at Akamai, where he specializes in advancing agentic AI systems and helping organizations leverage AI to solve infrastructure challenges and increase developer productivity. Drawing from his diverse background as a USAF veteran and technical experience at Cisco and Cerner, Du’An brings a unique perspective on implementing AI solutions in enterprise environments. He has delivered presentations at major industry events including AWS Re:Invent, We Are Developers, Code With Claude, and the AI Engineer World Fair, focusing on practical AI applications that transform how technical teams work. Through his hands-on experience with Agentic AI technologies and dedication to mentoring professionals through AI transformation, Du’An provides actionable insights and concrete strategies for developers to embrace AI-powered workflows and become productivity champions within their organizations.

Eberhard Wolff has 20+ years of experience as an architect and consultant - often on the intersection of business and technology. He is Head of Architecture at SWAGLab. As a speaker, he has given talks at international conferences and as an author, he has written more than 100 articles and books e.g. about Microservices and Continuous Delivery. His technological focus is on modern architectures – often involving Cloud, Domain-driven Design, and Microservices.

Co-founder & CTO at PingCAP (the company behind TiDB), an entrepreneur with a hacker's mind & heart. Full-stack engineer, for real. Pioneer in building cloud-native systems. Building distributed systems and databases with ♥️. Building a company like a distributed system.

Ed is an engineer and marketer experienced in distributed ledger technologies (DLT), model-based design, and advanced vehicle systems. He specializes in translating complex systems into scalable, user-centric solutions.

At Hashgraph, Ed helps grow the Hedera network by onboarding developers and partnering with organizations building the next generation of Web3 applications. He is passionate about leading interdisciplinary teams, fostering developer communities, and turning emerging technologies into practical impact. Ed holds a Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from Virginia Tech and a Master’s in Management from Harvard.

Edith Harbaugh is the CEO and Co-founder of LaunchDarkly, the comprehensive feature management platform that equips software teams to proactively reduce the risk of shipping bad software and AI applications while accelerating their release velocity. LaunchDarkly serves over 5,500 of the most innovative enterprises, including a quarter of the Fortune 500. Edith holds two patents in deployment and earned a B.S. in Engineering from Harvey Mudd College.

For over a decade, Eduard has been working as an application developer at Finanz Informatik, currently working as a Data Engineer in the Data Analytics division. He designs data-driven solutions for analyzing and optimizing business processes, with a strong focus on efficiency, customer value, and technological innovation. He is especially passionate about working with modern database and analytics technologies and continuously explores new ways to turn complex data into actionable insights.

Edvin Teskeredzic is a Senior AI Software Engineer at Infobip, working on developer tooling, streaming data, ML system design, and AI agents. With a background in Electrical Engineering and experience across both academia and industry, he focuses on building systems that make complex technical workflows easier, faster, and more observable. Edvin works on improving developer experience through practical tooling, inference optimization, and observability for AI-powered systems. Outside of work, he enjoys pub quizzes, science fiction, and probably optimizing things that did not need optimizing.

I am a backend engineer at SoundCloud with a passion for solving technical problems and delivering results through fast, incremental progress. I transitioned into tech from social sciences by earning a computer science degree in Germany. My previous background gave me an edge in soft skills and communication, which I believe are often the biggest challenges in software engineering—not technical complexity, but aligning teams and ensuring clear communication. I was fortunate to be mentored by exceptional women and now mentor amazing women, hoping to help pave the way for greater diversity.

Elaine is a Senior Staff Engineer at SFEIR where she works on projects including mobile technologies since 2013. She has worked on several projects in native Android/iOS and Flutter. She's a GDE for the Google Assistant, Flutter and Dart

Elena is a seasoned program manager with over 13 years of experience leading complex cross-functional initiatives in engineering organizations. Her career took a transformative turn during a collaboration with SAP Sustainability, where she led the creation of the SAP AI ethics policy, aligned with UNESCO’s Recommendation on the Ethics of AI—a project that sparked a deep passion for artificial intelligence and its broader ethical and societal implications.

This enthusiasm brought Elena to the forefront of AI innovation. Most recently, Elena founded and built the AI.ccelerate program at SAP Signavio, an initiative designed to empower developers by integrating agentic coding practices into their daily workflows. Through this program, Elena bridges cutting-edge AI with practical, responsible implementation in enterprise environments.

Elisa Schön works at the intersection of psychology, leadership, and organizational design - making emotional dynamics visible and usable for leaders navigating complex systems.
With 10+ years of People & Culture leadership (Chief People Officer at GotPhoto, CHRO at UMI/Volkswagen, scaling teams from 10 to 100+), she's seen firsthand how cognitive overwhelm breaks high-performing teams. Currently pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Clinical Psychology at Medizinische Hochschule Brandenburg, she bridges neuroscience with practical organizational application.
As a certified Systemic Coach and Counselor, Elisa specializes in translating psychological research into actionable frameworks for tech leaders. Her approach: Emotions are data - and in the AI age, brain health is the ultimate competitive advantage.
Currently Senior HR Business Partner at Der Tagesspiegel, she also runs a coaching practice focused on helping professionals build resilience in high-change environments.

Ellen Abarr (Jones) is a Customer Success Manager at DX, Atlassian's developer intelligence platform, where she partners with enterprise engineering organizations to measure, benchmark, and improve developer productivity. Based in Tennessee, Ellen works directly with engineering leaders to identify workflow friction, quantify the impact of AI investments, and build data-driven strategies that improve developer experience at scale. She brings a practitioner's perspective on what it actually takes to shift engineering culture through measurement.

Emil Sorensen and kapa.ai has been on the forefront of AI-based documentation assistants since they launched in June 2023 and completed the YCombinator S23 batch. 

Now Emil works closely with top tech companies including OpenAI, Docker, Mapbox and OSS projects to help provide instant AI-answers to developers worldwide.

Born in Modena in 1980, I have been working with Ruby on Rails for over 20 years. 

I'm a software engineer and consultant, passionate about the Rails framework and its community. Since the early days of Rails, I’ve been helping small and medium-sized businesses build and maintain robust web applications. I enjoy sharing what I’ve learned over the years, and I’ve had the pleasure of speaking at conferences like Modena DevFest and RubyDay 2024 in Verona, where I focus on delivering practical, real-world insights from my experience in the field.

Born in Brazil, loving tech since very young!
Graduated in Computer Science and worked for ExxonMobil for a bit, afterwards came to Germany to work at e.Solutions and then finally at EGYM SE!
Magic the Gathering aficionado and hoping to lead bigger and bigger teams in the future!

For the last 15 years I have been working on Web Development, projects from scratch, helping clients and agencies to build the entire idea until the final product. Coding on both Front-end and Back-end, depending on the project and the available resources.
I have worked with a variety of technologies and frameworks, and I am always looking for new challenges and opportunities to learn and grow.
Currently I am working as a Tech Lead for one of the biggest Airlines in Europe @ Globant. Using Next.js, Typescript, Tailwind CSS, Docker, Storybook, and more.
I have used Javascript, Typescript, React, Vue, GraphQL, MongoDB, MySQL, CSS, Sass, Docker, PHP, Wordpress, Laravel and more. I also love Data related subjects, and been learning Data Science with R and Python on my spare time.
speakers.company_position: Senior Web UI Developer and Tech Lead

speakers.bio:
Erhardt Wunderlich works as a freelance  coach and supports companies in improving the development processes to deliver high quality products.
Since 1990, Mr. Wunderlich has dealt with the topic of software testing in various industries (including telecommunications, storage automation, rail vehicles, automotive) in different roles (including developer, tester, test manager, test department manager, quality coach) and has also supported the agile transformation in different organizations.

Eric Bowman, Chief Technology Officer at King, brings over 30 years of visionary leadership and technical expertise to his role, guiding the company’s technological future with clarity and creativity. Known for transforming complex technical ideas into actionable strategies, Eric shapes King's long-term vision and spearheads innovative solutions that redefine gaming experiences.

At King, Eric leads a dynamic, multidisciplinary tech team looking after the foundational technologies underpinning all King games, including the cutting-edge AI and Machine Learning group. He fosters a culture of collaboration, innovation, and continuous improvement. His leadership extends throughout the organization, empowering teams and individuals to leverage technology effectively and maximize their creative potential. Passionate about nurturing talent, Eric champions leadership development, cultivating a culture of inclusion and growth.

Eric’s work positions King at the forefront of a rapidly evolving technological landscape, driving innovations that captivate new players, delight dedicated fans, and advance King’s core mission: Making the World Playful.

Before King, Eric was CTO at TomTom, overseeing global technology initiatives. He also made significant contributions as Zalando’s first Vice President of Engineering, and as the technical programming lead for Electronic Arts' iconic game, The Sims 1.0.

Eric’s extensive track record underscores his unique ability to envision and execute transformative technology strategies, cementing his reputation as a thought leader and influential figure in the industry.

Erica is a developer lead at CloudAMQP, where she's spent several years building and operating message broker infrastructure with LavinMQ and RabbitMQ. She enjoys helping developers get comfortable with messaging patterns and the tooling that surrounds them.

Etienne is an AI/ML expert, co-founder & CEO of NuMind — a startup developing LLMs specialized in information extraction. Etienne holds a physics PhD (ENS+MIT), led the ML group of Wolfram Research, and wrote Introduction to Machine Learning.

Evgenij is a passionate technologist at the heart of GitHub's field organisation, Evgenij specialises in bridging the gap between technical complexity and real business value — helping companies navigate everything from GitHub Copilot adoption to security compliance and AI governance. Known for his hands-on approach and deep technical expertise, he brings both credibility and energy to the stage.

Senior Developer Advocate at Qdrant with 8 years of IT experience across software engineering, machine learning, and technical management, and 4 years in Developer Relations. Holds a Master’s in Machine Learning, Data Analytics, and Data Engineering. Passionate about NLP, data-centric AI, and the role of vector search in advancing AI technologies.

Fabian Hedin is an accomplished entrepreneur and technology leader with a diverse background in founding and leading innovative companies—including serving as Co-Founder and CTO of Lovable, Founder and CEO of Tentium, and Founder and CTO of TenFAST. His earlier experience includes roles as Frontend Lead at Depict and Fullstack Engineer at CurbFood.

Fabiano Amorim is a Microsoft Data Platform most valuable professional (MVP) since 2011. With over two decades of experience, Fabiano is well known in the database community for his performance tuning abilities and the many conference speaking engagements around the world.
He writes articles for Simple-Talk (https://www.simple-talk.com/author/fabiano-amorim/) and his blog is on http://blogfabiano.com.

Software engineer with extensive experience building robust backend and frontend applications. Skilled in writing clean, maintainable code, and optimizing performance and scalability of complex systems. Proven ability to lead critical architecture migrations, mentor development teams, and deliver efficient, impactful solutions under pressure. Passionate collaborator and continuous learner, eager to improve and share knowledge.

Florian is a Solution Architect at Lunaris Digital Solutions and a Microsoft MVP for Azure IoT and DevOps with many years of experience in DevOps, Cloud, and Digitalization. He helps companies develop efficient and effective solutions that sustainably lead their digital projects to success.

Florian Deter currently serves as General Manager EMEA Strategic Partner Sales, He has been responsible before for Microsoft's enterprise business in Germany as Managing Director of Microsoft Germany and Enterprise Commercial Lead since August 1, 2022.

Florian began his career in consulting and management roles at Oliver Wyman, Accenture, and PwC. He later served as Executive Vice President overseeing the global portfolio of Nordcloud, an IBM company. Prior to that, he led CIO Advisory and the Cloud Applications Service Line at IBM.

After earning his PhD in Physics, Dr. Florian Gather transitioned to software development and joined TNG, where he currently works as an Associate Partner. He specializes in analyzing software projects - conducting due diligence, architecture reviews, and technical assessments to help organizations improve system resilience and maintainability. Florian founded ArchUnit.NET, an open-source tool for enforcing architectural rules in .NET projects. With TNG’s Innovation Hacking team, he drives robotics initiatives that explore LLM integration and Robotics Foundational Models (RFMs), alongside prototypes like smart office systems and interactive furniture. Passionate about microprocessor electronics and smart home automation, Florian explores practical ways to merge hardware tinkering with modern software practices.

Frank Schlesinger is CIO DACH Platforms and a member of the management team at Nexi Germany. In his role, he is responsible for IT and infrastructure in Germany and the entire DACH region. 

In previous positions, he was Head of Technology at ImmobilienScout24 and, prior to that, Head of Agile and Project Management, where he was responsible for cloud migration and a major organisational restructuring. 

After graduating with a degree in computer science, Frank began his career as a Java software developer, helping German banks and savings banks equip their teams with modern technology for banking processes.

Dr Dr Franz Kiraly is the director for the German Center for Open Source AI, the German non-profit organisation supporting sovereign European open source AI software projects. Dr Kiraly has held technical leadership positions in industry, and academic faculty positions (University College London, Alan Turing Institute), with a career focus on open source and applied AI.
speakers.company_name: German Center for Open Source AI

speakers.bio:
Franziska Höhne is a Project Management Professional (PMP), industrial engineer, coach, and international speaker specializing in communication during organizational transformation.
With nearly 20 years of experience in complex, global environments, she works at the intersection of technology, leadership, and psychology to help leaders and teams navigate change with clarity and confidence.
Her perspective combines engineering precision with psychological insight. She focuses on how language, tone, and interaction influence alignment, decision-making, and trust - especially when organizations restructure, adopt new systems, or shift their operating models.
She supports and leads transformation projects by designing frameworks that make change understandable, transparent, and actionable. Her work turns uncertainty into shared direction and enables teams to move from reaction to ownership.
Her approach is grounded in practical tools, intercultural experience, and nearly two decades of hands-on project and audit work.
Franziska serves as Vice President Marketing of the PMI Germany Chapter, part of the global Project Management Institute (PMI), and is a member of the German Speakers Association (GSA).

As CEO of the Cardano Foundation, Frederik Gregaard brings over two decades of leadership experience, including his tenure at PwC, where he spearheaded transformative initiatives in digital innovation. Under his guidance, the Foundation has become a catalyst for blockchain adoption, driving impactful solutions in decentralized governance and sustainability. Frederik’s leadership emphasizes transparency, regulatory alignment, and fostering global collaboration to build a more inclusive and decentralized future.

With over a decade of experience spanning blockchain, cybersecurity, and web development, Gabe brings deep technical expertise and entrepreneurial spirit to the Algorand ecosystem. As a Senior Developer Relations Engineer at the Algorand Foundation, he bridges the gap between emerging technology and practical implementation. Previously, he co-founded TxnLab and helped build NFDomains, the leading identity solution on the Algorand blockchain. Having worked with both early-stage startups and established enterprises, Gabe leverages his diverse background to help builders navigate the full spectrum of challenges in blockchain development. He is committed to empowering developers through hands-on technical education and community building.

Gal Shubeli is a Software and AI Engineer at FalkorDB, building graph-powered retrieval systems for AI. He works on GraphRAG, modeling knowledge as interconnected nodes so LLMs can reason across multiple hops with greater accuracy and coherence. With experience developing machine learning and computer vision systems for medical ultrasound, and an MSc in Electrical Engineering from Ben-Gurion University, Gal is passionate about transforming cutting-edge AI research into practical, real-world applications.

Gaurav Gahlot is a Software Engineer passionate about building low-level systems, Rust, and Cloud Native ecosystem. He loves to share his learning via his blog, and is also a maintainer of CNCF’s Akri project.

Georg studied computer science and decided to become an app developer when the first iPhone was released. He learned Objective-C, then later Swift and Kotlin. Today, he focuses on architecting and developing apps with Kotlin Multiplatform, Flutter, and native technologies.

With over a decade of experience, he specializes in application architecture, data modeling, testing, and code quality. Georg has spoken at WeAreDevelopers, InfoQ Dev Summit, and Almato DevCon, as well as several meetups. He has also been a guest on a podcast and taught a course on mobile app development.

George is a distributed systems expert and a hands-on engineering leader. He is a Director of Engineering at MongoDB, focusing on implementing cloud native technologies at enterprise scale. He is an Ambassador of the Data on Kubernetes community and the author of The Platform Engineering Playbook, by Packt. Most recently, he has been a speaker at global events like Kubecon, OpenSource Summit, Hashiconf, LeadDev, SaaStr, and more.

George is a seasoned IT professional with 15+ years of experience, evolving from technical roles to leading engineering teams in security-critical sectors. Passionate about coaching and empowering others, he combines hands-on expertise with strategic leadership.

A former professional chess player, George won the "Romanian Chess Champion" title with his club team in 2004. At 16, he founded and grew an ISP company in Bucharest to 600 clients before selling it at 18. He later pursued an Executive MBA at IMT Paris to strengthen his leadership skills.

Recognized in the Financial Times' "100 Faces of Innovation" in 2016, George is a sought-after Cybersecurity expert specializing in Fraud and Deepfakes. He has spoken at over 20 conferences all around the world, and his beginner’s Cybersecurity course has helped 150,000+ students start their careers in the field.

Giampaolo Casolla is a Senior Data Scientist at GetYourGuide in Berlin, working at the intersection of machine learning, generative AI, and product development. He builds AI-powered experiences used by travelers at scale, including GetYourGuide’s AI Discovery Agent in ChatGPT and AI Itineraries on Google Search. Previously at Amazon, his work on anomaly detection and forecasting contributed to more than $20M in cost savings. He holds an MSc in Applied Mathematics cum laude, has nine published papers with 380+ citations, and supports the local data community as Chair of the PyData Berlin Video Committee.

Glenn F. Henriksen is a mentor and developer from Norway. As the co-founder and CTO of Justify, he gets to build new legal tools for everyone to use, helping to create better communication and less conflict in relationships. He's continuously exploring new tools, processes and technologies, and improving how he and his fellow developers work with code, tasks and projects. He has been a Microsoft Development MVP, a part of the Microsoft Regional Director program and is an ASP.NET Insider and an Azure Advisor. In the past 25+ years he has co-owned two companies, worked as a consultant, manager, support tech, network admin, developer, architect, technical lead and more, but his favourite things are still swapping code for food and building stuff that makes a difference in people’s lives.

Goetz Markgraf ist Consultant bei der codecentric AG. Als passionierter Softwareentwickler seit über 30 Jahren und Product Owner macht es ihm besondere Freude, zwischen Technik und Fachbereich zu vermitteln und zu "übersetzen". Er beschäftigt sich seit Mitte 2023 mit dem Thema "KI-gestützte Softwareentwicklung". Privat ist er Hobbyautor und stolzer Vater dreier Kinder.

I am a software engineer specialized in developing databases in Java. I love understanding how libraries and frameworks work under the hood and to design and implement high-performance systems. I have worked on prototypes such as ToroDB, the first Spanish database unicorn Devo and since 2022 I'm working at StarTree as an Apache Pinot contributor.

Grisha Pavlotskyi is the Chief Transformation Officer of Miro, the world’s leading visual workspace for innovation. Grisha spends most of his time with customers, helping them think through AI transformation topics - be it the technology side of it, ways of working, or how to re-engineer processes from an AI-first principles basis. Together with Miro's CIO, Grisha also drives Miro's own internal AI transformation. Prior to Miro, Grisha served as a Managing Partner at Deloitte for 19 years, focusing on helping organisations find ways to accelerate innovation.

Guillaume Esnou is Field CTO and Head of Technology for Invisible Technologies in EMEA, where he leads AI deployment strategy and technical delivery for enterprise clients. He brings deep expertise in AI and analytics, having built and scaled production-grade AI systems across sectors at McKinsey as an Associate Partner. Most recently, he led Engineering, Insights & Analytics at Surveyor Capital, Citadel hedge fund’s leading fundamental equities strategy. Guillaume holds an M.S. in Operations Research from Columbia University and a Diplôme d’Ingénieur in Mechanical and Industrial Engineering from Arts et Métiers. At Invisible, he focuses on building scalable AI systems that transform operations and drive measurable business impact.

I'm G/, Field CTO at Upsun, a cloud applications platform dedicated to simplifying application deployment and operations. With a focus on AI for DevEx and Sustainability, I strive to make running apps seamless for every developer.

As Group CIO at RWE, Gülnaz Öneş spearheads the energy transition through technology, with a strong emphasis on Data & AI and business agility.

She brings a distinguished track record across the automotive, financial services, and energy sectors. Gülnaz spent more than two decades within the Daimler
Group, holding executive roles including CTO at Daimler Truck and CIO at Mercedes-Benz Mobility.
Known for her people-first, integrative leadership style, Gülnaz turns technology and data into tangible business outcomes, strengthening collaboration and delivering measurable impact across global organisations.

Cloud Architect specializes in the management of large-scale systems and the development of cutting-edge solutions based on platform engineering , Infrastructure and operations best practices. My work is focused on cloud-native architectures , Disconnected cloud and lately I am mainly working on AI Infrastructure and operations. I am an international speaker, researcher and also an active contributor to the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) this involvement  led to my official recognition as a Kubestronaut.

I am a software architect and developer. I love to work on open source projects, producing articles, videos and giving talks on software development.

I'm the lead developer of the ABP framework (see https://github.com/abpframework/abp and https://abp.io/)

I'm mostly interested in developing reusable frameworks & libraries, distributed systems, multi-threaded and scalable solutions. I'm talking at local and international conferences about multi-tenancy, microservice architecture, domain-driven design, modular systems and developer tools.

Visit my website to see a list of my previous talks: https://halilibrahimkalkan.com

Hanna is Data & AI lead at teccle group, where she designs and implements data platforms using Microsoft Fabric, Azure AI and OpenAI services. With certifications in data, AI and Fabric engineering, she supports projects from architecture to deployment across industries. Passionate about empowering others, she supports the tech community through ITgirls and ReDi School, where she mentors, teaches and encourages more women to find their voice and confidence in tech.

Haoqi Wu is a research engineer at TikTok, where he works on building production-grade solutions for AI-powered services with regulatory compliance and user trust. His current work focuses on designing and deploying high-performance invisible watermarking algorithms and systems for AI-generated content provenance, ensuring robust content authentication that meets global regulatory requirements. He has contributed to projects spanning invisible watermarking for AIGC compliance, privacy-enhancing technologies for LLM inference/training services. His research interests lie at the intersection of deep learning and responsible AI. He is dedicated to building practical systems that balance technical performance, regulatory compliance, and user experience.

Hi, my name is Harald and I'm a passionate Python developer interested in development, DevOps and AI. I'm currently located in Austria working as a Senior Software Developer and Python Technical Lead for Anexia.

I also work on Open Source Projects and write Articles and Tutorials on my blog.

Hareesha Narayana Shirankallu is the Managing Director at Bosch Global Software Technologies GmbH, leading the European business. With over 22 years at Bosch, he brings extensive experience across automotive software, engineering, and global delivery models. He has led multidisciplinary teams across geographies and played a key role in building large-scale nearshore and offshore delivery centers, including in India and Vietnam. Starting his career as a software engineer, he has contributed to innovation initiatives and holds patents that are in production within Bosch products. He is passionate about building high-performing teams and believes people-centric leadership drives sustainable business success.

Passionate software craftsman with 29+ years experience across development technologies and cloud-native architectures. Expertise with Microsoft Aspire, distributed systems (Dapr), and AI-assisted development including Copilot SDK integration. Focus on application modernization, observability, and securing these environments.

As a Developer Relations Engineer at New Relic, Harry helps engineers, DevOps, and SREs implement proactive observability at scale - including observability for AI systems - leveraging open source technologies and the New Relic platform.

Hartmut is a software engineer and tech lead with a strong passion for architecture, data streaming, and distributed systems. He has designed and delivered solutions for mission-critical platforms, working with clients including HSBC, NEX Group plc, Raiffeisen Switzerland, Deutsche Bahn, and eu-LISA. Hartmut is driven by a desire to see the bigger picture and excels at aligning engineering teams through clear, compelling architectural designs.

Hazel is an experienced Analyst & Consultant with a strong track record of bridging technology, data and business needs. Currently with Schwarz Digits , an in-house technology powerhouse for Lidl & Kaufland supermarkets. 

Born & raised in Hong Kong, studied & lived in USA for 15 years; currently residing in Germany for 11 years. Lived and worked globally, bringing a multicultural perspective to engineering soft skills and team dynamics. 

Hazel is passionate about improving team communication and performance, bridging the communication gap between technical and business colleagues.

Hellmar is a Solution Architect at ClickHouse. He has 20+ years experience in Analytics, Big Data,  and Event Streaming. Hellmar worked at Hortonworks and Imply and helped implementing the first Data Lake at ING Bank.

Scalable tech-startups are Helmut Schönenberger’s passion as co-founder and CEO of UnternehmerTUM, the Center for Innovation and Business Creation at Technische Universität München (TUM). He also serves as TUM’s Vice President Entrepreneurship, as a professor at TUM School of Management and as a member of the advisory board of tech companies. 

With his background as a founder, aerospace engineer and venture capitalist at UVC Partners, he loves to help new ventures solve strategy, recruiting, business development and future financing challenges.

Hendrik Ebbers is the director of open source at Hashgraph and founder of Open Elements. He is a Java champion, a member of JSR expert groups and a JavaOne rockstar.
Hendrik is a member of the Eclipse JakartaEE working group (WG) and the Eclipse Adoptium WG. In addition, Hendrik Ebbers is a member of the Board of Directors of the Eclipse Foundation.
At Linux Foundation Hendrik is leading the Hiero project, that is the base of Hedera, one of the biggest public DLTs. He leads the technical steering committee for the Hiero project and is a member of a Technical Advisory Board of LF. He gave several sessions about decentralized ledger technology at conferences and even lectures about that topic at universities.

Hendrik Lösch is a management consultant and architect at ZEISS Digital Innovation. The focus of his work is on the development and evaluation of software, based on Microsoft technologies. He also enjoys writing and talking about his work as well as his enthusiasm for clean code, software evolution and test automation in its various forms.

Short bio: Hendrikje Rudnick is a business journalist and Managing Editor at Business Insider Germany. She reports on the labor market, careers, and artificial intelligence, with a focus on how these trends are transforming the workplace.

Henriette Brune is Chief of Staff at FINN, one of Germany’s fastest-growing mobility startups. In her role, she works at the intersection of technology, business strategy, and organizational design, driving initiatives that accelerate innovation, scale tech effectiveness, and unlock hidden business value.

Her path into tech leadership wasn’t traditional: starting her career in HR, Henriette now partners closely with FINN’s CTO and engineering leadership. This cross-disciplinary background allows her to connect dots that are often siloed, linking engineering, product, and business departments to deliver measurable impact.

At FINN, she has led projects such as the capitalization of R&D efforts and designing innovation frameworks that helped the company move quickly on frontier technologies like Agentic AI. She is passionate about transforming tech organizations from “cost centers” into profit multipliers, and about building structures where ambitious goals, fast iteration, and even failure become catalysts for growth.

Henriette speaks on automation, innovation, and organizational scaling, sharing practical playbooks for leaders who want to combine speed with resilience in fast-moving industries.
speakers.company_position: Chief of Staff to the CTO

speakers.bio: Hergy Fongue is a Lead Developer at Finanz Informatik, the central IT service provider of the German Savings Banks Finance Group. He plays a key role in the transformation of the central data warehouse from mainframe-based infrastructure to a modern, decentralized architecture. His focus lies on building modern cloud-native core components for business-rule-driven data services. This involves ensuring both the semantic integrity of data and the flexible processing of individual records as well as large files.

Holger Hammel is Vice President Engineering at HelloFresh, where he leads the Consumer Alliance – overseeing all customer-facing digital products and related services across HelloFresh brands globally. He joined the company in 2024 to further scale and strengthen its consumer technology ecosystem. With more than 15 years of leadership experience at companies such as eBay, Mobile International, Aiven, and OneFootball, Holger has a strong track record in building high-performing engineering organizations. He is particularly known for bridging the gap between engineering and business strategy, driving innovation, and delivering scalable, customer-centric solutions.

I am a recruitment industry professional with over 15 years experience as an agency recruiter, Recruitment manager, Internal Head of Talent, recruitment trainer, founder of award winning online recruiting platform WorkShape and now Editor and Community builder at Recruiting Brainfood - the best weekly newsletter in recruitment.

Hussein Jundi is a Munich-based AI Engineering Lead who enjoys turning ambiguous ideas into real, scalable solutions. At E.ON Digital Technology, he drives AI engineering projects across E.ON’s cloud native software engineering technology platforms, assessing new capabilities and leading initiatives that accelerate modernization and AI utilization. Previously, he led Data & AI squads at E.ON and helped build and scale data platforms that power reliable analytics and product development in the mobility/charging space.

M.Sc in Computer Engineering, specialized in advanced computer architectures. Now researcher at Fraunhofer FOKUS in Quality Engineering for Quantum Computing. Works in several industry and research projects in the fields of quantum computing, artificial intelligence, IoT, IT security and telco. Main interest is researching efficient application development and implementation of algorithms especially in the quantum domain (Quantum DevOps). Other areas of interest are quantum computing, quantum networks, IoT, Industry 4.0, 5G/6G, AI, robots and the high-level quantum  programming language Qrisp.

Ingo Eichhorst is a tech expert with over 15 years of experience as a developer, solution architect, and CTO. Currently, he works as an engineering trainer at IONOS and as a lecturer for Machine Learning and Generative AI at the University of Applied Sciences Nordhausen. As a solution architect at Monstarlab and KPMG, he developed AI solutions for industry, banking, legal tech, and e-commerce. In addition to his involvement with AI startups and open-source projects, where he is responsible for technical planning and implementation, he works as a mentor and regularly shares his knowledge at tech conferences.

Dr. Irina Brancovic is a Software Engineer at the Hamburg-based adtech company, adjoe. She earned her PhD in Information Engineering in Italy, where she not only conducted academic research but also taught at the university level in Italian, a language she learned from scratch during her studies. Since joining adjoe, Irina has been instrumental in overcoming challenges to build the backend infrastructure, which handles billions of requests from 600 million daily users. She takes pride in her contributions to creating innovative ad technologies and fostering a collaborative and productive work environment.

Irina Terekhova is Co-founder and Chief Product Officer at Banalytics, an open edge orchestration platform for cameras, sensors, and industrial devices.

At Banalytics she owns the product strategy, use-case definition, and roadmap across four customer segments: industrial sensing teams, AI data collection pipelines, SMB operations, and homelab builders.
