Her focus is on making edge infrastructure observable and deployable without requiring teams to build the operational layer from scratch.

Before Banalytics, Irina spent nearly two decades in product management and business analysis across AI, security, and financial services — including launching an AI line of business at an industrial hardware company and leading product teams at a global software consultancy.

Isha Salania is a Cloud & AI Solution Engineer at Microsoft, with experience spanning the technology, automotive, and insurance industries and every major cloud platform.
Her work centers on the architectural decisions that determine whether AI systems survive change: sovereign AI, confidential computing, edge RAG, and agent interoperability. She brings a standards-first perspective grounded in the conviction that open protocols outlive products. Isha is also a tech content creator with over one million followers and a regular voice on AI at events from the European Parliament, on a mission to make advanced AI approachable for the next generation of engineers.

Iulia’s mission is to make tech exciting, understandable, and accessible to the new generation.
With a background spanning data science, AI, cloud architecture, and open source she brings a unique perspective on bridging technical depth with approachability.
She’s building her own brand, Back To Engineering, through which she creates a community for tech enthusiasts, engineers, and makers. From YouTube videos on building robots from scratch, to conference talks or keynotes about real, grounded AI, and technical blogs & tutorials — Iulia shares her message worldwide on how to turn complex concepts into tools developers can use every day.

Jaipal Singh is CTO at Prem AI, where he leads the development of Sovereign AI solutions. Prem is focused on allowing enterprises to access frontier intelligence in a privacy-preserving manner. Prior to Prem, he led AI teams at Staple and EY, building large-scale document processing systems and low-code ML deployment platforms.  A researcher and engineer at heart, Jaipal has published at The Web Conference (WWW 2019) on multimodal fake news detection and at EAMT 2020 on multilingual sentiment analysis, work that grew out of his thesis on cross-lingual event extraction at IIIT Hyderabad. At Prem, he's focused on making private AI practical - proving that the most powerful AI is the one you own.

Jakob Steinschaden is editor-in-chief at the European tech outlet Trending Topics, co-founder of eustella — the sovereign European AI agent — host of tech podcasts, and author of two books, "Phänomen Facebook" and "Digitaler Frühling." He is based in in Vienna, Austria.

Jakobus Schuster is the founder and CEO of Notarity, the leading platform for digital notary appointments in Europe. He studied law at Bucerius Law School and worked in international law firms during his studies. Already as a student, he developed a strong interest in legal tech, taught himself fundamental coding skills, and implemented his first legal data science projects.

After completing his legal clerkship in Hamburg, he began building Notarity, which is now used worldwide to handle notarial processes digitally, efficiently, and independently of location.

I've been building cool stuff on the Internet since 1999. Trigram tech enthusiast (IBM, AWS).

I help enterprise companies navigate their digital transformation journeys, specializing in data center migrations, cloud migrations & modernizations, as well as cloud-native development.

When I'm offline, you can find me chasing the Endless Summer with my family, exploring surf spots around the world.

Currently based in Copenhagen, Denmark.

Software Engineer passionate about building products users love. Believes that efficient team collaboration is the key to success. When not coding, he’s probably (once again) reminding everyone why proper automated testing is essential for a happy life. After hours, he takes on most challenging role yet - taming his two kids.

I'm a developer advocate at Snowflake who gets excited about helping developers do cool things with AI and data in the cloud. I found my happy place bridging the gap between complex tech and the developers who want to use it. Think of me as a technical matchmaker – connecting developers with the right tools and watching the magic happen. When I'm not geeking out about AI, you'll find me spending time with my son and collecting new hobbies (because why stick to just one?)

Jamie Madden leads education at n8n, the AI workflow automation platform, where he runs the function end to end: strategy, curriculum, certification, assessment, and partner enablement. His connection to AI and automation runs long. He co-authored a machine learning paper on web search and news delivery back in 2002, two decades before the current wave, and has spent much of his 25-year career building automation and developer tooling across full-stack engineering and distributed teams. Jamie built the n8n Academy from the ground up, from the courses and the learning platform to the certification program that trains the company's users and partners. He has also contributed to the WordPress Training Team, helping a global open source community learn. Across all of it, one thread holds: make automation approachable, and keep people in control of the decisions that matter.

Jan Curn is the founder and CEO of Apify (https://apify.com), a full-stack web scraping and automation platform that powers (not only) AI agents with up-to-date data. He has a lifelong passion for software engineering, which earned him an MSc and PhD in computer science and eventually led him to founding Apify. Jan lives between SF and Prague, is active in the tech community in both cities, organizes meetups, and talks about software, startups, or AI.

Jan de Vries is a senior trainer, business IT consultant, coach, researcher and public speaker in the fields of Agile, DevOps, Business Information Management, Service Management, Antifragility and Strategy Development.

He founded:
- BlueOceanRecon.com to facilitate the development of Blue Oceans and Lean Startups.
- Antifragility.works to conduct research on the practical application of antifragility in organisations.

I am a Software Developer working at ClickHouse. My work touches databases, distributed systems, networking, and cloud infrastructure.

In my free time I enjoy learning Spanish, cooking, and windsurfing.

I’m a software engineer working at Sentry and Pebblebyte, where I focus on frontend, DevOps, and making sure observability is everywhere. When I’m not deep in code, you’ll probably find me kitesurfing, road biking, swimming, or hanging off a rock wall. Whether I’m debugging or chasing wind, I’m always looking for that perfect flow.

Jannis is Head of Analytics and Customer Engagement at Finanz Informatik, the central IT service provider of the Savings Banks Finance Group. He is responsible for the strategic use of data across the entire value chain – from data platforms and advanced analytics to AI-driven customer engagement.

His goal is not just to make data available, but to turn it into tangible business value through integrated engagement management, data-driven decision-making, and a strong platform and architecture strategy.

Previously, he led the Data Platform department and served as a project manager for the introduction of modern software technologies at Finanz Informatik. His technical background spans both mainframe and Java development.

Jannis stands for pragmatic innovation, strategic thinking, and collaborative delivery
speakers.company_position: Head of Analytics and Customer Insight

speakers.bio: Jarek is holding Lead Technical Cloud Architect position in Atos Cloud Enterprise Solutions practice. During last decade he was mastering his knowledge and expertise in virtualization and cloud related domains. He is holding multiple technology professional certificates and is recognized as an Atos Senior Expert. Jarek is passionate about sharing knowledge on meetups and conferences. In his free time he loves to play board games with his family and is constantly unraveling new cloud mysteries.

Javier is a member of Google Cloud's Application Modernization practice in EMEA. He focuses on bringing best practices in the areas of developer productivity and the effective use of cloud native development practices to enterprise customers. He works closely with the Google Cloud product teams to scale new technologies and share best practices in the form of webinars, blog posts and open source assets.

Ahmad Jawed Sahebjan is an IT professional with over a decade of experience as a lead developer and senior IT consultant, having worked with organizations such as Volkswagen, Deutsche Bank, Postbank, R+V, and Deutsche Telekom.
Over the years he has worked alongside numerous IT freelancers, giving him a clear picture of what separates those who thrive from those who stay stuck competing on hourly rates. This experience led him to found IT-Freelance-Forge, a platform helping IT freelancers and entrepreneurs in the DACH region build strategic client relationships and financial independence.
He regularly shares insights on his YouTube channel: youtube.com/@JawedSahebjan

Jeff Atwood has spent his career building places where developers learn together at scale. He co-founded Stack Overflow and the Stack Exchange network, transforming Q&A into a global, searchable knowledge base that reshaped how programmers solve problems. He later co-founded Discourse, an open-source discussion platform used by companies, open-source projects, and public communities to host high-signal conversations on the modern web. 

Jeff is also the author of the influential Coding Horror blog, known for pragmatic (and occasionally contrarian) perspectives on software engineering, product decisions, and the incentives that shape human behaviour online. His work sits at the intersection of technology and community: how you design systems that are not only technically scalable, but socially sustainable, especially as AI changes how we search, learn, and collaborate.

Jeff Blankenburg spent the early part of his career in digital advertising, building websites for Victoria’s Secret, Abercrombie & Fitch, and Ford Motor Company, among others.  He also spent 8 years at Microsoft, primarily as an evangelist for any new technology he could get his hands on.  For the next 7 years, he worked on the Amazon Alexa team helping developers make Alexa even smarter. Today, he is the Head of Developer Relations for DigitalOcean. Jeff has also spoken at conferences all over the world, including London, Munich, Sydney, Tokyo, and New York, covering topics ranging from software development technologies to soft skill techniques. He also serves as an organizer for the Stir Trek conference.

I am a Full-stack Engineer turned DevRel Engineer at New Relic. With a background in software engineering and marketing direction, my experience involves: designing and developing user interfaces, addressing customer/client pain points, marketing automation, and community development.

Jennifer Wjertzoch is a Senior Frontend Engineer at DKB Code Factory GmbH, where she builds scalable and accessible web systems in the financial domain.
She specializes in accessibility architecture and believes that inclusion is not an add-on but a fundamental principle of software quality and risk management.
Her work focuses on integrating accessibility into system design, developer workflows, and CI/CD processes to create products that are resilient, compliant, and user-centered.

Jenny Speelman is a Staff Engineer for Developer Relations at Edge Impulse, where she is a technical speaker, developer evangelist, and technical content creator. She's co-author of the O'Reilly book "AI at the Edge: Solving Real World Problems with Embedded Machine Learning" and has presented workshops and tech talks for major tech conferences such as the Grace Hopper Celebration, Edge AI Summit, Embedded Vision Summit, Embedded World, and more. In addition to maintaining the Edge Impulse documentation, she has also created developer-facing resources for Arm Mbed OS and Pelion IoT. Jenny previously worked as a software engineer and IoT consultant for Arm Mbed and Pelion. She graduated with a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from The University of Texas at Austin.

Jens is CTO of Home-of-Tech and Mercedes-Benz Tech Innovation. His journey into tech began around 2006: debugging Internet Explorer 6, writing PHP that would make any modern linter weep, and genuinely believing that document.all was a reasonable API. Somehow, that did not scare him off. 
 
He joined Mercedes-Benz as a software engineer in 2014, and by 2017 had taken on additional responsibility as Principal for Connected Vehicle Services, shaping how cars talk to the cloud and, occasionally, back. Since 2023, he serves as CTO, driving innovation, embedding AI across the product development lifecycle, and steering a 7,500-person engineering organization at the heart of Mercedes-Benz's global software ecosystem. 
 
He still has opinions about JavaScript. They've just gotten harder to revert.

Jens Posingies is an Engineering Manager at Redcare Pharmacy with 25 years of experience in software development, architecture and engineering leadership. Born and raised in East Germany, he studied Business Information Systems at the University of Lüneburg and Media & Education at the University of Rostock.

Over his career, Jens has worked in roles ranging from Java and Node.js developer to Solution Architect and Engineering Manager, including positions at Zalando, TUI, HRS and Redcare Pharmacy. His current focus is on lateral leadership, end-to-end ownership and modular software architectures such as microservices, microfrontends and Kotlin Multiplatform.

He is also a certified nerd with deep expertise in Back to the Future and Star Trek.

Jeremy Murray is the Founder and CEO of Stack8s, a UK-based company on a mission to commoditise GPU compute on Kubernetes and make AI infrastructure as accessible and interchangeable as electricity.

With over a decade of experience in enterprise cloud transformation, Jeremy has led large-scale migrations from legacy platforms to modern cloud environments across highly regulated industries. He holds a PhD from the University of Edinburgh, has authored more than 12 peer-reviewed research papers, and is the inventor of three patents spanning AI, IoT, and advanced computing technologies.

Before founding Stack8s, Jeremy worked across cloud, data, and infrastructure programmes, helping organisations modernise mission-critical systems while balancing performance, security, and compliance requirements. Today, he is focused on solving two of the biggest challenges facing AI adoption: GPU scarcity and cloud lock-in.

Under his leadership, Stack8s has built a sovereign-by-design AI compute platform that enables organisations to access and orchestrate GPU resources across multiple cloud providers, data centres, and on-premises environments through a unified Kubernetes control plane. The company works with research institutes, enterprises, and startups to accelerate AI innovation while maintaining control over data sovereignty and compliance.

Jeremy is a regular speaker on Kubernetes, AI infrastructure, sovereign cloud, and multi-cloud computing, advocating for a future where compute becomes a true commodity and innovation is no longer constrained by infrastructure availability.

Jessie is the Founder of Stakkd: the go to place to discover HR tech. A strategic leader with 13+ years of unique experience in both corporates and startups. She is known for her unconventional approach to People topics and is passionate about HR tech and leveraging the benefits to build the future of work.

Joe is the Co-Founder of PromptMates, a community helping Recruiting & HR leaders become AI-native. Recently Director of AI at Scede (now AI advisor) leading an AI transformation from scratch for a recruiting services business. Passionate about building the skillset of the future in public!

Joël Mesot has been President of ETH Zurich since 2019. At ETH he studied physics and obtained his doctorate with a thesis on high-temperature superconductors. He spent several years researching quantum materials in France, the USA and above all at the Swiss Paul Scherrer Institute (PSI) where he was promoted to head the Laboratory for Neutron Scattering in 2004. In 2008 he became director of the PSI and was promoted full professor at ETH Zurich. Since taking office at ETH Zurich, Mesot has deepened dialogue with political actors both in Switzerland and internationally and driven forwards strategic fields such as AI, energy, space and cyber security.

Joerg Krall is a Senior Industry Business Development Manager for Automotive EMEA at NVIDIA, where he drives business development and strategic partnerships across Europe’s automotive ecosystem. Based in Germany, Joerg works with leading OEMs, Tier 1 suppliers, and technology partners to accelerate the adoption of NVIDIA’s automotive platforms — including NVIDIA DRIVE — across the region.

With deep expertise spanning enterprise GPU solutions, XR/visualisation, and autonomous vehicle technologies, Joerg brings a broad technical and commercial perspective to NVIDIA’s automotive go-to-market in EMEA. He is a regular speaker at industry events, having presented at forums such as the XR-Symposium at SRH Hochschule Heidelberg, and has worked extensively with enterprise and industrial customers across Germany and Europe.

Prior to his focus on automotive, Joerg held roles within NVIDIA’s Worldwide Field Operations supporting enterprise visualisation and vGPU deployments for customers including Thales, Atos, and science+computing ag.

Johannes Kopf, LL.M. is the Chairman of the Executive Board of the Public Employment Service Austria, AMS, where he continues to shape Austria's policymaking for labor market and employment.

Prior to becoming executive chairman in July 2023, Johannes served as a member of the Executive Board of the Public Employment Service Austria from July 2006 to June 2023, as well as Chairman of the Network of European Public Employment Services (PES) from July 2019 to June 2023.

Born and raised in Vienna, Austria, Johannes holds a doctorate in law and an LL.M. in European law. He is a well-respected labor market expert, having gained valuable professional experience in the cabinet of former Minister for Economics and Labour, Dr. Martin Bartenstein, and as a member of the Board of Directors of the AMS Austria, both from 2003 to mid-2006.

Before his time in the ministry, he was a consultant for the Federation of Austrian Industries, also with a focus on labour market and employment policies.

In addition to his ongoing work at AMS Austria, Johannes was awarded the Humanity Prize of the Heinrich Treichl Foundation by the Austrian Red Cross in 2017. He is also an award-winning photographer and highly regarded DJ.

John’s first job was as a professional snowboarder, winning the Junior World Championship in 1999. Since then, he has spent 20 years developing software across Australia, Spain and Germany. He continues to build software, loves to speak at conferences and trains people to accelerate companies from the bottom up, using technical excellence, human interaction and fun.

Jordan is the CEO and Chief Duck Herder of MotherDuck, a data warehouse built on DuckDB. He was previously a founding engineer of Google BigQuery, Director of Product at Google, and Chief Product Officer at SingleStore.

Jordi Spranger is an independent AI and robotics engineer based in Munich, bringing agentic AI to mobile robotics. He has delivered production-grade machine-learning solutions for large enterprises and built the intelligence layer for autonomous mobile robots. He is currently develop
ing spatial-memory systems for embodied agents on top of knowledge graphs, in line with his aspiring robotics startup for agentic robots.

Experienced software developer with 17 years of experience in frontend development. Specialized in the development of web applications with Angular and extensive knowledge of JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. 
Great emphasis on accessible web content and is an Atos Accessibility Champion as well as a Certified Professional in Web Accessibility (CPWA). 
Successfully developed web-based user interfaces while considering the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2.
Leads frontend teams, plans, and implements microfrontend architectures, thus ensuring structured and modular development.

Joerg Tewes joined Exasol as CEO at the beginning of 2023. He spent the first 12 years of his career at data management software company Poet Holdings supporting the company through its early growth stages to its IPO at the German stock exchange. He relocated to the San Francisco Bay Area in 2007 and continued to pursue his career in general management roles at Avid Technology Inc. and Logitech International. In 2014, he joined video and augmented reality start-up Avegant as CEO, where he raised $50M in venture capital funding and strategically expanded the company’s product offerings. From 2018 – 2022, Joerg Tewes held senior leadership roles at Amazon, driving the development and commercialization of its tablet, e-reader and video communication technologies. Joerg Tewes is an active angel investor and mentor to technology start-ups. He holds a degree in computer science from the Technical University in Berlin, Germany.

Jorrin is a former web developer and web development instructor turned blockchain builder and educator. He has spent the past 8 years in the blockchain industry and has been fully committed ever since, making the leap full-time. He is driven by a passion to help build the next layer of the internet — and to equip other builders with the tools, mindset, and conviction to do the same.

Joseph is a leading voice in cybersecurity and AI, developing software and content that shape how developers build securely. His open source game gh.io/scg has helped 10K+ developers gain future-proof security skills. His videos, with 2.8M+ views, simplify complex security topics and deliver actionable tips to a global audience. As a speaker, Joseph has delivered 68 talks across 25 countries over the past four years, captivating audiences with his insights and energetic stage presence.

Josh is a developer at AG Grid, where he's been building AG Studio — a new dashboard library with multi-agent AI baked in. He's a mathematician by training and a maker by instinct, with a particular love for developer tooling, type systems, and intelligent interfaces. Outside of work, Josh can be found tinkering with 3D printers, ski touring in the Alps, or out on long walks with his dog.

As the CTO of Sofascore, a leading live score service that provides real-time sports data, Josip is responsible for overseeing the architecture, development, and scaling their web and mobile platforms, as well as leading a team of talented engineers and developers.

Since joining in 2010, he has overseen the architecture, development, and scaling of the company's web and mobile platforms, guiding a talented team of engineers and developers. His journey at Sofascore began as a software engineer, progressing to system architect and ultimately CTO, reflecting a deep understanding of the company's evolution and a significant contribution to its success. Josip’s passion for technology finds its roots in a youthful curiosity for programming languages, ultimately leading to a formal academic background in computer science. With extensive experience and expertise in web technologies and distributed systems, he thrives on tackling complex challenges that demand creative and innovative solutions. His unwavering mission is to deliver exceptional, reliable solutions that not only meet the needs of users and partners but also continuously improve and optimize Sofascore's performance and functionality.

Jovana is a Senior Technical Evangelist and a Software Engineer at Atlassian, where she bridges the gap between product engineering and the developer community. Based in Sydney, Jovana specialises in showing, not just telling, how modern development teams can work smarter with AI-native tooling. With a background spanning and developer advocacy and hands-on engineering in platform engineering, performance and scalability, she's passionate about making complex workflows tangible through live demos and practical, real-world use cases. She's a 3 times finalist for Tech awards in ANZ region.

As CTO and CCO, Julia Koch is responsible for the strategic direction and execution of software development, guiding her teams in delivering innovative and customer-centric banking solutions for the German Savings Banks Group, which serves over 50 million people in Germany. Her core objective is to lead her teams in shaping the future of modern banking. She’s committed to harnessing AI and other innovative technologies to enhance customer experience.

Julia is a Software Global Black Belt at Microsoft, helping large-scale organizations accelerate dev workflows through pragmatic, end-to-end platform engineering. With a background as a Software Engineer, Dev Lead, and Cloud Solution Architect, she speaks fluent developer and management—and knows how to bridge the gap without losing either side in translation.

She’s a big-picture thinker who also loves to understand the details. Web and frontend will always have a place in her ❤️.

Outside work, Julia is a gamer, a proud fahrradmaus, and a former quarterback for a women’s American football team (yes, really).

Senior Developer from Germany with over 15 years of experience building web apps and leading frontend teams, currently living and working down under in Australia. Pragmatic perfectionist, always looking for opportunities to learn and grow.
Julian Teicke

Julian Totzek-Hallhuber is Lead Solutions Architect at XBOW and has spent nearly two decades on the front lines of application security. Before XBOW, he led the solution architect team at Veracode. From application development and penetration testing to web application security, Julian helps enterprises ship code that holds up under real-world attack.

He's the author of the book Mastering the Art of Application Security Testing, a regular voice on the international conference, trade fair, and webinar circuit, and a familiar name in tech PR, quoted across numerous articles and trade publications. He has also contributed to projects under www.webappsec.org. Between keynotes, you'll find him writing and sharing field notes at julz.de.

Equal parts technologist, strategist, and storyteller, Julian turns deep security expertise into clear, actionable guidance that resonates with developers, security leaders, and executives alike.

Julian Wood is a Principal Developer Advocate at AWS helping developers and builders discover how serverless architectures can transform the way they build and run applications at any scale. He loves exploring how things work and bringing technical topics to life so developers can apply them in their own applications. He also spends time learning from developers in the community and bringing their real-world insights back to AWS product teams to make the best products and services.

Juraci Paixão Kröhling is a software engineer at OllyGarden, a maintainer of the OpenTelemetry project, a member of the project's governing board and CNCF Ambassador. He has presented about distributed tracing, OpenTelemetry, and other related topics at conferences like KubeCon, OpenSource Summit, FOSDEM, and a few DevOpsDays, among others. Previously, he was also a maintainer of both the OpenTracing and Jaeger projects, having also delivered talks about those projects.

Jürgen Hartwig has been a member of the Board of Management of Daimler Truck Holding AG since December 2021 and is responsible for the Human Resources division. 

Since June 2022, he has also served as Labor Director of Daimler Truck Holding AG, and since 2019 he has been a member of the Board of Management and Labor Director of Daimler Truck AG.

Before taking over the leadership of the Human Resources department for Daimler Trucks within Daimler AG, Jürgen Hartwig spent three years in Beijing as Director Human Resources Greater China. Prior to that, he held various positions in the areas of Human Resources, Corporate Organization & Change Management, as well as Process
Consulting & Organization. 

He began his professional career in 1993 at the former Daimler-Benz AG in Gaggenau in the field of Human Resources & Organizational Development, after completing his studies in Business Education at the University of Mannheim.

Justin Reock is the Deputy CTO of DX (getdx.com) and is an engineer, speaker, writer, and software practice evangelist with over 20 years of experience working in various software roles. He is an outspoken thought leader, delivering enterprise solutions, numerous keynotes, technical leadership, various publications, and community education on developer experience and productivity.

Jyoti Bansal is a multi-unicorn founder and serial technology entrepreneur. He is the founder and CEO of Harness, the $5.5B AI Software Delivery Platform automating everything after code — testing, deployment, security, and compliance — for the largest enterprises in the world. Previously, he founded AppDynamics (acquired by Cisco for $3.7B), launched startup studio BIG Labs, and co-founded VC firm Unusual Ventures. He has been recognized as EY Entrepreneur of the Year, Forbes' Best Cloud Computing CEO, SiliconANGLE's Most Innovative Tech Startup Leader, and ranked No. 24 on Fortune's Most Innovative Companies list. He holds a BS in Computer Science from IIT Delhi and over 25 U.S. patents.

Software Developer at cycos AG (an Atos company) since 2001, specializing in real-time media streaming, web conferencing, and IVR solutions. Shifted focus to Applied AI in 2017, working on research projects in the fields of Natural Language Processing and Deep Learning. Creator of `kpe/bert-for-tf2`, one of the pioneering implementations of the Transformer architecture for TensorFlow 2, dedicated to bridging the gap between cutting-edge AI research and practical software engineering.

Dr. Karin Krobath is founding partner of identifire® – Austria's first agency for Employer Branding and Corporate Culture. Her focus for 20 years: start from the inside. She turns brands into movements, employees into ambassadors, and "yeah-whatever" sayers into genuine fans. With sharp positioning and strong formats – including Learning Journeys for innovation enthusiasts to Nairobi and Kigali.
identifire.at – 20 years of corporate culture. Now also prompt.
Germany's Federal Minister for Digital Transformation and Government Modernisation
speakers.company_position: Federal Minister for Digital Transformation and Government Modernisation

speakers.bio: Katharina Schneeberg is Head of Innovation & Technology at the German Association for the digital economy (BVDW). In this role, she is deeply engaged in developments and trends in emerging and future-oriented technologies. Alongside leading and coordinating projects and initiatives, she also acts as a source of inspiration and a technology expert for the digital economy, the association, and its members. In addition, she regularly serves as a moderator and speaker at industry events and conferences.

Kent Hviid Frederiksen. VP Rewards at the LEGO Group 
Kent is currently Vice President, Reward at The LEGO Group and brings over two decades of global experience in Reward having navigated the complexities of multinational corporations. Throughout his career, his focus has been on creating awesome value propositions aligned with company strategy, purpose and values and adaptation to the evolving employee and diverse employment landscape. Responsibilities include Well-Being, hybrid working, Mobility, Executive Reward, Business Travel, Performance Management, STI/LTIP and overall ability to attract & retain talent.

At Deutsche Bahn AG, Kerstin Wagner serves as Executive Vice President of Talent Acquisition, reporting directly to the Chief Human Resources Officer. In this role, she consolidates recruiting, employer branding, global governance, and temporary employment under one roof. Her primary objective is to position DB as a top employer and attract talent to the DB Group.

Working with her team, she oversees the recruitment of a workforce equivalent to the size of a small town every year, while ensuring the talent acquisition process remains innovative and modern. Kerstin Wagner is a member of several supervisory boards and chairs the Advisory Board of the Deutsche Bahn Foundation. Additionally, she is a dedicated advocate for New Work, technology, and diversity.

In 2021, she was named one of the "100 Women of the Year" by Focus magazine, and in 2024, Personalmagazin recognized her as one of the 50 most influential HR thought leaders.

Kesha is an engineer with over 20 years of experience in making customers happy through building product, running distributed systems, tuning socio-technical systems, and more recently building with AI. Kesha is a Principal Engineer at Intercom/Fin building the future of AI-centric customer service.

Kiran is an AI & APPS Solution Engineer at Microsoft, working with enterprise customers, especially in the automotive industry EMEA to design and scale AI-driven applications. She focuses on translating complex business challenges into practical, production-ready solutions using Microsoft Foundry, AI Services , frameworks and modern agent architectures.

With deep hands-on experience across architecture design, workshops, and real-world deployments, she operates at the intersection of business and engineering bridging the gap between strategy and execution without losing pace on either side.

When she isn’t debugging runtime telemetry or configuring Foundry Agents, Kiran channels her focus into martial arts, music, and canvas art. She tackles rogue bugs and heavy enterprise scale with the same grit, curiosity, and zero-sugarcoating mindset she used to navigate moving across the globe from India to Germany.

Co-founder of OtterlyAI — the AI search monitoring & GEO platform. Previously Head of Growth at Usersnap (acquired by saas.group), has.to.be ($300M exit), Wikidocs (acquired by Atlassian) and Gentics (acquired by APA). Building the category for how brands show up in ChatGPT, Perplexity & Google AI Overviews.

Krasimir Tsonev is a coder with over ten years of experience in web development. Author of books on Node.js and React he works at Antidote.me where he helps people reaching clinical trials. Loves React and its ecosystem.

Hi, I’m Kristiyan Velkov, and I’m excited to be here! I’ve been a front-end developer for over a decade, specializing in React.js, Next.js, and modern JavaScript frameworks. I’m passionate about building scalable and efficient solutions and sharing knowledge with the tech community through writing, speaking, and mentoring.

I’m an active Medium author with nearly 6,000 followers in the tech niche and 30,000 connections on LinkedIn. I hold multiple professional certificates and have authored books, including Core TypeScript Utility Types and the upcoming Mastering React JS Interviews. My work focuses on topics like TypeScript utility types, SOLID principles, and React.js best practices.

Beyond development, I enjoy organizing workshops and contributing to the growth of developers worldwide. It’s a privilege to share my journey and ideas with you, and I’m looking forward to connecting and learning together.

My blog: https://medium.com/@kristiyanvelkov
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/kristiyan-velkov-763130b3/

Kristjan Ulst leads Developer Productivity and AI adoption at Arrive, where his team supports 800+ engineers across 90+ countries. He builds the platforms, tooling, and AI workflows that help teams ship faster, safer, and with less friction.

His career sits at the intersection of AI and engineering productivity. At MindTitan, he led teams that shipped AI products for telecom, government, and education. At Arrive, he applies that experience at scale, driving AI coding tool adoption across the engineering org, from strategy and enablement to guardrails and measurement.

Before that, he co-founded two startups (Fanvestory, acquired, and Fairmus) and ran a CTO-as-a-service practice for over a decade. He has built products from zero to one and scaled engineering cultures from startup to enterprise.

What he cares about: making engineers' lives easier, building teams with real ownership and autonomy, and treating AI adoption as a culture change, not a tool rollout.

Krzysztof is a photographer, globetrotter, and public speaker. He works as a Staff Research Engineer at [GitHub Next](https://next.github.com/), where he designs and builds the next generation of developer tools such as GitHub Copilot, NES or [REDACTED]

After the work, Krzysztof is an open-source software maintainer passionate about functional programming (especially F#), editor tooling and developer experience - he has created and maintained several OSS projects varying from [language servers](https://github.com/fsharp/FsAutoComplete) to [web frameworks](https://github.com/SaturnFramework/Saturn) to [VSCode plugins](https://github.com/ionide/ionide-vscode-fsharp).

Additionally, he is a passionate public speaker. You can meet him at many conferences around the world, where he is always trying to meet new people and learn how to improve software developers' lives.

Lars Langerbein joined the AI Infrastructure Team at Finanz Informatik as ML Ops Engineer in 2025. Previously, he implemented a Model Registry and Model Monitoring Framework. Currently he designs and builds an Open Lakehouse.

Lars Rogge has been a member of the Executive Board of Generali Deutschland AG as Chief Operating Officer (COO) since October 1, 2025. In this role, he is responsible for operational management, the further development of IT and business processes, and key transformation initiatives—with a particular focus on digitalization and the use of artificial intelligence.

Rogge brings more than 20 years of international management experience in the insurance and financial services sector. Before joining Generali Deutschland, he served as COO and a member of the Executive Board at Allianz Partners in Paris, where he was globally responsible for operations, claims and all respective transformation programs and advanced the modernization of IT and process landscapes.

Previously, as Global Head of Operations Travel, Assistance & Digital, he led large international units with several thousand employees and consistently drove digital transformation and efficiency improvements. Other stages of his career include senior roles at Allianz Deutschland AG and Allianz SE, as well as at Deutsche Börse AG and the strategy consultancy Roland Berger, where he gained extensive experience in strategy, transformation, and process optimization in a global environment.

Lars Rogge studied business administration at Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich and the University of Passau, graduating with a degree in business administration.

As COO of Generali Deutschland AG, he is particularly focused on driving the integration of innovative technologies into day-to-day operations. He emphasizes the targeted use of AI to boost customer focus, efficiency and innovation and aiming to provide the best possible support to employees while creating sustainable added value for customers.

Laura Tacho is a subject matter expert in measuring the efficiency of engineering organizations. Currently, she is a Senior Principal Technologist at AWS focusing on developer experience and AI-native development. Laura was previously the CTO of DX, where she led the company’s executive advisory and research programs. She is the co-author of the Core 4 Framework of Developer Productivity, used by companies like LEGO, Netflix, Airbnb, Pinterest, and Snowflake to measure and improve developer experience. In 2025, she was awarded Austrian Innovator of the Year. Laura’s been working on developer tools for 15 years, including building out some of the first developer tools for the Docker ecosystem.

Leandro Gomes da Silva is the founder of The AI Laundry and Head of AI Operations at a European recruitment firm, where he built a talent intelligence platform for candidate outreach, job descriptions, and interview optimization. No engineering team. No enterprise software contract.

His AI outreach assistant doubled candidate response rates for a 3-person team. He works with HR and recruitment teams across Europe to fix broken processes and build AI tools they run themselves.

He speaks on AI implementation for non-technical teams, vibe coding, and the future of talent operations.

Returning speaker at WeAreDevelopers World Congress, with previous stages including HREX Vienna, TNW, and Recruiting Brainfood.

He's the person to find if your team has tried AI and it isn't sticking.

Lena Hall is an expert in practical AI adoption, data engineering, cloud and pragmatic architecture, driving strategic AI integration at scale combined with extensive experience leading large, high-performing technical teams. She is the Founder and CEO of Droid AI, where she helps businesses get real results from AI, defining Data +  AI strategies, integrating LLMs into complex systems, connecting AI solutions with custom data and business tools, and optimizing outcomes through proven and innovative architectures. Lena has 15+ years of deeply technical background as a solution architect, technical leader in large-scale data, analytics, machine learning, and cloud computing. Prior to Droid AI, Lena worked as Head of Developer Experience, North America at Amazon Web Services and led Big Data DevRel at Microsoft. She frequently shares practical knowledge on her YouTube channel (youtube.com/@lena-hall) and at industry conferences as an international keynote speaker.

Hi, my name is Leszek Włodarski, you can find more about me at https://www.linkedin.com/in/leszekwlodarski/. 

I am passionate enigineering manager/director, still with hands-on experience. I feel that proper architecture, efficient SSDLC pipeline and development processes are curcial to make our developers life better. I am not afraid of big things and challanges. 23 years of work in one company makes my experience and perspective on Software Development unique or simply special.

Lilia Gargouri stands for sustainable, scalable, and efficient quality assurance in complex eGovernment projects. She is committed to accessibility, trains career changers, and passionately advocates for greater visibility and equal opportunities for women in IT. As the inventor of a model-based, low-code test automation tool, she has significantly simplified and accelerated quality assurance in critical eGovernment projects. A member of the German Testing Board, she actively drives the development of industry quality standards.
speakers.company_position: Board Member and Head of QA

speakers.bio: Linda is a Strategic Advisor specializing in the field of enterprise innovation. She is co-founder and MD at Experience One, a digital agency focusing on innovative technologies for designing and scaling new and better CX for companies like Mercedes-Benz, BOSCH, AOK and FI. She focuses on strategic consulting and positioning of changemakers as well as in the implementation of customer centric change programs to create a sustainable culture of innovation. She benefits from her many years of experience in rollout and architecture consulting for the major digital platforms of large enterprises.

Lindsey is the Global User Growth & Product Partnerships Lead at TikTok. In her capacity as Lead, Lindsey is responsible for innovating and scaling the TikTok app and product features through partnerships with the company's most strategic technology, telecommunications, and content partners. Lindsey has worked for TikTok across the New York and London offices.

Before joining TikTok, Lindsey was based in NYC and led multi-billion dollar fundraising campaigns, growth strategy, and product management for the $420 billion Alternative Investments platform within the $14 trillion AUM asset manager, BlackRock.

Lindsey received a MBA from The Wharton School at the University of Pennsylvania and a BA from Vanderbilt University. Lindsey is an avid squash and padel player and skier and enjoys traveling around the globe.

Liran Hason is VP of AI at Coralogix, where he leads the company’s AI strategy, architecture and execution across autonomous agents and enterprise AI. A builder, founder, and engineering leader with more than 20 years of experience, he has spent much of his career making AI systems more reliable, responsible, and ready for the real world. Before Coralogix, he founded and served as CEO of Aporia, a pioneer in AI observability and guardrails, later acquired by Coralogix, and earlier was an AI Architect at Adallom, acquired by Microsoft.

Lisa Becker is a Solutions Engineer at GitHub, where she helps corporate customers across EMEA evaluate, adopt, and get the most out of the GitHub platform. She specializes in translating real-world technical challenges into clear solutions, partnering with sales, product, and engineering teams to drive platform adoption at scale.

Her background is in machine learning and applied AI. She holds a master’s degree in Cognitive Systems from the University of Potsdam, with hands-on industry experience in natural language processing, large language models, and recommendation systems. This technical foundation gives her a unique perspective on the increasingly AI-driven conversations shaping how organizations build software today.

Alongside her work at GitHub, Lisa develops on-demand courses and facilitates live webinars on NLP and LLMs for O’Reilly Media. She is a Google Women Techmakers Ambassador, a certified #IAmRemarkable facilitator, and a passionate advocate for diversity, equity, and inclusion in tech.

Loredana Crisan is the Chief Design Officer at Figma, where she leads design, UX writing, brand studio and research. Prior to joining Figma, she spent nine years at Meta, where she held product and design leadership roles across Messenger, Instagram and GenAI initiatives. Before Loredana’s career in product and design, she was a musician and sound engineer.

Lorenzo is a Consultant and Fullstack Developer at codecentric, currently working with TypeScript, Angular, and Kotlin. He’s also tracking trends like Large Language Models and enjoys diving into new fields, with a particular interest in the future of Quantum Computing.

Over the past decade, I've architected production AI/ML systems processing 
100M+ daily events through event-driven serverless architectures. AWS Serverless 
Hero and Cursor Ambassador focused on cost-optimized AI infrastructure and real-time data pipelines.

Production track record:
- Built production AI Agents for legal domains, managing very long contexts, 
  planning, and dynamic loading through event-driven orchestration
- Built production GenAI platform serving 500K+ requests/day with <200ms p95 latency
- Scaled cloud SaaS to 10M+ users, reducing p99 latency from 2s to 1.2s through 
  event-driven architecture migration  
- Migrated 80% of workloads to serverless event-driven patterns, cutting 
  infrastructure costs 10x while improving deployment velocity

Led engineering teams of 100+ through cultural transformation toward continuous 
delivery and experimentation-driven development. Co-founder of the Serverless Italy 
community, Milano Mongo User Group, and Cursor Meetup Milano.

Luca Trușcă is a Software Engineer at Adobe, building core font experiences for Adobe Express. He specializes in web performance, developer tooling, and bridging the gap between frontend and backend teams. Before joining Adobe, he was an indie game developer whose work reached over 400,000 players. He is passionate about sharing knowledge and believes that lifting up the whole team is the only way to build great software.

Lucía Conde-Moreno is Head of the AI Research Center at Info Support, where she also works as a consultant software engineer specializing in data and AI applications. She is known as a Jack Of All Trades by her colleagues, having worked in varied roles ranging from .NET or Java developer to data scientist or machine learning engineer. She has worked for different national and international clients, in diverse fields such as finance, health care, energy, or education. She is part of the AI Champions chapter for promoting AI-augmented engineering tools, and she is responsible for supervising internal research in subfields of AI like explainability or computer vision. When she is not working, she is busy switching across random hobbies, from filmmaking to DJing. She holds a MSc in Computer Science, and a BSc in Telecommunications Engineering.

Luis is the SVP of the Copilot Agents Product, focused on making GitHub the place where humans and agents collaborate to develop software. With a career spanning leadership roles at Microsoft, Reddit, Rappi and Loft, he has a proven track record of scaling high-growth engineering, product, and data organizations. A lifelong builder, Luis started programming when he was eight years old and today also serves on the Board of Trustees for the Wikimedia Foundation.

Luisa Rink is a Product Engineer in ING where she plays a key role in shaping customer‑centric digital products. With a strong foundation in change projects and cross‑functional collaboration, she helps teams move beyond feature delivery toward creating meaningful customer impact.

Luise is an Azure & Power Platform Architect based in Germany. She is an awarded Microsoft Most Valuable Professional working with organizations that want to move beyond digital theatre. Her focus is on AI-enabled solutions that are designed to last in real-world environments. She helps teams build accessible apps, robust data models, and automation that respects governance, compliance, and human effort. A recurring theme in her work is making the invisible visible: unmeasured work, emotional load, operational friction, and the real cost of “quick wins”. Her motto "Changing the world one app at a time" stems from her extensive work with non-profit organizations.
Alongside client work, Luise writes and speaks about productivity myths, pseudo-agile practices, AI readiness, and why many digital initiatives fail long before technology becomes the problem. She is known for combining clear technical guidance with a sharp eye for organizational patterns; and for asking the uncomfortable questions others tend to avoid.

When not working and traveling, she is a runner, a mother of (teenage) dragons, a LEGO-builder and a lover of the number 42. Her favorite color is #ff69b4.

Luke is a Developer Relations Engineer at Hashgraph, working directly with projects building on Hedera to make sure they have the tools, guidance, and support they need to build. Luke’s day-to-day is spent alongside builders: unblocking integration issues, feeding their requirements back into our SDKs and docs, and connecting them with the right people across the ecosystem. He has been working in DLT since 2020, across multiple projects tokenising real-world and financial assets, which keeps him close to the practical challenges teams hit when value starts moving on-chain.

Lutz is an independent consultant working at the intersection of software architecture and organizational design, a frequent conference speaker, and a blogger. Until recently, he was Head of Engineering at Upvest, a Berlin-based FinTech company that makes investing accessible to everyone. Previously, he led engineering teams building software for container shipping, online retail, financial institutions, and even cruise ship entertainment systems. His current focus is on event-driven architecture, highly available and scalable systems, and strategic domain-driven design.
speakers.company_position: Fractional CTO, Interim Manager and Architect

speakers.bio:
Mahmoud is a Talent Acquisition expert with over a decade of experience recruiting for some of the world's most recognizable brands, including Nike, Semrush, Samsung, and PepsiCo.

Throughout his career, Mahmoud has taken on several Talent Acquisition roles, from Tech Recruiting, Talent Sourcing, Executive hiring, to Talent Ops.
He currently leads global Go-To-Market Recruitment at Semrush, and previously led the Data & AI business at Nike.
He's also the Founder of Egypt Recruiting Community, the first and largest network in Egypt for the People & Culture space.

Mahmoud is an international keynote speaker who has delivered keynotes at The Sourcing Summit, HR Leaders Summit, and The Recruitment Conference, among others.

Mahran Meißner is Head of the Technology & Innovation Space at Siemens IT, where he focuses on turning emerging technologies into business value. His work spans AI agents, software engineering, humanoid robots, XR, and digital twins, with a strong emphasis on making advanced technologies accessible across the enterprise. He is the creator of CREATE, an AI platform that enables anyone to transform ideas into production-ready software. Mahran is a frequent speaker on enterprise AI, innovation, and the future of software development, sharing practical insights from building AI-powered products inside a global organization.
speakers.company_position: Head of Technology and Innovation Space

speakers.bio:
Maish Saidel-Keesing is a Senior Developer Advocate at AWS.

He's been working in tech for over 20 years, and if there's one thing that's been consistent throughout his career, it's this: if something can be automated, it should be. He's an automation junkie. Always has been.

He helps developers build in the cloud. Not by talking at them, but by building with them. He live-codes at meetups. He writes. He speaks. He creates. He's got a blog with over 700 posts because he genuinely believes that when you share what you've learned, everybody wins.

What gets him excited right now is the intersection of GenAI and developer workflows. Not the hype. The practical stuff. How do you actually use these tools to ship faster, automate your infrastructure, and focus on the code that matters? That's what he helps people figure out.

He works with everyone from students writing their first lines of code to senior engineers architecting complex distributed systems. Dev communities. Academic institutions. Because making cutting-edge tools accessible to everyone is the whole point.

His mission is simple. Equip developers with the knowledge, tools, and confidence to build anything they can imagine in the cloud.

Malte Lantin is a Strategic Solutions Engineer at GitHub, where he helps large enterprises adopt modern software engineering practices, AI-powered developer workflows, and platform engineering at scale. Malte is based in Germany and brings more than 15 years of experience spanning software development, technical evangelism, program management, and solutions engineering. He is also the co-host of the German software engineering podcast TodoCast, where he discusses developer productivity, emerging technologies, and industry trends.

Malte Lensch started using Lovable and Claude in spring 2025, and it changed his life. What began as an experiment to rebuild his own consulting website turned into a production-grade workflow now running for multiple clients.

Mainly helping companies now kill their WordPress and Webflow setups and replace them with modern AI-native stacks that actually ship.

He's Managing Director at Till Freitag Consulting, a Berlin-based consultancy specialising in AI-native workflows and automation. In his spare time, he is spending more and more time in the garden.

Malte Ubl is the CTO of Vercel, where he leads the development and delivery of the company’s suite of tools and features to enable developers to create at the moment of inspiration. Prior to joining Vercel, Malte was the Principal Engineer for Google Search Rendering and Engineering Director for Google’s Search on Laptops, Tablets, and Desktop. Malte has also created the frontend infrastructure for a number of Google Web Apps and the web at large. He is also the founder and curator of JSConf EU.

Principal Cloud Native architect and developer with a passion for integration and automation. But wait, it's more! Passion does not only have to be in tech! There is also a burning passion for neurodiversity and diversity in tech, across ages, genders, identities, etc... 

Loves integration, automation and making processes more streamlined. A back-end developer for over 20 years. Have worked with building integration platforms in the cloud for several bigger organizations.  Worked with .Net since early 2000, and Azure since launch. 

Have also been a team lead, worked with process streamlining, enterprise architecture and everything between that and made my hands dirty as a developer and cloud native architect.

Trainer and Consultant with focus on Angular. Google Developer Expert (GDE) for Angular and Trusted Collaborator in the Angular team. Writes for O'Reilly, Hanser and the German Java Magazine. Regularly speaks at conferences.

Recognised as Global Top 200 Biggest Voices in Leadership alongside names such as Simon Sinek and Marshal Goldsmith by Leaders Hum, Match.io etc. 
- AI Advisory Board Member for Goodtime.io
- Keynote Speaker, Author and StartUp advisor.
-Mastermind of Global Talent Dynamics with a proven prowess of pivoting orgs to success by building a culture of high performance and engagement.
- Rich International experience in Talent, TA, TD, TM, HR Business Partnering and HR Transformation!!
-Leadership Experience, built & nutured high performing HR / Talent teams of 40+ to 140+ globally.

Manuel is an OSS Software Engineer at Docker where he works in the Clients & SDKs team, also maintaining "Testcontainers for Go". Since 2003, he has held various roles in different parts of the development process, starting in 2003 working for the regional public administration in Castilla-La Mancha (Spain) until 2007, then worked at more traditional consulting firms. In 2011, he transitioned to product-oriented and Open Source companies, where he has served as a support engineer, trainer, and Core Engineer at Liferay, as QA Tech lead at Liferay Cloud. From 2019 to 2022 he was involved in Engineering Productivity at Elastic as part of the Observability product, and since 2022 doing pure OSS at AtomicJar, which was acquired by Docker in Dec' 23. In every job he tries to improve the quality of the software products and processes from the automation and testing point of view.

He has also founded and managed a couple of small web development and systems consulting companies. Additionally, he organises the Google Developers Group in Toledo, Spain (GDG Toledo), where they run monthly discussions about software in its various aspects, serving as a small community outside the bustling Madrid. Manuel has delivered talks at different national and international events too.

Manuel holds a BS in Computer Science (UNED Spain), and a master degree in Research in Software Engineering and Information Systems (UNED Spain). You can find him on Internet as "mdelapenya" everywhere.

Manuel Weichselbaum is co-founder and CTO of anyconcept, a research company building a world model for software interfaces. His background spans physics, telematics, and computer science, and he has spent over a decade doing AI research before that was the obvious thing to do. In 2020 he began exploring a question that the industry had largely ignored: what if a model could learn to understand software purely through visual interaction data, the way a human does, without ever reading a line of code. That question became the Vision Action Model, a domain-specific foundation model trained on pixels, mouse, and keyboard data alone. Manuel leads all VAM research and architecture at anyconcept. GHOSTS, the first product built on the VAM, is already running in production for enterprise customers. He is based in Graz, Austria.

As TomTom’s SVP Product Engineering, Manuela leadsTomTom's efforts to create game-changing location products that integrate our technical offerings and serve a wide range of customer use cases. 
With over 25 years of experience in software and product roles, Manuela has led organizations through transformative growth, innovation, and operational excellence at companies such as Microsoft, the World Economic Forum, and Amazon. 
She holds Master’s degrees in Software Engineering from the Politecnico of Milano, and in Business Administration from the University of Washington.

Mara is a multidisciplinary professional with a background in both the natural sciences as well as the technology industry. She has a passion for helping others to tackle challenging problems and build elegant solutions and is an active advocate for innovation and open source.

Marc Arndt has over 20 years of corporate development experiance, primarily focusing on Go and Kubernetes over the last 8 years. 

Currently the technical Leader at Evana AG, Marc Arndt has overseen the digital transformation of the organisation and brought the company forward into an AI 2.0 world.

Marc Plogas discovered his passion for technology at the age of six when an Atari sparked his interest in programming. He holds a Master's in Computer Science and spent nearly a decade freelancing as a software, mobile, and test engineer, as well as a solution architect, before joining Microsoft. At Microsoft, he played an instrumental role in pioneering technologies such as Windows Mixed Reality, Desktop Bridge, and WinUI 3, and later advised startups worldwide on optimizing their cloud architectures with a focus on reliability, scalability, cost efficiency, and security. Now at RoboTwin, Marc builds reliable cloud and edge IoT backbones for no‑code robot teaching and continues to deliver talks and workshops on IoT, AI, and cloud architecture.

Marc specializes in bridging the gap between technical execution and executive vision by "speaking AI" to everyone from developers to C-suite leaders. Driven by a deep curiosity and a passion for emerging technology, Marc excels at uniting enthusiastic teams to uncover the full potential of artificial intelligence. Outside of his professional life, Marc loves pushing his limits by signing up for intense sports events (even if he occasionally questions that decision the next morning).

Marcin Makowski is CEO at BeOne and a software architect focused on building production-grade AI and orchestration platforms. With over 20 years of experience in distributed systems, enterprise automation, and large-scale process execution, he specializes in designing deterministic architectures in non-deterministic environments.
Marcin works at the intersection of AI infrastructure, workflow orchestration, and decision modeling. His recent focus includes LLM platform engineering, model gateways, hybrid retrieval architectures, and building reproducible AI systems with strong observability and auditability guarantees.
He is a strong advocate of open ecosystems and engineering-first approaches to AI adoption. Instead of treating AI as a feature, he designs it as a runtime component that must meet the same standards of reliability, scalability, and traceability as any other production system.
Marcin co-authored research on dynamic decision model generation for tax compliance (accepted at ISD 2025) and regularly speaks about distributed systems, AI platform design, and deterministic orchestration in intelligent systems.

Marcin is an Engineering Manager with over a decade of experience spanning small startups to big enterprises. Having served in multiple roles - from Senior Engineer to Tech Lead and Manager - he treats his career as a laboratory for leadership and philosophy.

Most recently, Marcin navigated a unique "Pendulum" career arc: transitioning from management roles in large organizations (like William Hill and NetEnt) to a Staff Engineer role at Qonto, before returning to leadership. He uses this cross-functional experience to help teams bridge the gap between backend infrastructure and organizational strategy, advocating for "Radical Career Ownership" along the way.

Marcin Wawryszczuk, PhD, is Head of AI at Andersen and a seasoned AI strategist, solution architect, and IT project manager with 15 years of industry expertise. He has delivered over 50 successful projects for leading financial institutions across financial markets, real estate, and tech startups.

Marcin serves as an assistant professor of AI research and advisor to national AI bodies, driving generative AI adoption, digital transformation, and enterprise AI strategy. He leads transformational and strategic initiatives where senior stakeholder management, vendor management, and technology intersect.

I am a solution architect at NVIDIA, where I guide clients through integrating our technologies to help them fully embrace the AI revolution. I graduated with a master’s degree in Data Science and Engineering from Politecnico di Torino.

Marco Sussitz is an engineer at Dynatrace who lives at the boundary of Java and C++. He writes JVMTI agents, instruments bytecode, and digs into JIT, memory, and thread behavior. You’ll often find him sorting out class loaders or, reading the spec longer than he should. Before Dynatrace, he worked on cloud video encoding with FFmpeg. Off hours he’s bouldering or in the mountains.

Marcos Placona is the founder of DevRel Bridge, a Developer Relations consultancy that helps developer-first companies scale through strategy, content, and community building. With over 20 years in tech and 12+ years of DevRel leadership at companies like Twilio and Circle, he treats DevRel as a revenue-driving function, focusing on activation over awareness and delivering measurable business outcomes. 

Marcos also founded LinkIntel, a SaaS platform providing actionable LinkedIn analytics for content creators and marketers. His contrarian approach challenges industry norms and helps startups build developer adoption infrastructure that drives real growth.

I’m Marcus, a growth operator and former VC. Over the past decade, I’ve worked with fast scaling B2B software and fintech companies to turn strong traction into repeatable, structured growth. Today, I partner with high-growth teams as a hands-on consultant, interim executive and advisor, aligning marketing, partnerships, sales and success. I focus on building sustainable, defensible go-to-market & engines, while driving commercial and product excellence.

Marcus Ross is a Member of the Platform Engineering & Process Framework team. He's developing the next step in scalable telematics and healthcare digitalization by building platforms with Kubernetes, GitOps, and platform tooling that put the Golden Path ahead of cognitive load. He's also keen to turn AI into real value in his everyday work.

Marcus Wermuth is VP of R&D at Safety, building tools to give security teams visibility into developer workstations. Previously, he held engineering leadership roles at Remote and Buffer, two of the most recognized distributed companies globally. With 15 years of engineering experience, Marcus writes about technology leadership, mentors engineering leaders, and facilitates international workshops on distributed team management.

Experienced software engineer focused on AI and product development. Currently leading the development of AI solutions for global medical device manufacturers at Flinn. Prior to this, I worked with Apple Music's data engineering team, built a decision support SaaS used by large software organisations such as Apple and Move Inc, and led teams building solutions to monitor supply chain risks across millions of global data points at Prewave.

I'm passionate about the intersection of software engineering, data, and product, and at my best when leading teams that build data-centred digital products.

Mariana de la Roche Wills is an international lawyer and founder of BlackVogel and DAAvern, advising institutions, regulators, and Web3 companies on digital assets, DAOs, and trusted data infrastructure. She translates policy into implementation, designing technology-ethical and compliance frameworks, compliance-by-design roadmaps, and cross-border adoption plans. Mariana convenes expert roundtables globally, bringing together industry leaders and authorities to advance regulatory clarity and institutional adoption. Her focus spans MiCA, AML/CFT, DAO governance, digital identity, and AI×Web3 convergence across the global level with particular focus on Europe and Latin America.

Marie Theresa has spent the past decade helping developers thrive by bridging the gap between technology and people — drawing on her background in both education and computer science. 

She has a proven track record of making complex tools feel usable and human, and now works with some of the largest enterprise customers in EMEA to make AI a true peer programmer: one that supports developers everywhere. 

Marie once helped run a cybersecurity book club, believes good documentation is a form of kindness, and rarely leaves the house without a paperback (or two) in tow.

Marina is a Developer Relations Engineer at Google, focused on building native Android apps. Her mission is to help developers excel in building and growing their apps using Firebase, GCP and our suite of AI tools! Before joining the Firebase team in 2022, Marina worked as a software engineer for 8 years, 5 of which were dedicated to Android app development. She loves all things Jetpack Compose, Firebase and AI!

Mario is a Software Engineer on the Edge Services team at Twilio, where he works on building the company’s high-volume, public-facing edge infrastructure. Previously he worked at EPAM, where he developed large-scale media software for several of the biggest European tier-1 TV operators. Specializing in Infrastructure and Platform Engineering, he focuses on creating developer platforms that help internal customers ship software at a higher pace, while also ensuring a reliable, high-performance experience for the end users relying on those services.

Marius' passion is exploring the latest communication technologies – and, even more, sharing his knowledge and learning from others. Originally from the Black Forest in Germany, he started his software development career as a UI developer in Silicon Valley, where he discovered his love for web technologies, especially JavaScript and the entire Node.js ecosystem.

After countless creative (and sometimes frustrating) battles with CSS, he relocated to Munich, where he now works as a Developer Evangelist at Twilio. In this role, he inspires and empowers developers around the world to build cloud-native apps with outstanding customer engagement. He has also spent nearly a decade working on enterprise frontend development and even wrote a book on the topic.

Whether on stage, in workshops, or through hands-on prototypes, Marius loves exchanging ideas with fellow developers and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. When he’s not doing that, he dives into the world of Web 3.0 – always on the lookout for the next exciting technology.

As CEO of Trenkwalder Group AG, Mark had the opportunity to lead the development of an innovative solution for automating recruitment. His focus was on leveraging technology to optimize and enhance the recruitment process, ensure to remain industry leaders and offer the best possible service to their clients. In addition, Mark and his team successfully built an outsourced customer service solution in which transactions are handled by AI agents. With revenues of €550 million and 16,000 employees across 15 countries, they are proud to deliver first-class services to their clients worldwide.

Markus Blume has served as Bavarian State Minister for Science and the Arts since February 2022. Prior to this, he was Secretary General of the CSU from 2018 to 2022. He has been a member of the Bavarian State Parliament since 2008, representing the Munich-Ramersdorf constituency as a directly elected representative.

A father of three, he lives in Munich. Before entering politics, Blume worked as a founder and board member of a medium-sized enterprise.
speakers.company_position: Bavarian State Minister for Science and the Arts

speakers.bio: I help Java teams build AI-ready, cloud-native systems that are fast to change and boring to operate. For more than 20 years I have worked with large enterprises on modernizing Java platforms. From heavy monoliths to Quarkus-based, container-first architectures. Today I focus on how Java, open source, and local AI models come together in real systems, not demos. I am a Java Champion, author, speaker, and developer strategist at IBM. I write the Enterprise Java and Quarkus Substack, The Main Thread, where I publish deep, hands-on tutorials.

Markus leads the Enterprise Business for NVIDIA in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland. Based in Munich, he oversees the company’s go-to-market activities for enterprises in Central Europe, focusing on advancing AI, visualization, and virtualization technologies.
Markus’ career began in Microsoft’s Central European Data Centeroperations. Before joining NVIDIA in 2019, he held leadership roles at Akamai, Lionbridge, T-Systems, and Colt Technology Services. His two decades of experience in sales and business management have equipped him with deep expertise in the IT industry's evolving needs.
Markus is committed to fostering innovation and making valuable contributions to the field of AI. At NVIDIA, Markus leverages his knowledge to help businesses achieve their AI and industrial digitalization goals.

Markus Hofmarcher develops AI workflows at ExtensityAI using autonomous agents and modern language models, particularly for complex multi-stage processes.
Previously, he conducted research for several years at the Institute for Machine Learning at JKU Linz, where he focused extensively on deep learning and reinforcement learning.

Markus Rautert is Chief Technology Officer at adidas, where he leads global technology strategy across enterprise architecture, platform engineering, IT infrastructure, cybersecurity, and Data & AI. With nearly two decades at adidas, Markus has helped build the company’s digital backbone—scaling platforms, securing operations, and enabling data-driven decisions across brand, supply chain, and finance. His current focus is on strengthening cybersecurity and unlocking business value through advanced analytics. Markus speaks regularly at internal and industry events, connecting with developers and senior leaders alike. Offstage, he’s a lifelong gamer and passionate tech enthusiast.

Markus Stauffenberg is leadership coach, change management advisor, and entrepreneur with over 20 years of experience helping leaders to create impact in their organizations. As co-founder of Experience One, founder of Aseus, and the creator of the 'How to Pitch CX'-Community, he has assisted numerous companies in navigating successful digital and customer-centric transformations. As expert in effective change management and leadership communication, he is also a passionate mentor, having trained teams in startups, universities, and corporate settings.

Senior Solutions Architect with a background as a Software Developer in Advertising Technology, now working at Amazon Web Services helping customers build scalable, large-scale applications on AWS. Elixir Developer and Database Enthusiast.

Martin is CEO of Codemagic. Codemagic started as a CI/CD product for mobile developers and now offers and over-the-air (OTA) updates as well. Fun fact: Martin studied to become a chemist, but after graduation, he began his career in technology in 2017.

Martin Sakowski is a Solutions Architect at Amazon Web Services. He supports enterprise customers, particularly in the retail sector, on their digital transformation journeys. He has a strong background in building digital platforms and serverless stacks and specializes in helping organizations unlock the potential of Agentic AI and improve the Agent Experience (AX). With this experience he loves to dive deep into architectures and development processes to drive performance, operational efficiency, and increase the speed of innovation.

Martina Kraus has been active in web development since her early years and has become an expert in web security. As an Application Security Engineer, she focuses on integrating security best practices into all phases of software development. In her role as a Google Developer Expert (GDE) in Angular and Identity, she enjoys sharing her knowledge of Angular security at both national and international conferences. She is also currently writing a German book on authentication and authorization in web applications.

Mateusz Gren is Head of Software Development at Atos in Austria, where he leads large-scale software and AI initiatives with teams of several hundred engineers. With a background in Business Informatics and more than a decade of hands-on experience as a software architect, developer, and engineering leader, he operates at the intersection of architecture, execution, and business impact.

Beyond enterprise systems, he actively builds and operates his own AI-assisted applications using modern web stacks, applying production-grade thinking to personal productivity systems - building and evolving personal software systems for sports performance, finance, and decision-making using generative AI and minimal code. His talks focus on what actually works, what breaks, and how developers can stay in control while leveraging AI as a genuine multiplier rather than a buzzword.

Matheus Guimaraes (@techwithmatheus) is a software architect and technical educator focused on designing scalable, production-grade systems. With over 25 years in tech, he has worked as a developer, architect, CTO, and tech co-founder, helping organizations modernize legacy platforms and build reliable systems. Today, he shares practical architecture insights through talks, articles, videos, and demos. He currently works as a Senior Developer Advocate at AWS.

Matic Petrič studied theoretical physics at University of Ljubljana, Faculty of Mathematics in physics. 

During his studies, Petrič worked at T-Labs, the Deutsche Telekom Laboratories, where he derived a problem formulation for optimizing traffic flow in real time with Quantum Annealing.

After finishing his degree during the pandemic, he is now working as a Quantum Computing Researcher at Fraunhofer FOKUS, focusing on hybrid quantum-classical algorithms, and working on Eclipse Qrisp - a high level programming language he'll be presenting on stage together with Raphael Seidel during WAD'24.

Mats Kuuskemaa started his career as a diplomat for Estonia, working with trade and investment policy at the Estonian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (2011-14). He was Consul & Economic Officer at Estonian Embassy in New Delhi (2014-18) and Political Counsellor at Estonian Embassy in Beijing (2018-19).
