In 2018 Mats joined Government of Estonia’s e-Residency programme, initially as Head of Business Strategy and from 2022 as Country Director for Germany, Poland, Austria and Switzerland based in Berlin. On two occasions he has also been part of startup teams as Co-Founder and Advisor. As a hobby, he has occasionally taken up journalism.

A seasoned Presales Engineer with over 30 years of experience, specialising in Application Security (AppSec), Supply Chain, and Performance Engineering. Throughout a long-standing career, I have partnered with organisations to design and deliver secure, scalable, and high-performing solutions that address complex business and technical challenges. Known for bridging the gap between business needs and technical execution, I bring deep domain expertise, strategic insight, and a customer-focused approach to every engagement.

Matthias Kordt is involved in the planning and provision of the AI and data analytics platform for 50 m Sparkasse clients, and has been working for Finanz Informatik since 2019. The focus here is on automation. Matthias coordinates between the various players on the platform, is always keen to accelerate regulation through automation and enjoys advancing new technologies. A clear focus is the automation and monitoring of the container platform.
speakers.company_position: Team Lead LLM Ops Data and AI Platform

speakers.bio: Matthias Niehoff works as Head of Data and Data Architect for codecentric AG and supports customers in the design and implementation of data architectures. His focus is not so much on the ML model, but rather on the necessary infrastructure and organization to help data science projects succeed.

Mattias is a Principal Software Engineer at PingCAP, where he works on TiDB's SQL layer and currently on the optimizer's statistics management. He has worked with databases, and MySQL in particular, for more than 20 years. Before PingCAP he spent years at Booking.com working with MySQL at scale, and earlier worked on the MySQL Server code at MySQL AB, Sun, and Oracle. Table partitioning is a recurring theme across his career: he built native partitioning in InnoDB for MySQL, and global indexes for partitioned tables in TiDB.

Director Engineering Trade Republic, responsible for the Engineering, Data and Cyber Security organisation. 
Earlier CTO Bluestep Bank, VP Klarna, Executive VP Nordea, Chief Business Architect SEB, Chief Architect TeliaSonera

Max began his career at Bridgewater Associates before moving into startups, including co-founding a company in a second language. He’s built and scaled teams across marketing, operations, finance, engineering, and product, bringing a systems-level perspective to building complex organizations and tools. Outside of work, Max loves traveling, cooking, and extremely long walks through cities and nature.

Max works with Google Cloud customers to realize their AI ambitions. As the Director for Customer Engineering for AI in EMEA, he leads Google's teams of expert AI engineers in the region in deploying the Gemini model family, Gemini Enterprise and Vertex AI, as well as Google Cloud's AI infrastructure offerings. Besides his role at Google, Max is also the co-lead for the AI in Women's Health research group at TUM University Hospital in Munich, where he and his team work on improving treatment outcomes for patients with rare gynecological tumors through AI for clinical decision support systems and patient digital twins.

Maxim Schepelin has been an engineering leader in various industries, including Game Dev, e-commerce, and travel building teams capable of launching products from idea to world-scale. With over a decade of experience leading engineering teams, Maxim has mastered the art and science of management, as well as how to exit both Vim and Emacs.
Maxim continues exploring ways to build effective engineering teams that deliver value at a sustainable pace.

Maximilian Jesch is an AI Engineer and Developer Advocate at IBM, where he focuses on Agentic AI and the watsonx ecosystem. In his role, he helps developers and enterprises understand, build, and scale AI solutions, while also serving as a technical leader for IBM Bob. Beyond his work at IBM, Max is active in the broader AI community, sharing insights through open-source projects. His work reflects a blend of hands-on engineering, advocacy, and community leadership in modern AI development.

May Walter is the Co-Founder and CTO of Hud, where she builds Hud's Runtime Code Sensor that gives coding agents ground truth from production, so AI speed can reach real-world systems safely. Previously founding CTO at Santa and CTO at Bond (acquired by REEF Technology), she specializes in runtime internals and adversarial security, mentors engineering leaders, and speaks on building and operating agent-driven software in production

Mehdi is a senior software developer and system architect.  Since 2000, he has been responsible for the design, implementation, and development of several different systems in the field of office automation, CRM, healthcare management, and automation in industry and logistics solutions. Mehdi specifically works in the field of software development based on .NET technology. Mehdi is a staunch fan of object-oriented programming.  Since 2016, Mehdi has worked more or less with cloud computing systems.
speakers.company_position: Tech Lead and Lead Cloud Engineer

speakers.bio:
Mei Dent has an excellent track record as a technology executive in the software and IT industry. She started her career as a software developer at IBM and spent nearly 25 years in various product and engineering related leadership roles at Canadian software giant OpenText. As part of the senior leadership team of the company’s software development organization, she was responsible for global engineering practices and processes for security, performance, user experience, as well as reusable and shared services. In her most recent role as CTO at customer communications management and engagement software provider Doxim, Mei led the entire technology organization and scaled it to support the company’s growth.

Mei holds a Master in Computer Science from the Western University in Ontario.

Staff Software Engineer at EIDU, a social enterprise dedicated to improving learning outcomes for children in low-resource settings worldwide. Experienced in full-stack development, including on the Android platform. As an engineer, I'm passionate about functional programming, static typing and property-based testing. As a human, I am dedicated to improving learning outcomes and opportunities for children all around the world.

Hi, I’m Mert! I’m a Software Engineer based in Berlin. I’m a "user-driven" engineer at heart, which means I care just as much about the human at the other end of the screen as I do about clean, type-safe code.

Whether I’m closely working with other engineers or designers, my goal is always the same: making the experience faster, more inclusive, and a little more fun. Beyond the day job, I love writing about tech—from deep dives into designs to the joys of programming—and I built a browser-based mystery game to explore the limits of interactive storytelling. I'm a big believer in "learning by doing" and love a good live-coding challenge!

Michael “Monty” Widenius is the co-founder of MariaDB, a renowned advocate for the open source software movement and one of the original developers of MySQL, the predecessor to MariaDB. He is currently serving as the CTO at MariaDB Plc., and was previously co-founder at SkySQL, and the CTO of MySQL AB until its sale to Sun Microsystems (now Oracle). Monty was also the founder of TCX DataKonsult AB, a Swedish data warehousing company. He was awarded the Finnish Software Entrepreneur of the Year prize in 2003. In 2015, Monty was selected as one of the 100 most influential persons in the Finnish IT market. Monty studied at the Helsinki University of Technology and lives in Finland.

Michael is the President of SAP Business Technology Platform and Member of the Extended Board. His team prioritizes security, reliability, and next-generation user experience for SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). As SAP’s leading platform, SAP BTP offers the technical foundation essential for driving customer success and enabling Generative AI-powered applications. Additionally, Michael’s team is responsible for the success of core products such as SAP Build, SAP Integration Suite, SAP HANA Cloud and SAP BTP ABAP environment.

He joined SAP in 2006 as a Research Associate and has since advanced through various technical leadership roles, including Development Manager and Senior Vice President and Managing Director of SAP Labs Dresden. He has a strong passion in for software architecture and fosters close collaboration across all boundaries.

Michael holds a Ph.D. in computer science and a diploma in media computer science from Technische Universität Dresden. A father of four, Michael lives with his family in Dresden.
speakers.company_position: President SAP Business Technology Platform, Member of the Extended Board

speakers.bio: Michael Coté studies how large organizations improve how they build software to run and grow their business. His books Changing Mindsets, Monolithic Transformation, and The Business Bottleneck cover these topics. He’s been an industry analyst at RedMonk and 451 Research, done corporate strategy and M&A, and was a programmer. He also co-hosts several podcasts, including Software Defined Talk. His daily-ish newsletter is at newsletter.cote.io.

Michael Friedrich is a Principal Developer Advocate at GitLab, focusing on production-ready Agentic AI use cases and DevSecOps efficiency. His journey from Hardware/Software Systems Engineering to DNS, monitoring, and GitLab trainings fuels his embedded homelab research on practical AI implementations. Michael educates at industry events through tutorials and live coding. When not traveling, he builds LEGO models and explores new tastes and cultures.

Michael Kagan serves as a Chief Technology Officer (CTO) of NVIDIA since May 2020, following the company’s acquisition of Mellanox Technologies, the company which he co-founded.

With over 40 years in the tech industry, Michael’s career began at Intel, where he contributed to 80387 math co-processor design, led 8060XP processor architecture and managed Pentium MMX design team. At Mellanox he played a pivotal role in developing accelerated networking technologies that enhanced cloud data center efficiency.

Today, as Nvidia’s CTO, Michael is focused on architecting solutions for evolving demands of artificial intelligence, developing architecture of data center as a single unit of computing – the AI factory. Michael focuses on full-stack solution, emphasizing importance of software in conjunction with hardware, advocating for the future where AI computing is accessible across diverse platforms from mega-scale data centers to mobile devices.

Michael O'Toole is a Staff Engineer at Trade Republic. Over the past decade he's been building, breaking, and migrating data platforms across Europe and across the pong. When he's not obsessing over query performance, he plays in TwoFoxOut. He brings the same energy to both — loud, fast, and no unnecessary downtime.

Michele Zuccala is Senior Vice President of Engineering at Sysdig, leading the global engineering organization across open source R&D, runtime security, monitoring, AI, and core platform areas. He drives technical execution and innovation across both commercial and open source initiatives. Michele joined Sysdig to lead the open source team and helped scale Falco, guiding the CNCF runtime security project to graduation and broad industry adoption. He later unified open source and commercial development for runtime security before expanding his leadership across the wider engineering organization. Before Sysdig, he was CTO at ProntoPro and held leadership roles at several high-growth startups.

Miguel Toscano is a Data and AI Specialist @Google, focusing on architecting foundational data systems and enabling AI-Agentic applications for high-performance enterprise solutions. Bringing a strong background in building and operating mission-critical data infrastructure, he has a deep appreciation for the rigorous reliability and scale demanded by modern computing. He spends most of his time working on optimizing the data layer for large-scale GenAI systems and sharing practical strategies on how modern data architectures can fuel the future of AI.

Mihai Faur is Chief Information Officer at UiPath, where he leads enterprise technology and transformation initiatives focused on automation, AI, and operational excellence. With extensive experience across finance, operations, and technology leadership roles, including Controller, CAO, and CFO positions, he brings deep expertise in enterprise systems, finance transformation, RPA, technical accounting, and business operations. Mihai is passionate about helping organizations harness automation and AI to drive efficiency, innovation, and smarter decision-making.

﻿Mike Bursell is the Executive Director of the Confidential Computing Consortium. He is one of the co-founders of the Enarx project (https://enarx.dev), and was CEO and co-founder of Profian, a start-up based on Enarx.  He has previously served on the Governing Boards of the CCC and the Bytecode Alliance and currently holds advisory board roles with various start-ups.  Previous companies include Red Hat, Intel and Citrix, with roles in security, virtualisation and networking.

Mike is author of "Trust in Computer Systems and the Cloud", published by Wiley in 2021.

Michael Fynes is Head of Engineering at Wolt within Doordash.  A technical leader who focuses on getting teams to work smarter, not harder. Coaching teams to work together better wherever they are is his passion.  Has helped Wolt scale the fintech organisation from just a handful of people to its current size.  Working in a fast moving, rapidly changing environment in a global marketplace, solving problems in innovative ways that get the job done, in a scalable way.

Milan Todorović is an Apple Certified Trainer, Consultant, and Software Engineer with decades of experience in high-level developer training. A Swift expert since its inception, he serves as an associate lecturer at the Apple Authorized Training Centre in Serbia, specializing in machine learning, AR, and advanced software architecture.

With a background in Electrical Engineering, Milan has a long history of IT leadership, including heading Borland’s regional office (2001–2010). Since becoming a certified Apple trainer in 2017, he has been a regular speaker at the WeAreDevelopers world congresses in Vienna and Berlin, focusing on ML and IT education methodology.

As a consultant for major European companies and founder of Edulibris (Estonia), Milan currently focuses on leading engineering teams using advanced ALM techniques. His unique approach combines deep technical mentorship with real-world project delivery to solve the global talent shortage in high-end software engineering.

I’m a senior software engineer with expertise in mobile development, particularly React Native. I focus on building performant, scalable applications and a great user experience.

Beyond code, I’m passionate about community and mentorship. As co-founder of Women in Tech Niš, I organize events, mentor aspiring professionals, and advocate for diversity in tech. I’ve also spoken at international conferences and hosted large-scale events as an MC, combining technical knowledge with clear and engaging communication.

I am driven, detail-oriented, and always learning. I bring a mix of technical depth, people-centered leadership, and a genuine love for building things that matter.

Milin Desai currently serves as the CEO of Sentry. Used by more than 4 million developers and 150,000 organizations worldwide, Sentry helps every developer detect, understand, and fix broken code, fast. Milin joined Sentry from VMware where he helped build out multiple lines of business from networking (0->1b+) to multi cloud (0->100M+ SAAS). Earlier in his career, he held engineering and product roles at Veritas and Riverbed.

The StartUp Headhunter. 
Recruited for +150 startUps and Scale Ups for various roles from Tech to Commercial. After years of supporting founders in building their teams Mina has turned to StartUp founder herself: building Hi Remi - a AI Sidekick for recruiters while leading a headhunting agency

Mingshen is leading application and innovations of the trusted/confidential computing technologies at TikTok. Previously, Mingshen worked on multiple projects towards building safe, secure and trustworthy systems. Notably, he was fortunate to initiate the Apache Teaclave open-source project. Mingshen got his PhD from the Chinese University of Hong Kong, and has broad research experience on topics at the intersection of privacy and security, operating systems, and programming languages. He also serves on Technical Advisory Council of Confidential Computing Consortium.

Mirko Novakovic is the CEO and co-founder of Dash0, an observability platform built on OpenTelemetry and AI. Previously, he co-founded Instana, a leading application performance monitoring company acquired by IBM, and codecentric. As an active angel investor, he has backed more than 70 B2B SaaS startups. Mirko is passionate about building developer-first products and shaping the future of observability in the age of AI

Misha Bragin is a Berlin-based tech entrepreneur, software engineer, and open source contributor who is the co-founder and CEO of NetBird, an open source cybersecurity company focused on Zero Trust networking.

I created the Mobile DevOps Playbook book It focuses on Quality Engineering, test automation, and passion for quality. I started as a Software Engineer and became a Quality Engineer and SDET. Then I became a Developer Advocate. I help mobile and quality engineers improve app delivery efficiency and quality. I currently lead a team that creates automated tests for QA teams. The tests can handle a large workload.

Throughout my career, I gained experience and developed skills for supervising. I have 12 years of experience in software development, quality assurance, and testing. I have achieved notable milestones.

I made mobile and web automation systems using tools like Selenium WebDriver. I also used Appium, XCUITest, and Espresso.

I created CI/CD pipelines using CircleCI, GitHub Actions, GitLab, and Bitrise.

My contributions extend to many open-source projects.

I help people learn about test automation worldwide. To do this, I teach free online courses using testing tools like Espresso and Appium. I also use TestCafe. I do this through Test Automation University.

Besides to my job, I am an AWS Community Builder and a DevNetwork Advisory Board Member.

Moe is an Edge AI Solutions Architect at Edge Impulse (A Qualcomm Company), where he empowers partners and customers to bring cutting-edge machine learning into a diverse range of industries. Prior to Edge Impulse, Moe was leading the research and implementation of the first ML based navigation system for Dyson robots. With over 10 years of experience in robotics, machine learning, and embedded systems, Moe has a proven track record in architecting, leading, and implementing state-of-the-art AI and IoT solutions. His expertise spans predictive maintenance to smart automation, turning complex technologies into practical, real-world applications

Mohak Chadha is a Solutions Architect at NVIDIA, based in Munich, where he focuses on designing solutions for enterprise AI applications. Before this, he was at Firebolt, a unicorn startup, where he managed their cloud infrastructure and worked on improving security. With a background in distributed systems, Mohak specializes in cloud computing, parallel computing, and high-performance computing. He shares his expertise in distributed systems by presenting at major developer conferences, including KubeCon + CloudNativeCon and the Open Source Summit.

Mohammad-Ali A'râbi is a Senior Backend Engineer, Docker Captain, Snyk Ambassador, CNCF Organizer, public speaker, and author of the "Docker and Kubernetes Security" book, with 20 years of experience in software development.

As a Docker Captain, Mohammad-Ali has spoken at various conferences and meetups. In 2022, he founded the Docker meetup group in Freiburg, where he has since organized over a dozen events. Mohammad-Ali’s passion for security and containers shines through in both his technical work and community efforts.

I am a professional software engineer for over ten years now, I have worked with object oriented and functional programming on large-scale distributed systems. I find joy in speaking and writing about software engineering. Some of my articles and blogs are available here: https://mourjo.me

Nabil Bousselham serves as AI Go-to-Market Strategy Lead at GitHub, where he guides the EMEA rollout of GitHub Copilot and related AI-powered developer tools. Working in close partnership with enterprise engineering, product, and executive teams, he helps large organisations shape their adoption strategies and leads large-scale AI transformation initiatives from concept through to production.

Prior to GitHub, Nabil spent more than two decades in cybersecurity, with deep expertise in software supply-chain integrity, SDLC governance, quality assurance, and application-security testing. This combination of AI-driven innovation and rigorous security experience uniquely positions him to help organisations modernise their development practices while upholding the highest standards of trust and quality.

Natanael Copa is the creator of Alpine Linux and a maintainer of the k0s Kubernetes distribution. He has over 20 years of experience in Linux and open-source infrastructure, with a focus on minimal, secure systems.

With over a decade of experience in solution engineering, consulting, and digital transformation, I help organizations design scalable technology solutions that solve complex business challenges.

At Apryse, I work with enterprises and development teams on document processing, OCR, SDK integrations, workflow automation, and AI-driven extraction pipelines. My focus is on building reliable, secure, and efficient systems that turn unstructured documents into actionable data.

My background spans R&D in AI, knowledge management, and Industry 4.0 across Europe. As a speaker, I share practical engineering patterns that help developers build smarter, more predictable document AI solutions.
Nazim Ünlü

Neel Sundaresan is General Manager of AI Products and Productivity Tools at IBM. Before joining IBM, he served as Vice President of Data & AI at Microsoft, where he led several data and cloud productivity initiatives and played a key role in integrating AI into these tools. He is the creator of Bob, IBM’s AI software development partner. He pioneered the concepts of “Code as Data” and “Teaching Machines to Code,” land created Microsoft’s first AI code assistant, Intellicode which evolved into GitHub Copilot.He was instrumental in bringing key AI techniques and innovations into the product. Earlier in his career, Neel headed eBay Research and Data Labs, driving advancements in search, recommender systems, computer vision, economics, human-computer interaction, and distributed computing. He began his career at IBM’s Almaden Research Center, where he developed IBM’s first search engine and contributed to foundational work in web mining and XML systems. A frequent speaker at international conferences, Neel holds over 320 patents and has published more than 150 research papers. He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from Indiana University Bloomington and is an alumnus of the Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay.

Leading Enterprise platform focused on AI Governance, Data Security, Agent permissions & authorizations, observability & Analytics for AI AppGen to build secure apps, and create intelligent workflows & agents

Nicolò brings international experience in product strategy and leadership to Prima. He began his career at Rocket Internet before moving to Lazada and later Alibaba, where he helped shape product strategy across six Southeast Asian markets. In 2018, he co-founded Lifepal, building it into Indonesia’s leading digital insurance marketplace with over one million policies issued. He then led digital transformation at Oona Insurance and served as SVP Product at Shipper, driving major efficiency gains while sustaining rapid growth. Nicolò holds a Master’s degree from ESADE Business School.

Nikhil Gandhi is the Senior Vice President of Engineering for Yahoo Mail, where he oversees global engineering, product, and design for one of the world’s largest consumer communication platforms. He oversees a global team, leading technical strategy and execution across core messaging infrastructure, AI-driven experiences, spam and safety systems, and platform modernization, while driving user growth, engagement, and monetization. His organization also supports critical priorities including privacy, data governance, security, and global infrastructure at scale.

Prior to Yahoo, Nikhil was Head of Engineering for Ads Platform at TikTok, responsible for monetization systems powering a significant share of revenue, including creator tools and in-app commerce. He previously held leadership roles at Meta, scaling the Ads Experience organization globally, and spent a decade at Apple helping grow the Apple Online Store into a multi–tens of billions business while leading global engineering for Apple.com. A deeply technical leader, he combines strong product and platform instincts with a track record of building high-performing global teams and delivering complex systems at scale.

Microsoft Certified x6, MVP for Azure & IoT, Senior Cloud Architect and tech enthusiast. Passionate about software architecture and solution design.

During my 10+ years of hands-on experience in Software Engineering, I have primarily focused on .NET-based solutions and Azure as a cloud-services provider. I have worked with pretty much all kinds of applications, from small Windows Forms tools to large-scale enterprise web-based solutions. The more I code, the more I realize that my true passion is solution design and software architecture, regardless of tech-stack and platform.

Sharing knowledge and insights is the finest thing I know and the one thing I love the most in the IT community.

Nils Berger is the Founder and CEO of Viewpointsystem, a deep-tech company developing and producing internationally awarded smart glasses with integrated eye tracking. With its “Digital Iris” integration platform, Viewpointsystem enables seamless eye tracking in third-party wearables. By capturing gaze in real time, eye tracking enables more intuitive interaction and a deeper connection between users and digital environments. Nils is an experienced founder and serial entrepreneur with a proven track record in information services, technology consultancy, and the automation industry.

Nimrod Kor is a software engineer specializing in code analysis, cloud security, and developer tooling. As the #1 contributor to Checkov, he has helped shape best practices in Infrastructure as Code (IaC) security. His work on open-source projects like Checkov, Yor, and Airiam—focused on creating and analyzing code—has been central to his past talks.

Nnenna Ndukwe is a Principal Developer Advocate and Software Engineer, passionate about AI. With 8+ experience spanning across media tech, cybersecurity, and AI, she's an active global AI community architect championing engineers to build in emerging tech. She studied Computer Science at Boston University and is a proud member of Women Defining AI and Reg.exe. Nnenna believes that AI should augment: enabling creativity, accelerating learning, and preserving the intuition and humanity of its users.

Nune Isabekyan is a Berlin-based founder and cloud architecture expert with over 15 years of experience building scalable, intelligent systems. With a mathematics background and deep expertise in AWS, AI, and DevOps, she specializes in practical AI implementation and cloud-native architectures.
As founder and CTO of OpsWorker, Nune is building AI-powered alert investigation automation. When an alert fires, OpsWorker automatically investigates, pulls logs, checks dashboards, correlates events, and gives on-call engineers a summary of what's wrong and suggested next steps. Previously, as CIO of powerdata GmbH, she led teams building SaaS products and AI-powered customer service automation for the utilities sector. Her earlier career includes engineering roles at Teradata, Amadeus, and high-scale startups handling millions of daily users.

Staff software engineer and technical author with 5+ published books. Since 2017, he’s written extensively about software and product development, with his work reaching 5M+ readers. He specialises in turning complex problems into simple, reliable systems and building delightful user interfaces that hold up in the real world.

Alex Korotkykh is a fintech engineering leader with over 15 years of experience building large-scale, regulated software systems. Originally from Ukraine, he has been living and working in Germany for more than 10 years.

He has spent most of his career in fintech and data-driven products, working at companies such as Kreditech, figo, and Zalando, where he helped design, build, and scale complex platforms at the intersection of finance, data, and software engineering.

Alex joined Pliant at a very early stage and now leads its technology organisation of nearly 100 people, focusing on scalable architecture, security, and building reliable AI-powered products for B2B payments and accounting.

Oliver Krizek is the Founder and CEO of the NAVAX Group. Mid-sized and large enterprises rely on the company’s expertise in digital business processes and automation.

With his vision and strong focus on innovation, Krizek has grown NAVAX from its entrepreneurial beginnings in the 1990s into an international group with more than 330
employees. He combines technical know-how and entrepreneurial experience with clear, decisive leadership.

Off the business stage, the father of three is an enthusiastic skier, golfer, and triathlete. His energy and positive attitude towards life shape not only his athletic ambitions but also the future of NAVAX.

I've been a software developer at heart for ten years and am interested in everything related to software development, like containerization and orchestration, machine learning, security, and the cloud. I work as an Associate Manager @ Accenture in Munich, Germany. I love fishing, Microsoft Flight Simulator, and TikTok.

Ondřej is a Senior QA Engineer at Canva who has spent 9 years in testing, always with an eye toward what's next. He has a knack for finding innovative approaches that others might consider 'out of the box' for QA, specializing in visual testing, quality measurement, and AI applications in quality.

Ondřej Kukla runs product development of StellarBase, an AI-powered knowledge management platform currently in early access. He has spent over ten years in enterprise software, moving from data analytics and system integration into product ownership and team leadership. He is a Certified LeSS Practitioner trained by Craig Larman, co-creator of Large-Scale Scrum, and holds Scrum.org certifications in Product Ownership, Agile Leadership, and UX integrated into product discovery and the Scrum cycle. At StellarBase, he leads a team building something he believes the market is missing: a platform where organizational knowledge actually comes together instead of staying scattered across tools that don't talk to each other.

Oren is a seasoned IT executive with over 20 years of leadership across enterprise and financial sectors, now serving as Global Field CTO in the Tanzu division. A long-time platform advocate, he’s helped organizations navigate the evolution from Cloud Foundry to Kubernetes and now toward AI-native platforms. Oren is a recognised expert, author, and frequent speaker at VMware events, sharing insights drawn from real-world platform transformations at scale.

Designer by degree, frontend dev by passion, manager when necessary. 15+ years in web development. After designing and coding for blockchain stable coin financial infrastructure, shifted to AI SRE agents — helping SREs and DevOps engineers finally get some sleep.

Patrick Feige is a Senior Solutions Architect at Veracode with over a decade of experience across software engineering and cybersecurity. Having transitioned from hands-on engineering into security, he brings an engineer’s perspective to modern application security challenges. His work includes designing secure DevSecOps workflows, integrating application security tooling into CI/CD pipelines, and advising enterprise teams on software supply chain risk. At Veracode, Patrick focuses on helping organisations operationalise security across the SDLC, enabling faster delivery without compromising resilience or compliance.

Patrick Fink is Divisional CTO Digital Applications at Atos Germany, focusing on application modernization, AI-assisted software development, and the integration of intelligent features into business applications.

He drives the adoption of AI in software development at Atos Germany, helping teams increase developer productivity, improve software quality, and move from idea to prototype faster. His work combines software engineering, product thinking, security, governance, and maintainability — with a strong focus on practical implementation in enterprise environments.

Beyond his role at Atos, Patrick contributes to several open-source projects, including openHAB, one of the leading open-source platforms for smart home automation.

I'm a dedicated and highly skilled Tech Lead with a strong passion for Distributed Systems and Cloud-Native Development.

My key areas of expertise include:
- Backend Development (Go, Python, Rust)
- Distributed Systems (Microservices, Service Mesh, Message Queues)
- Cloud-Native Technologies (Kubernetes, Docker, Serverless)
- Scalable & High-Performance Systems
- Continuous Integration & Deployment (CI/CD)

Patrick Schnell is a distinguished computer scientist and acclaimed author specializing in the integration of AI into software development and business-processes. With a solid foundation in computer science and software-projects, Patrick has dedicated his career to exploring the transformative potential of AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) within the software industry. His team established Vectense (www.vectense.com) as an AI-automation-platform for small and mid-sized businesses.

At NVIDIA, Paul has responsibility for supporting customers and partners in delivering accelerated solutions for the Higher Education, High Performance Computing and AI communities in the UK. As well as providing advice on making the best use of NVIDIA hardware, Paul teaches on how to program for GPUs, mentors at hackathons and regularly engages with research software engineering groups. Paul is an advocate for using accelerated computing in HPC, and the use of AI as a powerful tool for researchers.

Pavel Malyarevsky is an engineering leader and infrastructure practitioner with nearly 20 years of experience running large-scale, mission-critical systems. He is currently the Engineering Owner of the Container as a Service part of Private Cloud at Deutsche Bank in Berlin, where he designs and operates Kubernetes-based platforms supporting latency-sensitive and high-throughput workloads.

Pavel has led cloud and infrastructure organizations across multiple countries, managing fleets of tens of thousands of servers and network devices in highly regulated environments. His background spans Linux internals, distributed systems, observability, and production engineering, with a strong focus on performance and operational correctness.

Having worked extensively with containerized runtimes in real production environments, Pavel focuses on closing the gap between Kubernetes abstractions and the underlying behavior of the Linux kernel and application runtimes—especially where hidden performance costs emerge.

Peter leads Mastercard’s Central Europe Division, driving strategy and tech-driven innovations across 12 markets in Europe, including Germany, Poland, and Switzerland. 
With nearly two decades at Mastercard, he has shaped digital payments, business development, and innovative solutions like wallets and tokenization in Europe, AP, and MEA. 

He held prior roles in Mastercard Advisors, Roland Berger and Batten & Company. Peter holds an MBA in Banking & Finance, studying in Australia, Germany and the U.K.

Peter is an experienced C++ software developer with a particular interest in making things simpler. Part of making things simpler is to understand the full complexity of a particular problem and then seeing what the simplest solution that is still sufficient would be. He is a part of the ISO C++ Committee since 2019 and participates mostly in the Tooling (SG15), Unicode (SG16), Contracts (SG21) and Safety (SG23) study groups.

Peter Caron is CEO of 3T Software Labs, the team behind Studio 3T, the leading productivity platform for MongoDB teams. With deep experience spanning software engineering, data platforms, and enterprise technology, Peter works closely with global organisations to help them scale MongoDB securely, efficiently, and with confidence. He is a vocal advocate for practical, responsible technology adoption, cutting through hype to focus on real-world outcomes, strong data foundations, and sustainable innovation.

Peter Hock is Head of Digital Applications for Germany, Austria, Czechia and Poland. As a senior executive in digital applications and software-driven transformation, he brings more than 20 years of experience in IT services and consulting—on both the customer and provider side—and across international business environments.

He leads global delivery teams across on-, near-, and offshore and helps organizations design, build, and operate business-critical digital solutions end to end.

His focus is not on technology for its own sake, but on how organizations translate strategy into execution and measurable outcomes. He specializes in structuring complex initiatives across the full lifecycle—from idea and requirements to delivery, operation, and continuous optimization.

Peter is particularly driven by challenging implementations—where complexity, scale, and real-world constraints demand clear ownership, strong governance, and tight alignment between business, users, and engineering.

------------

Mateusz Gren is Head of Software Development at Atos in Austria and a driving force behind large-scale digital transformation in enterprise environments. Starting his career as a software developer, he brings a hands-on perspective to leading complex programs that combine modern software engineering, DevOps, and AI.

In recent years, Mateusz has been at the forefront of integrating generative AI into software development - from code generation and automated testing to new collaboration models between engineers and AI systems. His focus is not on hype, but on making AI work in real-world enterprise projects with strict requirements for governance, quality, and scalability.

As a speaker, he shares practical insights on how organizations can adopt AI in software engineering without losing control. His perspective bridges technology, delivery, and leadership - with one goal: building better software, faster.
Developer Relations Engineer and unrepentant web technology trash panda Peter Kröner reads web standard specifications for fun and writes about frontend tooling since the early 2000s.

Mr. Kürpick holds a Ph.D. in Theoretical Physics from the University of Kassel and has conducted post-doctorate research at Kansas State University and the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. He also holds a Masters in Environmental Sciences from the University of Kassel as well as a Corporate MBA from INSEAD.

Mr. Kürpick is Senior Vice President at EPAM Systems in Berlin. In a dual role he is both responsible for the German entity of EPAM and global CTO Enterprise Software. He joined EPAM Systems from BainCapital where he oversaw the adoption of latest IT strategies in the BainCapital portfolio companies in the role of a Technology Operating Partner. He also is a Non-Executive Director of PTV and DualFluid.

Previously, he has held numerous technology leadership roles at HERE Technologies, Unify, Software AG and SAP, where he was instrumental in the conception and development of SAP NetWeaver.
speakers.company_position: Senior VP Germany and Austria and CTO

speakers.bio:
Peter Swierzy is the CEO of BlockyDevs, a R&D software development company specializing in blockchain, Web3, fintech, Agentic Commerce, and AI-driven software development.

He helps startups, foundations, and enterprise clients turn complex technical ideas into production-ready products.

Peter is especially focused on Agentic Commerce, enabling AI agents to discover services, initiate payments, interact with APIs, and participate in digital transactions autonomously. His work also explores how AI-assisted development, agent-based workflows, and decentralized infrastructure can create practical value beyond the hype.

Phil Leggetter is an experienced Developer Experience, Developer Relations, and DevTools PLG leader with a background guiding startups through growth, acquisition, and scale within a publicly traded enterprise.

He has spent his career building software experiences that maximize developer productivity at companies such as Pusher (acquired by MessageBird), Nexmo (acquired by Vonage), and, most recently, Hookdeck, where he helps build platforms and tools that empower developers to create interoperable and dependable event-driven systems.

Philipp Schmid is a Senior AI Developer Relations Engineer at Google DeepMind working on Gemini, Gemma with the mission to help every developer and builder to create and benefit from AI in a responsible way.
Pierre-Louis Cedoz

An active contributor to open source, content creator on Medium and YouTube, focusing on practical, scalable solutions in cloud-native environments. DevOps and Platform Engineering practitioner and advocate.

PJ Hagerty is a well-known figure in the tech industry, particularly within the developer relations and DevOps communities. He is recognized for his work as a developer advocate, community builder, writer, and speaker. PJ founded DevRelate.io, a company focused on helping tech organizations build communities and enhance developer relations. He has a strong presence in the open-source and tech communities, often speaking at conferences and producing content on topics such as software development, DevOps, and community engagement.

Rasmus Klärck is the Founding GTM Lead at Oplane. He is not a security engineer, and that is the point. He builds AI products himself (like Cosos, an AI agent that runs sales pipeline inside Slack), which is how he ran headfirst into the problem Oplane solves: AI now writes and ships code faster than any team can review it, and the risks that matter most are architectural, not line-level. Oplane is an AI security engineer that continuously threat-models your codebase, surfaces the real architectural risks in minutes, and drives the fix inside the developer workflow. Hey there! 👋🏾 I’m Samuel, a Developer Advocate at Redis focused on building and explaining AI systems that actually work in the real world.

I spend my time exploring how to design reliable AI agents, especially around memory, state, and retrieval. In practice, that means helping developers understand how to move beyond demos into systems that are scalable, observable, and useful in production.

My background is in Applied AI and software engineering, with experience across machine learning, backend systems, and developer education. I’m particularly interested in how tools like Redis can act as the memory layer for modern AI applications, making agents more consistent, context-aware, and performant.

I care a lot about clarity. Not just what AI can do, but how it behaves under real constraints like latency, cost, and failure. Most of my work is about breaking these ideas down into something practical that engineers can actually use.

Alongside this, I host Stories That Shape Us, where I speak with professionals and creatives about the experiences and decisions that shape their lives and careers.

At the core, I believe good technology should be understandable, dependable, and built to serve people.

At the core of Seth’s work is a simple belief: technology is ultimately about people — and the best tools don’t just increase productivity, they expand human potential.
