Entered the world of testing in 2014 and loving it ever since!

Upon entering the world of software development, I learned quickly that in order to bring the right product to customers, having people with the best tech knowledge with you is simply not enough – there is so much more to it. This is why besides learning new tools and techniques I always take the time to work on my communication and organizational skills as well as on my understanding of human interactions.

Supporting multiple teams working in an e-commerce, I had the opportunity to take my coaching skills to a serious test and prove that being a tester or later on a staff engineer doesn’t have to be a lonely position in the organization.

Always interested in sharing my experience and knowledge as well as learning about yours! System quality, chaos engineering, observability and testing in isolation are just a few of the topics close to my heart.

I was fortunate enough to be able to take the stage at Agile Testing Days, HUSTEF and AutomationSTAR and become a program committee member of HUSTEF this year!

Sophie-Theresa Kleinke is a Data Engineer and Business Analyst at Finanz Informatik, the IT service provider and digitalization partner of the Savings Banks Finance Group.
With an academic background in mathematics, she focuses on the analysis and optimization of business processes, specializing in process mining methodologies.
Her expertise spans the entire data value chain—from working with SQL and backend data structures to designing advanced reporting and data visualization solutions using modern business intelligence tools.
Combining data engineering with business analysis, she enables data-driven decision-making and drives efficiency across complex financial systems.

Stan Bühne is Managing Director at IREB, with over 20 years of experience in Requirements Engineering, business analysis, and project management. During 15 years in IT consulting, he led international digitalization and transformation projects, particularly in the telco sector. 
In addition to his role at IREB, Stan is an active board member of the Bitkom Working Group for Software Engineering and Architecture and a frequent speaker at international conferences, sharing insights on digital trends and best practices.

Stas Lebedenko is a Microsoft Azure Most Valuable Professional in AI & Azure categories, full-stack cloud architect and platform engineer at Eficode. He is also a co-organizer of the Odesa Microsoft. NET User Group, a speaker and author, and an active member of the international and Ukraine .NET/Azure/Operations communities. He has been building and designing software for the last twenty years and has been the MVP since 2020.

Stefan is a software architect at ZEISS Digital Innovation in Berlin and has worked on various Angular projects in different industries throughout his career. He loves experimenting with new technologies and sharing his knowledge by speaking at conferences and contributing at community events. Recently, he has been leading an engineering design team focused on leveraging micro frontend architecture for performant healthcare digitization.

Stefan Ganß is a journalist, TV reporter, and host with more than twenty years of experience in German public broadcasting, including ARD and MDR. He has won the German Radio Award and knows what it means to communicate under pressure: live on camera, in front of an audience, with no second take.
Since 2023, he has also worked as a double-certified business trainer, trained at TAM Academy and certified by BDVT. As a storytelling trainer, he helps leaders, sales teams, researchers, and experts turn complex topics into stories that create clarity, trust, and connection.
His clients include Siemens Energy, Techem, Berlitz, and several universities. His core belief: in the age of AI, human connection is becoming a real advantage. Storytelling is one of the most practical tools to build it.

Stefan Lehmann was appointed Chief Executive Officer of Generali Deutschland as of September 2022 and is a member of the Group Management Committee of Assicurazioni Generali. He is responsible for the strategic direction of Generali Deutschland, which is one of the leading primary insurers in the German market, and is driving profitable growth, sustainable value creation, transformation across the organization and digitalization.

Stefan holds a degree in Economics from the University of Bochum and an Executive MBA in General & Strategic Management from the Maastricht School of Management. Prior to his studies, he completed a banking apprenticeship. Stefan started his professional career at WestLB Panmure Investment Banking in London and Düsseldorf.

Since 2004, he has held a series of senior management positions at Generali Deutschland, including Head of Participations Management, Head of Corporate Controlling & Development at Generali Deutschland Claims Management, Head of Customer Service at CosmosDirekt Versicherungen, and Head of Strategic Planning & Controlling of Generali Deutschland.

In 2017, Stefan was promoted to Chief Financial Officer of Generali Deutschland and in this role, he held different board roles in group companies. From January to August 2022, he was appointed Chief Business Officer Exclusive of Generali Deutschland with responsibility for the partnership with Deutsche Vermögensberatung AG. Since September 2022 he has been Chief Executive Officer of Generali Deutschland.

Since his appointment as CEO, Stefan has been shaping Generali Deutschland with a clear focus on profitable growth, sustainable value creation, cultural transformation, and digitalization. Under his leadership, the company has achieved significant profitable growth across all lines of business. At the same time, he drove a fundamental cultural transformation, repositioning Generali Deutschland from a more traditional insurance organization to a truly modern and attractive employer.

Stefan Lemke began his career as an IT architect, consultant, and manager of transformation projects at Accenture after completing his studies in physics and his doctorate, ultimately serving as a Partner in Germany and the Netherlands. From 2010, he held leadership positions at Allianz Technology in Munich and Paris before moving to the Zurich Group in Germany as CIO. In 2017, he was appointed to the Board of Management of AXA Konzern AG, where he drove agile organization and culture, technological modernization, and the renewal of the application landscape. 

Since 2023, Stefan Lemke has been a member of the Boards of Management of the SIGNAL IDUNA Group. He says: "Application development is a core business process, differentiating for our customers and distribution partners - and no longer conceivable without AI, cloud, and an agile setup."

Stefan Mesquita leads the Platform Experience team for Corporate Bank platforms at Deutsche Bank. He is responsible for scaling the bank’s core API, Kafka, and BigQuery infrastructure to drive organizational efficiency. With over 15 years of experience in the financial sector, Stefan specializes in Platform Engineering and defining the architectural patterns that allow product teams to deliver faster. By decoupling infrastructure complexity from product development, he enables the organization to focus on business value and innovation. Stefan is a strategic leader dedicated to bridging the gap between large-scale systems engineering and digital business strategy.

Stefan Ostwald is Chief AI Officer and Co-Founder of Parloa, a leading AI company redefining customer service through AI agents. Stefan's passion for conversational AI dates back to 2016, when he co-founded Future of Voice — one of Europe's first voice AI agencies. In 2018, he went on to found Parloa, where he now leads the agentic AI research team, bridging state-of-the-art research with enterprise-grade solutions. His mission: making AI so reliable and intuitive that every conversation deepens the customer relationship.

Stefanie Manzinger is a Senior Solutions Architect for Automotive and Manufacturing at NVIDIA. In her role, she helps customers accelerate physical AI development with NVIDIA Cosmos and drives the adoption of generative AI for industrial applications. Previously, as a Development Engineer at MAN Truck & Bus SE, she focused on developing autonomous driving solutions. Stefanie holds a Ph.D. from the Technical University of Munich, where her doctoral research specialized in decision-making and trajectory planning for autonomous vehicles. She is passionate about supporting developers in adopting and improving physical AI systems, driven by her interest in the evolving role of foundation models within industrial AI and robotics.

Stephanie Cohen joined Cloudflare in 2024 as the company’s first-ever Chief Strategy Officer. A seasoned operating executive, she brings more than two decades of experience at Goldman Sachs where she advised the leadership and boards of some of the world's most important companies and institutions. In addition to her time in Investment Banking, she was Goldman Sachs's Chief Strategy Officer, ran two multi-billion dollar global segments and served as a member of their Management Committee. In her role at Cloudflare, Stephanie leads the company's efforts in expanding the breadth and depth of its presence in boardrooms and the C-suite on its path to $5B in revenue and beyond.  She focuses on the most important initiatives driving Cloudflare's growth, including the future of the network, international expansion, AI and the developer platform. Stephanie has a Bachelors of Science in Finance from the University of Illinois.  She is a trustee for the Economic Club of New York and Park City Day School, a member of the Board of Quill.org and an Ambassador for US Ski & Snowboard.  She is also a member of the Board of Directors of Fiserv.
Stephen Batifol

Steren is a founding member of Google Cloud Run, and is today the product leader for Google Cloud's serverless portfolio (Cloud Run, Cloud Run functions, App Engine, Tasks, Scheduler, Eventarc, Workflows). Steren also notably founded Google Cloud Carbon Footprint.

Steren is an engineer turned product manager, he graduated from École Centrale (France). Before joining Google, he was CTO of a startup building connected objects and multi device solutions.

Steven is a Software Engineer with 5+ years of experience building and productionizing machine learning systems that solve high-impact business problems. He has worked across domains including user acquisition, customer retention, content generation, supply optimization, and lead generation.

Currently at GetYourGuide, he is part of the Machine Learning and AI Platform team, where he focuses on enabling teams to independently build and operate production-grade LLM systems.

Outside of work, Steven co-organizes the AI in Production meetup in Berlin, a community dedicated to showcasing real-world AI applications across industries. In his free time, he enjoys exploring food and building tools he'd otherwise have to pay for.

Stuart is a Microsoft Azure & AI MVP and software engineer with a passion for cloud technologies, AI and modern programming languages. He also keeps a close eye on the evolving world of DevOps and the tooling, practices and patterns that surround it.

Stuart closely follows F1 in his free time, likes to do several sports such as cycling, golf and throwing weights around in the gym, and he tries to keep up with the endless stream of series and movies (with varying degrees of success).

I’m a frontend engineer with over five years of experience in building scalable, user-focused web applications. For the past 2.5 years, I’ve been working at Delivery Hero in Berlin, developing modern, high-performance interfaces using React, Next.js, and TypeScript.

My journey includes impactful work with Adevinta (mobile.de) and SUSTLabs – IIT Bombay, where I contributed to projects in blockchain, energy informatics, and e-commerce. I’ve published research with ACM e-Energy and explored AR-based ideation tools at IDC School of Design, IIT Bombay.

I’m passionate about creating clean, accessible interfaces and integrating design with sustainability and innovation. I’ve attended Google I/O Connect in 2024 and 2025, staying connected to the evolving developer ecosystem and the latest in web technologies.

Susanne (She/Her) is a C# backend engineer in the .NET stack who loves clean, maintainable code, automating the boring stuff, and letting tools like Renovate do the heavy lifting. She started her career as a full-stack developer, but these days she’s all about backend magic—automation, productivity, and delving into the depths of Agentic development. 

As an international conference speaker, Susanne shares clear, relatable stories about building software, working smarter with automation, and boosting developer productivity, all delivered in a practical, approachable, and yes, sometimes fun way. She also champions inclusive, neurodiverse-friendly environments in tech.

When she’s not coding or speaking, Susanne enjoys exploring creative projects and slaying demons in Diablo IV. Her approach to work and life is rooted in curiosity, creativity, and finding joy in the process—whether that’s writing code, telling stories, or leveling up her character in-game.

Creator of Rozenite, Harness and Voltra. The kind of person who dives into code and somehow makes the "impossible" happen. Focused on building the next generation of developer tools so you can ship your apps even faster.

I believe that simplicity strongly resonates with a good architecture and results in an amazing performance of both: the engineering and the product itself. I like my data to be served fast. I don't allocate before evening.

I help teams and leaders get genuinely good at using AI. Not the "watched a webinar" kind of good. The "transformed how we work" kind.

I have been building software for over 25 years, training technical teams at Fortune 500 companies, and speaking at conferences worldwide. These days I focus on AI fluency: giving people the understanding and hands-on skill to use AI tools effectively in their actual work.

Taroon Mandhana is CTO of AI and Teamwork at Atlassian, a leading provider of productivity and collaboration software. In his role, Taroon oversees engineering teams responsible for Atlassian’s flagship products, including Jira, Confluence, and Loom—which are bundled within the Teamwork Collection. He also leads the development of Rovo, Atlassian's AI, which includes Search, Chat, Agents, and Studio.

Outside of Atlassian, Taroon holds nearly 15 years of AI experience. In his previous roles, he led the Search team at Microsoft Bing and later joined Meta to lead their Facebook Search and Marketplace teams. In his second stint at Microsoft, he led the company’s content teams and built the personalized recommendation feed, Microsoft Start.

Taroon graduated from the Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi, and received his Master’s from the University of Texas at Austin. In his free time, he enjoys going on hikes around the Pacific Northwest with his family.

Taylor Smith, Senior AI Developer Advocate at Red Hat, is an advocate of open source AI innovation and democratization. She has a background in software development, open source technologies like Kubernetes and linux, and technical partnerships. Taylor loves music, animals, and helping to solve real-world problems with technology. Based out of North Carolina.

Tessel is a consultant in data & AI at Info Support, specialized in interpretable AI. In her daily work, she applies AI-augmented engineering to build and evaluate intelligent systems. She is known as an enthusiastic knowledge source and an insatiably curious lifelong learner. Outside of work, she channels her strategic thinking into board games (with a soft spot for complex strategy games) and escapes into fantasy novels. With an MSc in Artificial Intelligence, an MSc in Computer Science, and an MA in Linguistics, she combines a strong technical foundation with a deep interest in language and reasoning.

Theresa Heine is a Product Owner with a software engineering background, focused on software quality, test automation, and release processes. Passionate about improving reliability in legacy systems while adapting quality practices for AI-driven development. Known for making complex technical topics understandable and bridging the gap between technical and non-technical stakeholders.

As the Technical Evangelist at Varnish Software, Thijs Feryn focuses on web performance, software scalability, and content delivery. He demonstrates content-driven and technical messaging through presentations, videos, books, blog posts, social media posts, podcasts, and other media.

Thijs is a published author and wrote Getting Started with Varnish Cache and Varnish 6 by Example. As a public speaker, he has a track record of over 340 presentations in 23 different countries, where he is often praised for his energetic and engaging presentation style.

As an evangelist, Thijs is also active in many open-source communities, most notably the Varnish and PHP community. He has contributed to various communities for over 15 years both technically and as an organizer and facilitator.

Prior to joining Varnish Software, Thijs Feryn spent 15 years in the web hosting industry, tackling web performance and scalability issues on a daily basis and evangelizing these topics.

Fascinated by software development since his childhood in Germany, Thomas Dohmke has built a career creating tools developers love. He is currently working on a new startup in this space. Formerly, Thomas was Chief Executive Officer of GitHub, where he led the company’s growth to over 150 million developers and oversaw the rise of GitHub Copilot — the world’s most widely adopted AI developer tool — now with over 20 million users and counting. Thomas is a celebrated TED speaker and holds a PhD in mechanical engineering from the University of Glasgow, UK.

Thomas Endres is a Managing Partner for TNG Technology Consulting in Munich. Besides his normal work for the company and the management of the customers he is creating various prototypes, he and the the Innovation Hacking Team develop various prototypes, including an Augmented Reality application that displays the world from an artist's perspective, real-time deepfakes, or an AI for generating presentations. He works on applications in the field of AR/VR, AI, and gesture control to e.g. autonomously fly quadcopters or control them without physical contact. Furthermore, he is involved in various Open Source projects.

Thomas studied computer science at TU Munich and is a passionate software developer. As an Intel Software Innovator and Black Belt, he presents new technologies such as AI, AR/VR, and robotics worldwide. For this, he has received awards including a JavaOne Rockstar Award and several Best Speaker Awards.

Thomas Eriksson, Founder and CEO of SASHA- Safe Share, is a Danish technology leader at the forefront of watermarking technology. Driven by the mission to end online image abuse, Thomas developed SASHA’s patented "in-pixel" signature technology that embeds ownership and consent directly into digital content.

As a serial entrepreneur with a background in edtech and medtech, Thomas has scaled SASHA from a vision to a robust product presented at the EU Presidency Meeting for Digital Affairs and awarded the 2025 World Summit Global Champion Award. SASHA tech provides platforms with the tools to streamline moderation and neutralize image abuse at the source.

Thomas Fricke has been working with containers and Kubernetes for 7 years, with Linux and networks for 30 and with computers for 40. His focus is on security in KRITIS environments (energy, health care, government, international transport) and the agile transformations that are necessary in these environments. He is a freelancer, founder (Endocode, Mitigant), and volunteered on various boards, including the German IT Planning Council.
He designed the security architecture for the cloud native OpenDesk application.

Thomas Froment is the Program Manager for Development Tools at the Eclipse Foundation, where he oversees all tooling projects, including Eclipse Theia and Theia AI, as part of the Cloud DevTools Working Group. He also leads the Open VSX and Eclipse IDE working groups.

Thomas Hugle is a Senior Consultant with TNG Technology Consulting, with a focus on AI and frontend development. His PhD thesis at the Max-Planck-Institut für Kernphysik in Heidelberg was mostly about data analysis for the CONUS neutrino experiment and computer simulations of physical systems. Besides his work as a software consultant, he handles data mining projects, to apply his data analysis skills to the Spotify data of his colleagues, and gives talks about how to turn from a physicist into a software developer.

Thomas Jarzombek was born in Düsseldorf and has been active in politics for more than 25 years. He served as a member of the Düsseldorf City Council and as a member of the State Parliament of North Rhine-Westphalia.

Since 2009, he has been a Member of the German Bundestag.

From 2013 to 2018, he acted as the spokesperson for the Digital Agenda of the CDU/CSU parliamentary group. Between 2018 and 2021, Thomas Jarzombek served as the Federal Government Coordinator for Aerospace, and from 2019 to 2021, as the Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs’ Representative for the Digital Economy and Start-ups.

Most recently, from 2021 to 2025, he was the spokesperson for Education and Research of the CDU/CSU parliamentary group and a member of the Committee on Digital Affairs. Since May 2025, he has served as Parliamentary State Secretary to the Federal Minister for Digital Transformation and Government Modernisation.
speakers.company_position: Parliamentary State Secretary to the Federal Minister for Digital Transformation and Government Modernisation

speakers.bio: I lead a team focused on making life a little easier for SAP developers. We create tutorials, blog posts, videos, sample code, and show up at in-person events—all aimed at cutting through the noise and helping you actually build things. No fluff. Just tools and content that make you more effective.

Thomas Saueressig is a Member of the Executive Board and Chief Customer Officer of SAP SE.

His board area, the Customer Value Group (CVG), brings together SAP’s go-to-market and delivery functions to help customers effectively drive their business transformation, fully leverage the benefits of AI, and build long-term customer value in the cloud.

Saueressig has a strong track record of delivering customer value. In his previous role, he led the Executive Board area Customer Services & Delivery, where he worked closely with customers to achieve business outcomes through innovation and end-to-end business transformation.

Before being appointed the head of SAP Product Engineering, Saueressig served as Chief Information Officer (CIO) and Global Head of IT Services at SAP. As CIO, he was responsible for SAP’s IT strategy and its execution with a cloud-first approach. Saueressig joined SAP in 2004 in consulting and subsequently held various international management positions with increasing responsibility.

Saueressig holds a degree in Business Informatics from the Baden-Wuerttemberg Cooperative State University in Mannheim and a joint Executive MBA from ESSEC Business School (France) and Mannheim Business School (Germany).

Since April 2022, he has been a member of the Board of Directors of Nokia and serves as vice-chairman.
speakers.company_position: CCO and Member of the Executive Board

speakers.bio:
Thomas Weinschenk is an integration specialist in the process industry and Co‑Lead of the Open Edge Computing (OEC) workgroup at the Open Industry 4.0 Alliance. He focuses on vendor‑neutral edge foundations—making applications and devices discoverable, operable, and interoperable in control‑cabinet environments.
Thomas bridges OT and IT with practical patterns for onboarding, app‑to‑app communication, identity/semantics, and reliable operations under intermittent networks. He speaks from hands‑on experience: turning proof‑of‑concepts into repeatable, multi‑vendor deployments without lock‑in, and enabling teams to unlock process data responsibly.
As a developer at Endress+Hauser, Thomas values open standards and reproducible engineering over tool marketing. His talks deliver guardrails, checklists, and field‑tested lessons audiences can apply right away.

Tilman Mürle is Managing Director and Co-Founder of Komplyzen, where he helps organizations turn AI governance from a compliance exercise into an operational capability. He brings over a decade of leadership experience across SaaS, enterprise IT, and regulated environments, including his role as former CEO EMEA at Valiantys.

Tilman works with engineering, risk, and leadership teams to design AI systems that are measurable, auditable, and production-ready by design. His focus lies on operational AI governance, AI risk management, and translating regulatory expectations into practical architecture and workflows that scale.

He regularly speaks at industry conferences on AI risk, governance, and the realities of bringing AI safely into production.

Tim Schade is Tech Office Lead, software architect, and software engineer at GFT. With more than 18 years of experience, he designs and delivers complex, scalable software solutions with a strong focus on JVM-based architectures.

His areas of expertise include cloud technologies, DevOps, artificial intelligence, and IT security. In addition, he is a recognized expert, speaker, and researcher in sustainable software engineering and green coding, focusing on the development of energy-efficient and low-carbon software systems.

In his talks, Tim combines extensive hands-on experience with modern technologies and demonstrates how sustainable software architecture can be successfully applied in real-world projects.

Toan Nguyen has been hailed as a "super-nerd" (Meedia), a "wunderkind" (Handelsblatt), a "magician" (RampStyle), and the "rockstar of marketing" (W&V).

He began his career as an intern at Jung von Matt and worked his way up to become the youngest equity partner in the agency's history. During his tenure at Jung von Matt, he founded several verticals, including JvM STARS, JvM NERD, and ALFR3D, and established an investment in Eintracht Spandau—a League of Legends and Baller League club co-founded with YouTube star Maximilian Knabe.

Toan Nguyen has been featured four times on "Top 40 under 40" lists (Capital, Horizont) and is one of the few Germans ever to be appointed twice to the Cannes Lions jury. Today, he advises executive boards as an advisory board member and senior advisor, while also acting as an investor in the longevity space and hosting a Top 30 healthcare podcast (Die Ärztin und der Nerd / The Doctor and the Nerd).

Tolga Elser has spent 25 years building things that work — from benchmarking GPUs with timedemo demo1 in the 90s to agentic AI systems running in production across regulated DACH industries.

Today he leads technical architecture pre-sales at Salesforce, focused on one question: what does it actually take to deploy autonomous AI in enterprises where GDPR compliance and data residency are hard constraints, not afterthoughts? His answer is always a working prototype, not a slide.

He lives at the forefront — building POCs before the product exists, prototyping agentic workflows before the frameworks are stable, and shipping into production environments where the margin for error is zero.

I’m a Senior Software Engineer based in Berlin, currently working at Pliant, where I build internal and customer-facing AI features. My focus is on designing end-to-end AI workflows that combine multiple agents, tools, and data sources, from BI-style database interactions to RAG-based systems. Before moving into AI, I spent several years working on integrations, building APIs and connecting Pliant with accounting and PayOps platforms like DATEV, Lexoffice, and Circula, as well as helping shape our Partner API.

Before Pliant, I worked on large-scale backend systems in telecom and enterprise software, mainly around integrations, and high-throughput data pipelines. Earlier on, I explored game development and computer vision through internships and student roles, which helped shape my interest in building things end to end. I enjoy working on complex systems, turning ideas into production-ready solutions, and learning new technologies along the way.

Who am I?
 - 10+ years in Software Architecture
 - 15+ years in .NET development 
 - 20+ years in Software Development
 - Diploma (M.Sc.) and PhD in Computer Science
 - with a strong academic research background in the area of big data, information retrieval, search, and distributed systems
 - applying broad technical knowledge with strategic guidance to develop innovative software solutions
 - combining advanced technology and efficient algorithms with real business cases
 - cloud transformation and cloud-native development
 - lead of multiple development teams
 - special interest in Software Analysis and Diagnostics, especially in .NET
 - always eager to learn and share knowledge
 - Coffee averse, Earl Grey addicted

Speaking & Sharing
- speaker at company-wide tech events
- speaker at company-wide live webinars
- creator and speaker in video tutorials
- actively supporting teams with software architecture, software analysis and diagnostics, and cloud-native issues

For early publications & public speaker presence (PhD related), see e.g.:
https://dl.acm.org/profile/81342492383

See also: 
- https://cid.com/author/tomcrecelius/
- my latest #techTalk @Tom Crecelius on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/tom-crecelius/recent-activity/all/

Alternating between software engineering and technical education roles, Tom Wheeler's career spans more than 25 years in the financial, healthcare, defense, and tech industries. Prior to joining Temporal, he created training courses at Cloudera, developed aerospace engineering software at Object Computing, Inc. (OCI), helped design and implement a distributed system for high-volume data processing at WebMD, and wrote code for some of the earliest web applications at A.G. Edwards. Tom enjoys learning new things and teaching them to others, which has led to presentations at tech conferences around the world. When Tom manages to step away from the computer, you can probably find him cooking, traveling, or playing guitar.

20+ years’ experience in developing .NET.
I have experience in designing solution architecture, technically managing development team and
deploying software to production environments.
The idea is matters, language is just a tool - not afraid to touch explore Python, Node.js, COBOL, JCL, etc.
Designing and implementing large-scalable online systems in C#.

Experienced AI/ML Engineer and Cloud Architect, specializing in Google Cloud and Generative AI. Distinguished as a Google Developer Expert. He manages a YouTube channel, and runs a technology blog dedicated to promoting Cloud Native development. His projects often involve the integration of various artificial intelligence domains. As a sought-after speaker, he delivers lectures and training on Public Cloud and AI, sharing his extensive knowledge and experience with a wide audience.

Tomislav Tipurić is a business and technology professional who connects technical expertise with business strategy to clarify new technologies and drive corporate innovation. As a Chief Technology Officer and Lead Hackathon Coach, he focuses on artificial intelligence, multi-cloud platforms, and modern software development practices. He has designed more than 20 custom learning hackathons and delivered over 500 hands-on educational experiences, including more than 150 sessions dedicated to machine learning, generative AI, and agentic AI solutions. Tomislav translates technical concepts into business strategies, delivering workshops and lectures to C-level executives on the business impact of AI in their industries. Additionally, he has experience in sales enablement and corporate training, serving as a program trainer to help enterprise partners market, sell, and adopt generative AI and cloud productivity tools, and delivering cloud sales enablement programs for global telecommunications firms. Tomislav is certified and authorized as an official trainer by Google and Microsoft.

Torsten Stiller is a seasoned tech leader and speaker, currently heading the Automation Factory Domain at E.ON Digital Technology. A former Microsoft and NVIDIA professional, he brings deep experience in cloud-native platforms, automation, and digital transformation. Torsten is passionate about the responsible use of AI and sustainable software engineering, combining hands-on expertise with strategic vision to drive ethical, impactful innovation.

As SVP, People & Culture at Flip, Trecilla Lobo specializes in building workplace cultures that drive high performance. She brings over two decades of expertise in scaling global tech firms and is responsible for Flip’s end-to-end people strategy, ensuring that inclusivity and engagement remain at the heart of the company’s growth.

Tug helps organisations across the EMEA region unlock the full potential of AI-powered development. Tug is passionate about bringing GitHub Copilot to developer communities — from enterprise teams to local meetups. He regularly speaks and runs hands-on workshops on topics like agentic AI, vibe coding, and building custom Copilot agents, skills, and MCP integrations. Based in France, Tug combines deep technical expertise with a genuine love for sharing knowledge and empowering developers to build smarter and faster.

I am a senior IT professional with extensive experience in the e-commerce domain, specialising in solution architecture and team leadership. I focus on building scalable, high-performance systems, migrating from legacy platforms, and driving innovation in competitive markets.

Ute Neher is Talent Strategy Director at Indeed, driving innovation at the intersection of data, inclusion, and HR strategy. She empowers leaders to reimagine hiring through skill-based approaches and responsible AI. With a strong voice in the HR community and as a Queb board member, Ute challenges the status quo and champions better work for all—globally and locally.

Utz Bacher is Vice President Software at Q.ANT, where he leads the development of the software ecosystem for Q.ANT’s photonic processors and drives the exploitation of photonic computing technology. After his studies in Information Technology, Utz spent more than 25 years in various business units at IBM. He started as a Linux kernel developer for high speed I/O and networking code, before leading the Linux on Cell/B.E. development team. After designing the hypervisor management for the hybrid mainframe, he became the lead architect for Linux on IBM Z. Utz also drove adoption of container technology and the concept of confidential computing on the platform and was appointed Senior Technical Staff Member. Later, he moved into IBM’s services organization, shaping Cloud solutions and their delivery for client projects. In 2021, Utz joined IBM Quantum where he was the responsible architect for bringing IBM's quantum capabilities to the Cloud. He also designed the integration of client IT environments and HPC clusters from scientific compute centers with IBM Quantum. Utz joined Q.ANT in 2026.

Uwe Korn is a CTO at the EconAI company QuantCo. His expertise is in building scalable architectures for machine learning services and the teams & culture around them. Nowadays, he focuses on the software engineering infrastructure needed to provide the building blocks for bringing ML/AI projects into production. As part of his work, he became a core committer to the Apache Parquet, Apache Arrow and conda-forge projects.

Hands-on Architect, IT Trainer, and Conference Speaker with 20+ years of experience, passionate about building high-quality software, mentoring developers, and sharing knowledge.

I've trained over 550 professionals in Architecture, EventStorming, and TDD, built and deployed more than 10 production-ready projects, led multiple development teams, and created a successful Java department from scratch. Along the way, I’ve mentored 20+ developers and conducted 100+ technical interviews.

My expertise lies in Java, but I also have experience with Kotlin, Groovy, and other programming languages. I believe that strong technical skills, continuous learning, teamwork, and knowledge-sharing are the keys to success in software development.

I’m a technologist at heart, though life often leads me into managerial roles. For the last 15 years, I’ve built products, led and advised companies, published papers, and shared my insights at conferences and in articles on the web.

Co-founder and CTO of Nx, an agentic build platform that scales CI and automatically fixes failed PRs.

A former member of the Angular core team at Google. Victor has spent 15+ years developing high-performance frameworks and build tools for large-scale JavaScript applications, with a focus on enterprise developer experience.

Viktoria Semaan is a Principal AI Evangelist at Databricks, specializing in AI agents, data engineering, and enterprise AI implementation. She has spent years helping organizations build production-ready AI systems, previously as a Senior Developer Advocate at AWS. 

With 15+ years of experience spanning technology, engineering, and management consulting, Viktoria has led Build and Go-to-Market efforts with top software companies. She holds an MBA from UCLA Anderson and an entrepreneurship program at HEC Paris.

Named an AI/ML Top Voice on LinkedIn, Viktoria is deeply passionate about coaching, talent development, and mentoring. She regularly shares educational content with her audience of over 630,000 tech professionals, fostering a collaborative and inclusive community across her platforms.

Dr. Vinesh Sukumar currently serves as Vice President of Product Management at Qualcomm Technologies, Inc. Vinesh acts as head of AI/ML, leading AI product definition, strategy and solution deployment across multiple business units. 

He has about 20 years of industry experience spread across research, engineering and application deployment. He holds a doctorate degree specializing in imaging and vision systems and also holds an MBA focused on strategy and marketing. He is a regular speaker in many AI industry forums and has authored several journal papers and two technical books.
speakers.company_position: VP Product Management and AI/Gen AI

speakers.bio: Vitaly is the Head of Rust Ecosystem at JetBrains. He is passionate about programming languages (all of them!) and works closely with Rust, and other projects at JetBrains. As the author of "Haskell in Depth", he firmly believes that writing code in a functional style and avoiding mutation whenever possible leads to better outcomes.

Vitaly loves beautiful content and does not give up easily. Born in Minsk, Belarus, he studied computer science and mathematics in Germany. While writing algebra proofs and preparing for software engineering at nights in the kitchen, at the same time he discovered passion for typography, interface design and writing. After working as a freelance designer and developer for 6 years, he co-founded Smashing Magazine back in 2006, a leading online magazine for designers and developers. His curiosity drove him from interface design to front-end to performance optimization to accessibility and back to user experience over all the years.

Vitaly is the author, co-author and editor of Smashing Books (https://www.smashingmagazine.com/books), and a curator of  Smashing Conferences (https://www.smashingconf.com). He is the UX lead with the European Parliament and Smashing Magazine (www.smashingmagazine.com) and front-end/UX consultant in Europe and abroad, working with large and small companies and organizations like Haufe-Lexware, Axel-Springer and others.

He also runs Measure UX (https://measure-ux.com) and Smart Interface Design Patterns (https://smart-interface-design-patterns.com), friendly video courses on UX and design patterns, along with a live UX training for passionate UX and product designers.

I am a Staff Engineer in the Mobile team at Redcare Pharmacy, specializing in Android application development and large-scale architecture design. I lead architectural transformations that enable teams to adopt multi-module strategies and take ownership of domain-driven development within a shared codebase.

I advocate clean architectural patterns that improve scalability, build performance, and team autonomy. Recently, I have been exploring a feature-driven “micro frontend” approach across mobile and backend systems, leveraging Kotlin Multiplatform to share business logic while preserving native UI independence on Android and iOS.

Wekoslav Stefanovski has about two decade of professional developer experience using a variety of development technologies. Has been using C# since the first public beta, and has a long and fruitful love relationship with it. Has been using JavaScript since the previous millennium and has a long and fruitful love/hate relationship with it. Currently, works at Sourcico as Head of development. He is passionate about functional programming, static code analysis, compiler design and code quality metrics.

Dr. Werner Vogels is Chief Technology Officer at Amazon.com where he is responsible for driving the company's customer-centric technology vision.

As one of the forces behind Amazon's approach to cloud computing, he is passionate about helping young businesses reach global scale, and transforming enterprises into fast-moving digital organizations.

Vogels joined Amazon in 2004 from Cornell University where he was a distributed systems researcher. He has held technology leadership positions in companies that handle the transition of academic technology into industry. Vogels holds a PhD from the Vrije Universiteit in Amsterdam and has authored many articles on distributed systems technologies for enterprise computing.

On his blog, all things distributed, Werner shares insights and thought leadership content on a range of topics, such as cloud computing, machine learning, artificial intelligence, as well as his annual technology predictions.

Over 20 years experience in software engenering
During these years I develop standalone applications, mobile applications for Android, IoT and much more
For the last 12 years at mbank on the mainframe to x86  migration project where I designing solution architecture and technically managing development team 
I boldly go where I haven't been before - not afraid to explore strange programming language

Dr. Wolfgang Oels studied engineering and business and started his career at McKinsey & Company before taking senior leadership positions in the solar industry in Germany and the US. Since 2016, he has served as COO of Ecosia, the largest European search engine, and as Director of European Search Perspective, building a sovereign European web index.

Wouter is particularly passionate about CICD and everything that involves automation in the tech industry. In the past years he worked in different countries on projects that involve automating Kubernetes workloads while at the same time speeding up and standardizing the landscape in ING. In his free time you can occasionally find him on a splitboard touring the Romanian mountains

Xuan Huang (@huxpro) is the Architect of Lynx at ByteDance, an open-source, cross-platform framework powering TikTok and CapCut. He is exploring what app frameworks should look like in the age of AI, and he was previously on the React Core Team at Meta.

Yanay Sela is the Chief Marketing Officer at Bright Data, the world's #1 web data infrastructure for AI. He was the first-ever CMO at Seeking Alpha, and previously served as CMO at Appsee and Galatea, where he led both companies through acquisition. Yanay is a resident professor of marketing in the global MBA program at Reichman University, and a visiting professor at leading business schools across Europe.

Yasmin Schneider is Head of Analytics Framework at Finanz Informatik, the central IT service provider of the Savings Banks Finance Group. She joined the company in 2017 as an application developer and has been leading the department since 2025. With a strong background in software development and analytics, she drives the evolution of data and analytics capabilities for Germany's largest financial IT service provider.

Yiyang Lu is a self-taught engineer and entrepreneur at the intersection of Artificial Intelligence and robotics. As the founder behind initiatives such as pluto and pdm R&D Labs, he is dedicated to developing advanced technologies that prioritize human-centric design and societal impact.

With a multidisciplinary background spanning from quantitative analysis at the London School of Economics to practical engineering, Yiyang advocates for a future where innovation acts as a catalyst for human capability rather than a replacement. Currently based in Berlin, he is active in the tech ecosystem, focusing on methodologies that bridge the gap between complex technical implementation and ethical, real-world application.
Yuval Dvir

Zaak Chalal is the founder and CEO of Mobioos, a company pioneering context engineering for software development and AI-assisted delivery.

A software engineer and entrepreneur for more than 28 years, Zaak wrote his first programs on a Commodore 64 and later contributed to some of Europe’s earliest large-scale digital initiatives, including Ooshop, Carrefour’s pioneering e-commerce platform.

Throughout his career, he has led software engineering, architecture, and digital transformation initiatives across multiple industries. His experience exposed a recurring challenge: organizations struggle to preserve and operationalize the business context behind their software systems.

Today, through Mobioos, Zaak focuses on helping organizations transform business and technical context into a strategic asset that can be understood and activated by humans, AI systems, and autonomous agents.

His work challenges the traditional assumption that code alone is the source of truth and explores how context can become the foundation for reliable AI-driven software engineering.

During 20+ years spent in ICT industry, I had a privilege to wear all possible hats, acting as a software developer, UX designer, university lecturer, startup entrepreneur, CIO and finally as a delivery manager at S&P500 engineering company. Creating software is my biggest passion. I stay hands-on by delivering PoC and MVP to clients, diving into all aspects of modern technology, be it cloud computing, blockchain or AI. Originally from Estonia, since 2016 I live in Switzerland and in free time I enjoy doing yoga and snowboarding.

As a Developer Advocate at Neo4j, I help developers harness the power of graph technology and AI. A Microsoft MVP and global speaker at events like TED, Microsoft Build, and WeAreDevelopers, I focus on making complex tech accessible through content, community, and hands-on support. My mission is to connect, empower, and inspire developers to build impactful solutions.

Žan Jonke is a software engineer at *codeplain, a company rethinking how software gets built through spec-driven AI code generation. With hands-on experience across machine learning, full-stack development, and leadership, he has built production AI systems across national security and medical imaging. At *codeplain, Žan is focused on scaling integration development through AI-powered specification-driven workflows eliminating boilerplate, automating implementation, and helping engineering teams build and ship integrations faster than ever before.

Zohar Fox is the Co-Founder and CEO of Aurora Labs, the company behind LOCI - bringing execution reasoning into modern software development.
Awarded the 2021 Artificial Intelligence Excellence Award, Zohar is now leading the transformation of how software is developed, deployed, and maintained, across both automotive and AI factory environments. LOCI by Aurora Labs makes large-scale rollouts more predictable while improving runtime performance and reducing energy consumption in production code. Zohar's vision bridges embedded systems and large-scale inference workloads, delivering observability and optimization directly at the binary and hardware levels, before systems even reach the test bench. Prior to founding Aurora Labs, Zohar spent two decades in embedded hardware/software design, orchestration, and deployment for TELCOs and technology service providers, helping deliver over 10 million connected devices across automotive, banking, and IoT industries. Mr. Fox have also led strategic sales and technology partnerships with Deutsche Telekom, France Telecom, DSP Group, and BOSCH.
